[Task 2]\n--- ATTACHMENT ---\r\n# PROJECT ONE VOYAGE\r\n\r\n## Canonical Domain Consolidation and Legacy Companion Retirement\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 1. Mission\r\n\r\nPerform a complete architectural consolidation of the Forever Treasure / Tall Tale Platform repository so that the application has **one canonical story, publishing, session, progression, identity, authorization, artifact, audit, and runtime domain**.\r\n\r\nThe repository currently contains two substantial and partially overlapping systems:\r\n\r\n## Legacy Companion domain\r\n\r\nIncluding, but not necessarily limited to:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nCampaign\r\nChapter\r\nArtifact\r\nSideQuest\r\nProgressEvent\r\nCampaignSnapshot\r\nPlayerAccess\r\nAdminAuditLog\r\nPreparedAction\r\nCommandExecution\r\nlegacy Player session/token flows\r\nlegacy Quartermaster control flows\r\nlegacy Companion APIs and UI surfaces\r\n```\r\n\r\n## New Tall Tale Platform domain\r\n\r\nIncluding, but not necessarily limited to:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nTallTale\r\nTaleDraft\r\nPublishedTaleVersion\r\nStoryBlock\r\nTaleSession\r\nTaleSessionEvent\r\nPlayerProfile\r\nPlayerIdentitySession\r\nPlaythroughMembership\r\nPlatformAuditEvent\r\nInvitation\r\nRevealState\r\nCreator Studio\r\nCaptain runtime\r\nPlayer runtime\r\npublished-version snapshots\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe final architecture must treat the Tall Tale Platform domain as the canonical system.\r\n\r\nThe legacy Companion domain must be:\r\n\r\n1. fully inventoried;\r\n2. semantically mapped;\r\n3. migrated without losing data or behavior;\r\n4. routed through canonical services;\r\n5. retained temporarily only where compatibility is genuinely required;\r\n6. explicitly deprecated;\r\n7. removed only after parity is proven.\r\n\r\nDo not perform a superficial rename.\r\n\r\nDo not simply make legacy models call new models while leaving duplicated business logic active.\r\n\r\nDo not delete legacy code merely because the new models appear conceptually similar.\r\n\r\nThe goal is not fewer files.\r\n\r\nThe goal is:\r\n\r\n> One authoritative domain model, one progression engine, one identity system, one authorization system, one audit trail, and one runtime path.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 2. Primary Outcome\r\n\r\nAt completion, the application must no longer require two independent implementations for:\r\n\r\n* story structure;\r\n* chapter progression;\r\n* Player access;\r\n* Captain commands;\r\n* runtime events;\r\n* snapshots;\r\n* artifact ownership;\r\n* reveal state;\r\n* audit history;\r\n* replay;\r\n* authorization;\r\n* session restoration;\r\n* content visibility;\r\n* or presentation acknowledgments.\r\n\r\nAll production behavior must flow through canonical Tall Tale Platform services.\r\n\r\nLegacy routes and interfaces may remain temporarily, but only as thin compatibility adapters over canonical behavior.\r\n\r\nA compatibility adapter may:\r\n\r\n* translate request shapes;\r\n* resolve legacy IDs;\r\n* translate canonical responses back into legacy response shapes;\r\n* redirect legacy URLs;\r\n* preserve old bookmarks and invitation links.\r\n\r\nA compatibility adapter must not:\r\n\r\n* contain separate progression rules;\r\n* write legacy and canonical state independently;\r\n* maintain its own authorization decisions;\r\n* maintain separate snapshot logic;\r\n* emit a second competing audit trail;\r\n* perform undocumented dual writes;\r\n* create divergent runtime truth.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 3. Repository and Branch Safety\r\n\r\nBefore editing:\r\n\r\n1. Confirm the repository root.\r\n2. Record:\r\n\r\n   * current branch;\r\n   * current commit;\r\n   * upstream branch;\r\n   * ahead/behind state;\r\n   * staged files;\r\n   * unstaged files;\r\n   * untracked files.\r\n3. Inspect all repository-specific instructions.\r\n4. Identify active worktrees and concurrent branches.\r\n5. Identify whether Lanternwake Phase 5 and the universal-language overhaul have already been merged.\r\n6. Preserve all current language, animation, accessibility, and runtime improvements.\r\n7. Do not overwrite newer work with copies from an older audit baseline.\r\n8. Target the current checked-out repository state.\r\n\r\nDo not use destructive commands such as:\r\n\r\n```text\r\ngit reset --hard\r\ngit clean\r\ngit checkout -- .\r\ngit restore .\r\ngit stash --all\r\nforce push\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not stage unrelated files with broad commands such as:\r\n\r\n```text\r\ngit add .\r\ngit add -A\r\n```\r\n\r\nStage only reviewed files belonging to this consolidation.\r\n\r\nDo not rewrite public history.\r\n\r\nDo not modify unrelated animation behavior, universal language, styling, or story content unless required by the canonical-domain cutover.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 4. Required Initial Investigation\r\n\r\nBefore implementing broad changes, inspect the complete repository and produce a precise architectural inventory.\r\n\r\nCreate:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_One_Voyage_Architecture_Inventory.md\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe inventory must identify every legacy and canonical component related to:\r\n\r\n* Prisma models;\r\n* migrations;\r\n* seed data;\r\n* server actions;\r\n* API routes;\r\n* route handlers;\r\n* domain services;\r\n* repositories;\r\n* snapshot builders;\r\n* event projectors;\r\n* command handlers;\r\n* authentication;\r\n* authorization;\r\n* Player access;\r\n* Captain access;\r\n* invitation flows;\r\n* legacy tokens;\r\n* Studio publishing;\r\n* published-version loading;\r\n* Player runtime;\r\n* Captain runtime;\r\n* artifact inventory;\r\n* map state;\r\n* side quests;\r\n* reveal state;\r\n* audit logging;\r\n* command execution;\r\n* replay;\r\n* recovery;\r\n* tests;\r\n* documentation.\r\n\r\nFor each item record:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nName\r\nFile path\r\nDomain owner\r\nLegacy or canonical\r\nReads\r\nWrites\r\nCalled by\r\nAuthorization source\r\nEvent source\r\nSnapshot source\r\nMigration relevance\r\nRecommended disposition\r\n```\r\n\r\nRecommended disposition must be one of:\r\n\r\n```text\r\ncanonical\r\nmigrate\r\nadapt\r\nmerge\r\ndeprecate\r\nremove-after-parity\r\nretain-shared\r\nfuture-review\r\n```\r\n\r\nNo legacy model, API, service, or route may be omitted merely because it appears unused.\r\n\r\nVerify actual reachability using:\r\n\r\n* imports;\r\n* route registration;\r\n* server actions;\r\n* API clients;\r\n* runtime network requests;\r\n* tests;\r\n* seed scripts;\r\n* documentation references;\r\n* direct database access;\r\n* dynamic imports;\r\n* URL construction;\r\n* user-facing navigation.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 5. Required Architecture Decision Record\r\n\r\nBefore broad source modification, create:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_One_Voyage_Canonical_Domain_ADR.md\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe ADR must define the final ownership boundaries for:\r\n\r\n1. Tall Tale authoring\r\n2. Published Tale versions\r\n3. Runtime sessions\r\n4. Runtime event history\r\n5. Player identity\r\n6. Player membership\r\n7. Invitations\r\n8. Captain authorization\r\n9. Player authorization\r\n10. Creator authorization\r\n11. Artifacts and inventory\r\n12. Chapters and story blocks\r\n13. Side quests\r\n14. Map and route state\r\n15. Reveal state\r\n16. Variables\r\n17. Snapshots\r\n18. Audit history\r\n19. Commands\r\n20. Presentation/viewed acknowledgment\r\n21. Replay\r\n22. Legacy compatibility\r\n\r\nThe ADR must answer:\r\n\r\n* What is the canonical aggregate root for authored content?\r\n* What is the canonical aggregate root for a live playthrough?\r\n* What is immutable after publishing?\r\n* What is mutable during a Tale Session?\r\n* Which state is event-sourced?\r\n* Which state is projected or cached?\r\n* Which state is derived?\r\n* What determines current story position?\r\n* What determines Player-visible content?\r\n* What determines authorization?\r\n* What determines artifact ownership?\r\n* What determines chapter completion?\r\n* What determines side-quest completion?\r\n* What identifies a Player?\r\n* What identifies a crew membership?\r\n* How are anonymous or legacy Players represented?\r\n* How are old invitation links preserved?\r\n* How are legacy identifiers resolved?\r\n* How are migrations made idempotent?\r\n* How is rollback performed?\r\n* When may legacy code be deleted?\r\n\r\nThe ADR must explicitly declare:\r\n\r\n> The canonical domain is the only writer of production story and runtime state after cutover.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 6. Canonical Target Architecture\r\n\r\nUse the existing Tall Tale Platform architecture wherever it is sound.\r\n\r\nDo not replace it merely to make the legacy migration easier.\r\n\r\nThe expected canonical ownership is approximately:\r\n\r\n## Authoring\r\n\r\n```text\r\nTallTale\r\nTaleDraft\r\nStoryBlock\r\nTaleArtifact\r\nTaleLocation\r\nrelated draft-domain entities\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Publishing\r\n\r\n```text\r\nPublishedTaleVersion\r\nimmutable published snapshot\r\npublished content checksum\r\nschema version\r\nasset references\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Runtime\r\n\r\n```text\r\nTaleSession\r\nTaleSessionEvent\r\nPlaythroughMembership\r\nRevealState\r\nruntime variables\r\nruntime inventory\r\ncurrent story position\r\nsession lifecycle state\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Identity and access\r\n\r\n```text\r\nPlayerProfile\r\nPlayerIdentitySession\r\nInvitation\r\nPlaythroughMembership\r\nCreator/Captain authorization\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Auditing\r\n\r\n```text\r\nPlatformAuditEvent\r\ncanonical command and event correlation\r\nsanitized metadata\r\n```\r\n\r\nExact names may differ based on current code, but the ownership boundaries may not be omitted.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 7. Mandatory Domain-Mapping Matrix\r\n\r\nCreate:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_One_Voyage_Domain_Mapping.csv\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe mapping must cover every legacy model and important field.\r\n\r\nAt minimum investigate these mappings:\r\n\r\n| Legacy concept         | Expected canonical destination                                     |\r\n| ---------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |\r\n| Campaign               | TallTale plus PublishedTaleVersion                                 |\r\n| Chapter                | StoryBlock graph and published chapter metadata                    |\r\n| Artifact               | TaleArtifact plus runtime inventory/reveal state                   |\r\n| SideQuest              | Canonical optional branch or dedicated canonical side-quest entity |\r\n| ProgressEvent          | TaleSessionEvent                                                   |\r\n| CampaignSnapshot       | TaleSession projection or canonical runtime snapshot               |\r\n| PlayerAccess           | PlayerProfile, Invitation, and PlaythroughMembership               |\r\n| AdminAuditLog          | PlatformAuditEvent                                                 |\r\n| PreparedAction         | Canonical pending command/action representation                    |\r\n| CommandExecution       | Canonical command/event/audit correlation                          |\r\n| Legacy session token   | PlayerIdentitySession or compatibility credential                  |\r\n| Quartermaster state    | Canonical Captain runtime services                                 |\r\n| Legacy viewed ceremony | Canonical presentation acknowledgment state                        |\r\n| Legacy map state       | Canonical published map data and runtime RevealState               |\r\n| Legacy route state     | Canonical map/route progression state                              |\r\n\r\nThe mapping must include:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nLegacy model\r\nLegacy field\r\nLegacy meaning\r\nCanonical model\r\nCanonical field\r\nTransformation\r\nDefault behavior\r\nNull behavior\r\nLoss risk\r\nValidation rule\r\nMigration test\r\nRollback handling\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not assume a direct one-to-one mapping.\r\n\r\nFor example, a legacy `Chapter` may map to:\r\n\r\n* chapter metadata;\r\n* multiple ordered StoryBlocks;\r\n* entry block;\r\n* completion block;\r\n* map reveal;\r\n* artifact grant;\r\n* transition event.\r\n\r\nIf a direct mapping would be lossy, preserve the semantic behavior using an explicit canonical extension.\r\n\r\nDo not hide unmapped data inside an undocumented JSON blob.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 8. Data Preservation Requirements\r\n\r\nNo meaningful legacy data may be lost.\r\n\r\nPreserve, where present:\r\n\r\n* stable public slugs;\r\n* display names;\r\n* chapter ordering;\r\n* current chapter;\r\n* current scene/block equivalent;\r\n* completion state;\r\n* artifact unlocks;\r\n* artifact assembly state;\r\n* side-quest state;\r\n* map reveals;\r\n* route reveals;\r\n* Player identities;\r\n* Player access;\r\n* crew roles;\r\n* Captain permissions;\r\n* event sequence;\r\n* event timestamps;\r\n* audit history;\r\n* viewed/presentation state;\r\n* invitation state;\r\n* session lifecycle;\r\n* pause/resume state;\r\n* created/updated timestamps;\r\n* archived state;\r\n* legacy references needed for support.\r\n\r\nIf a value cannot be represented in the canonical system:\r\n\r\n1. document it;\r\n2. add an explicit typed canonical representation where justified;\r\n3. migrate it;\r\n4. test it.\r\n\r\nDo not silently discard it.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 9. Stable Identity and Provenance\r\n\r\nThe migration must be deterministic and rerunnable.\r\n\r\nCreate an explicit legacy-to-canonical identity strategy.\r\n\r\nAcceptable strategies include:\r\n\r\n* deterministic IDs derived from legacy IDs;\r\n* a dedicated migration mapping table;\r\n* a migration manifest with immutable mapping;\r\n* another equally reliable approach.\r\n\r\nThe chosen design must support:\r\n\r\n* idempotent reruns;\r\n* partial failure recovery;\r\n* legacy URL resolution;\r\n* audit traceability;\r\n* duplicate detection;\r\n* rollback;\r\n* migration verification;\r\n* imported-record provenance.\r\n\r\nRecommended conceptual metadata:\r\n\r\n```ts\r\ntype LegacyEntityReference = {\r\n  sourceDomain: \"legacy-companion\";\r\n  sourceModel: string;\r\n  sourceId: string;\r\n  canonicalModel: string;\r\n  canonicalId: string;\r\n  migrationVersion: string;\r\n  sourceChecksum: string;\r\n  migratedAt: Date;\r\n};\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not expose internal migration identifiers to Players.\r\n\r\nDo not change existing public URLs without redirect or adapter support.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 10. Immutable Published-Version Migration\r\n\r\nEvery migrated Campaign must become a valid canonical Tall Tale with at least one immutable published version.\r\n\r\nThe migration must:\r\n\r\n1. create or resolve the canonical Tall Tale;\r\n2. construct a c…24 tokens truncated…rnwake animation behavior;\r\n* reduced-motion behavior;\r\n* focus management;\r\n* accessibility;\r\n* route transition behavior;\r\n* replay behavior;\r\n* responsive layouts.\r\n\r\nDo not redesign the UI during this project.\r\n\r\nThe user-facing experience should remain semantically equivalent while the backend becomes canonical.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 23. Migration Tooling\r\n\r\nCreate an explicit migration command.\r\n\r\nRecommended conceptual command:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nnpm run migrate:legacy-companion\r\n```\r\n\r\nAlso provide dry-run and verification modes:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nnpm run migrate:legacy-companion -- --dry-run\r\nnpm run migrate:legacy-companion -- --verify\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe migration must support:\r\n\r\n* one specific Campaign;\r\n* all legacy Campaigns;\r\n* dry run;\r\n* verbose report;\r\n* fail-fast mode;\r\n* continue-and-report mode where safe;\r\n* idempotent rerun;\r\n* checksum comparison;\r\n* rollback support;\r\n* verification-only mode.\r\n\r\nThe migration report must include:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nCampaigns inspected\r\nTall Tales created\r\nPublished versions created\r\nTale Sessions created\r\nPlayers migrated\r\nMemberships migrated\r\nInvitations migrated\r\nChapters translated\r\nStoryBlocks created\r\nArtifacts migrated\r\nSide quests migrated\r\nEvents migrated\r\nAudit records migrated\r\nLegacy identifiers mapped\r\nWarnings\r\nFailures\r\nUnmapped fields\r\nChecksum mismatches\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not run the migration against the canonical production database until isolated-copy validation has passed.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 24. Shadow Comparison Before Cutover\r\n\r\nBefore switching production reads, implement a temporary shadow-comparison mode.\r\n\r\nFor the same legacy Campaign/session, build:\r\n\r\n1. the legacy projection;\r\n2. the canonical projection.\r\n\r\nCompare normalized semantic values such as:\r\n\r\n* current chapter;\r\n* current block equivalent;\r\n* completed chapters;\r\n* artifact inventory;\r\n* side quests;\r\n* map reveals;\r\n* routes;\r\n* Player access;\r\n* session status;\r\n* pause state;\r\n* event sequence;\r\n* viewed presentation state.\r\n\r\nIgnore only explicitly documented non-semantic differences such as:\r\n\r\n* generated IDs;\r\n* canonical timestamps created during migration;\r\n* ordering of unordered metadata;\r\n* renamed internal enum values.\r\n\r\nProduce structured mismatch reports.\r\n\r\nDo not expose private content in logs.\r\n\r\nThe cutover gate requires zero unexplained semantic mismatches.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 25. Cutover Strategy\r\n\r\nUse explicit rollout stages.\r\n\r\n## Stage A: Inventory and ADR\r\n\r\nNo behavior changes.\r\n\r\n## Stage B: Canonical migration tooling\r\n\r\nLegacy remains active.\r\n\r\n## Stage C: Shadow reads\r\n\r\nLegacy remains authoritative.\r\n\r\nCanonical projections are compared but not served.\r\n\r\n## Stage D: Canonical reads\r\n\r\nCanonical state becomes authoritative for migrated sessions.\r\n\r\nLegacy routes adapt canonical reads.\r\n\r\n## Stage E: Canonical writes\r\n\r\nAll production commands write only canonical state.\r\n\r\nNo undocumented dual writes are permitted.\r\n\r\n## Stage F: Compatibility-only legacy layer\r\n\r\nLegacy routes and URLs remain as adapters.\r\n\r\nLegacy database tables become read-only or unused.\r\n\r\n## Stage G: Retirement\r\n\r\nRemove legacy code only after all acceptance gates pass.\r\n\r\nIf feature flags are used, they must have:\r\n\r\n* typed configuration;\r\n* safe defaults;\r\n* documented rollback;\r\n* tests;\r\n* eventual removal criteria.\r\n\r\nDo not leave permanent ambiguous flags such as:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nUSE_NEW_SYSTEM=true\r\n```\r\n\r\nUse explicit names and document the rollout stage.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 26. Rollback Strategy\r\n\r\nBefore canonical writes are enabled, document rollback.\r\n\r\nCreate:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_One_Voyage_Rollback_Runbook.md\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe runbook must cover:\r\n\r\n* database backup;\r\n* asset backup;\r\n* migration mapping backup;\r\n* restoring legacy reads;\r\n* restoring legacy routing;\r\n* disabling canonical cutover;\r\n* handling canonical-only events written after cutover;\r\n* irreversible boundaries;\r\n* verification after rollback.\r\n\r\nOnce canonical-only events have been written, rollback cannot simply point the application back to stale legacy tables.\r\n\r\nProvide either:\r\n\r\n* a reverse projection tool;\r\n* a maintenance-window restore strategy;\r\n* or an explicit no-simple-rollback boundary with backup restoration.\r\n\r\nDo not claim rollback is supported unless it has been rehearsed.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 27. Database Safety\r\n\r\nAll mutation testing must use an isolated database.\r\n\r\nBefore mutating tests:\r\n\r\n1. identify the canonical local database;\r\n2. record its path or database name;\r\n3. record hash/metadata where applicable;\r\n4. create a uniquely named test copy;\r\n5. set an explicit test `DATABASE_URL`;\r\n6. verify the running server uses that copy;\r\n7. record a database nonce;\r\n8. query the nonce through the application;\r\n9. run migrations only against the copy;\r\n10. verify the canonical database remains unchanged afterward.\r\n\r\nFor MySQL testing:\r\n\r\n* use a dedicated test schema;\r\n* never point tests at production;\r\n* use least-privilege credentials;\r\n* verify migration permissions separately from runtime permissions.\r\n\r\nOnly one database owner may perform:\r\n\r\n* schema generation;\r\n* migrations;\r\n* resets;\r\n* seed;\r\n* migration execution;\r\n* mutating browser tests.\r\n\r\nDo not run multiple migration implementations concurrently.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 28. Schema Migration Rules\r\n\r\nIf Prisma schema changes are required:\r\n\r\n1. inspect all existing migrations;\r\n2. create ordered forward migrations;\r\n3. preserve existing constraints;\r\n4. add indexes for migration lookups;\r\n5. add uniqueness constraints for idempotency;\r\n6. avoid destructive column drops until retirement;\r\n7. avoid renaming without a compatibility strategy;\r\n8. test SQLite and MySQL behavior if both remain supported;\r\n9. regenerate Prisma artifacts once under a single owner;\r\n10. document production migration order.\r\n\r\nDo not immediately delete legacy tables.\r\n\r\nRecommended retirement sequence:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nactive\r\nread-only\r\nunused\r\nverified-empty-or-archived\r\nremoved-in-later-release\r\n```\r\n\r\nA compatibility release should separate cutover from destructive deletion.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 29. Required Tests\r\n\r\n## 29.1 Migration unit tests\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n* one Campaign;\r\n* multiple Campaigns;\r\n* Campaign with no chapters;\r\n* Campaign with many chapters;\r\n* missing optional assets;\r\n* artifact assembly;\r\n* side quests;\r\n* map routes;\r\n* paused session;\r\n* completed session;\r\n* multiple Players;\r\n* anonymous Player;\r\n* legacy invitation;\r\n* duplicate migration run;\r\n* partial migration recovery;\r\n* source checksum change;\r\n* missing foreign reference;\r\n* malformed legacy data;\r\n* rollback metadata.\r\n\r\n## 29.2 Mapping tests\r\n\r\nFor every legacy model:\r\n\r\n* all meaningful fields mapped;\r\n* nulls handled;\r\n* defaults documented;\r\n* IDs stable;\r\n* timestamps preserved;\r\n* visibility preserved;\r\n* authorization preserved.\r\n\r\n## 29.3 Projection parity tests\r\n\r\nCompare legacy and canonical output for:\r\n\r\n* initial voyage;\r\n* active voyage;\r\n* paused voyage;\r\n* resumed voyage;\r\n* completed chapter;\r\n* artifact grant;\r\n* side-quest discovery;\r\n* side-quest completion;\r\n* map reveal;\r\n* route reveal;\r\n* invitation acceptance;\r\n* Player reconnect;\r\n* Captain reconnect;\r\n* against migrated fixture;\r\n3. compare semantic result;\r\n4. compare projected state;\r\n5. compare event effect;\r\n6. compare authorization behavior.\r\n\r\n## 29.5 Authorization tests\r\n\r\nTest that:\r\n\r\n* Player cannot access Captain projection;\r\n* Player cannot access another crew;\r\n* Player cannot access unpublished Tale content;\r\n* Captain cannot edit Tale drafts unless sequence;\r\n* idempotent command;\r\n* duplicate event migration;\r\n* restart recovery;\r\n* no double grants;\r\n* no double completion;\r\n* no duplicate acknowledgments;\r\n* canonical audit correlation;\r\n* legacy adapter creates only canonical event.\r\n\r\n## 29.7 Browser tests\r\n\r\nAfter database-isolation proof:\r\n\r\n* legacy invitation link still works;\r\n* legacy Player URL controls affect canonical state;\r\n* Quartermaster compatibility surface affects canonical state;\r\n* refresh restores canonical session;\r\n* revocation propagates;\r\n* reduced motion remains correct;\r\n* no legacy mutation endpoint writes legacy state.\r\n\r\n## 29.8 Restart tests\r\n\r\nProve:\r\n\r\n1. migrate data;\r\n2. start session;\r\n3. perform canonical mutation;\r\n4. stop Exact Acceptance Criteria\r\n\r\nProject One Voyage is complete only when all conditions below are satisfied.\r\n\r\n## Architecture\r\n\r\n* One canonical authored-content domain exists.\r\n* One canonical published-version domain exists.\r\n* One canonical runtime session domain exists.\r\n* One canonical event history exists.\r\n* One canonical Player identity system exists.\r\n* One canonical membership/access system exists.\r\n* One canonical artifact/inventory system exists.\r\n* One canonical snapshot builder exists.\r\n* One canonical authorization layer exists.\r\n* One canonical audit system exists.\r\n* One canonical command path exists.\r\n\r\n## Migration\r\n\r\n* Every legacy model has a documented disposition.\r\n* Every meaningful legacy field has a documented mapping.\r\n* Migration is deterministic.\r\n* Migration is idempotent.\r\n* Migration can resume safely after partial failure.\r\n* Every migrated Campaign has a valid immutable PublishedTaleVersion.\r\n* Every migrated runtime is pinned to a PublishedTaleVersion.\r\n* No unexplained semantic projection mismatch remains.\r\n* No meaningful data is silently discarded.\r\n\r\n## Runtime\r\n\r\n* Production reads use canonical state.\r\n* Production writes use canonical state only.\r\n* Legacy routes contain no independent business logic.\r\n* Legacy mutation routes do not write legacy tables.\r\n* Player refresh restores canonical state.\r\n* Captain refresh restores canonical state.\r\n* Restart preserves canonical state.\r\n* Event sequence remains monotonic.\r\n* Commands remain idempotent.\r\n* No artifact or completion is granted twice.\r\n\r\n## Compatibility\r\n\r\n* Existing valid Player links continue working or securely redirect.\r\n* Existing valid invitation links continue working or securely exchange.\r\n* Existing Quartermaster workflows invoke canonical commands.\r\n* Existing public slugs remain resolvable.\r\n* Universal language remains intact.\r\n* Lanternwake animations remain intact.\r\n* Accessibility behavior remains intact.\r\n* Reduced-motion behavior remains intact.\r\n\r\n## Security\r\n\r\n* No privilege broadening occurs.\r\n* No cross-Tale data leakage occurs.\r\n* No unpublished content reaches Players.\r\n* Legacy tokens cannot become global credentials.\r\n* Audit logs contain no secrets.\r\n* Migration logs contain no private narrative payloads.\r\n* Revocation works through compatibility routes.\r\n* Authorization negative tests pass.\r\n\r\n## Retirement\r\n\r\n* Every legacy component is classified.\r\n* Legacy writers are disabled.\r\n* Legacy read logic is replaced or adapter-only.\r\n* Deprecated code is clearly marked.\r\n* Removal gates are documented.\r\n* Code is deleted only where parity and zero production reachability are proven.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 34. unchanged proof;\r\n* restart proof;\r\n* idempotent rerun proof.\r\n\r\n## Remaining compatibility\r\n\r\nList every retained legacy component and explain:\r\n\r\n* why it remains;\r\n* whether it is reachable;\r\n* whether it can write;\r\n* its canonical replacement;\r\n* exact removal gate.\r\n\r\n## Remaining limitations\r\n\r\nDo not hide incomplete work.\r\n\r\nState explicitly whether:\r\n\r\n* all legacy data was migrated;\r\n* all routes were adapted;\r\n* all writers were disabled;\r\n* destructive table removal was deferred;\r\n* live MySQL integration was Concurrency and Token Discipline\r\n\r\nUse parallel investigation where file ownership does not overlap.\r\n\r\nRecommended lanes after the ADR and shared interfaces are frozen:\r\n\r\n## Lane A: Schema and migration\r\n\r\nOwns:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nPrisma schema\r\nmigration scripts\r\nmigration tests\r\nlegacy mapping\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Lane B: Canonical runtime services\r\n\r\nOwns:\r\n\r\n```text\r\ncommands\r\nevents\r\nsnapshots\r\nruntime projections\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Lane C: Identity and authorization\r\n\r\nOwns:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nPlayer identity\r\nsessions\r\nmembership\r\ninvitations\r\nauthorization tests\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Lane D: Compatibility adapters\r\n\r\nOwns:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nlegacy APIs\r\nlegacy route redirects\r\nQuartermaster adapters\r\nlegacy token exchange\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Lane E: Read-only architecture and privacy review\r\n\r\nReviews:\r\n\r\n```text\r\ndata loss\r\nauthorization drift\r\naudit leakage\r\nevent duplication\r\nmigration gaps\r\n```\r\n\r\nSerialized ownership is required for:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nprisma/schema.prisma\r\nshared canonical domain interfaces\r\ncentral command dispatcher\r\ncentral snapshot builder\r\nauthorization core\r\nmigration runner\r\nfinal browser server\r\nfinal test database\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not have multiple agents repeatedly reread the entire repository.\r\n\r\nCreate concise lane handoffs containing:\r\n\r\n* files inspected;\r\n* findings;\r\n* decisions;\r\n* files changed;\r\n* unresolved issues;\r\n* test evidence.\r\n\r\nPrefer focused searches and direct file inspection over repeated broad scans.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 36. ession.md\r\nForever-Treasure-Integration\\Codex_Chats\\chats\\019f7680-a621-7aa3-b206-63e6562de7d3--codex-session.md\r\nForever-Treasure-Integration\\Codex_Chats\\chats\\019f7680-7e54-77d1-8e54-6d6b4cd7eab9--codex-session.md\r\nForever-Treasure-Integration\\Codex_Chats\\chats\\019f7680-56be-7412-99c7-8e35deab4d64--codex-session.md\r\nForever-Treasure-Integration\\Codex_Chats\\chats\\019f7680-41cb-7b12-90d7-690de0c30f66--codex-session.md\r\nForever-Treasure-Integration\\Codex_Chats\\chats\\019f7680-0fa3-7b22-9c91-…24 tokens truncated…tp://127.0.0.1:3000/studio`\r\n- Captain: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/captain`\r\n- GM development[REDACTED: LOGIN CREDENTIAL]\r\n\r\nThese values are disposable local defaults, never production credentials. To opt into LAN access, run `powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/start-dev.ps1 -Lan`; Windows Firewall and network policy still determine whether another device can connect.\r\n\r\n### Portable manual setup\r\n\r\n```bash\r\ngit clone https://github.com/Kgray44/treasurehuntSoT.git\r\ncd treasurehuntSoT\r\nnpm ci\r\ncp .env.example .env\r\nnpm run db:generate\r\nnpm run db:migrate\r\nnpm run db:seed\r\nnpm run dev\r\n```\r\n\r\nSet unique local values in `.env`; the seedâ€™s fallback values are development-only and must never be used outside an isolated development database. Player path: `/tale/development-forever-treasure`. GM path: `/quartermaster`.\r\n\r\n## Commands\r\n\r\n| Command                                   | Purpose                                                               |\r\n| ----------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------- |\r\n| `npm run dev:full`                        | Idempotent setup, migration, progress-safe seed check, and server     |\r\n| `npm run dev:stop`                        | Safely stop only the recorded development process                     |\r\n| `npm run validate`                        | Fresh DB, format/lint/types/unit/E2E/a11y/build/restart proof         |\r\n| `npm run dev`                             | Next.js only; assumes setup is already complete                       |\r\n| `npm run build`                           | Production build                                                      |\r\n| `npm start`                               | Start production build                                                |\r\n| `npm test`                                | Domain, command-center, Studio, animation, and component unit tests   |\r\n| `npm run assets:validate`                 | Validate local Lottie/Rive contracts and fallback paths               |\r\n| `npm run test:e2e`                        | Cross-browser player, Command Center, Studio, and animation workflows |\r\n| `npm run db:migrate`                      | Apply local SQLite migrations                                         |\r\n| `npm run db:seed`                         | Load replaceable development content                                  |\r\n| `npm run db:preset -- mid-voyage`         | Reset to a repeatable development-only companion preset               |\r\n| `npm run db:generate:mysql`               | Generate production MySQL client                                      |\r\n| `npm run db:migrate:mysql:command-center` | Apply the Phase 3 production MySQL migration                          |\r\n| `npm run db:migrate:mysql:studio`         | Apply the production-parity Tall Tale Studio migration                |\r\n| `npm run db:migrate:mysql:platform`       | Apply the unified Tall Tale Platform migration                        |\r\n\r\nFull setup, validation stages, output locations, clean-clone instructions, and troubleshooting are in [local development](docs/local-development.md) and [testing](docs/testing.md). Unified identity, invitations, libraries, authorization, history, and migration are in [Tall Tale Platform](docs/tall-tale-platform.md); Studio architecture and operations are in [Tall Tale Studio](docs/tall-tale-studio.md). The future recognition seam is in [vision helper boundary](docs/future-vision-helper.md). Animation ownership, scene contracts, assets, performance, and the development lab are indexed from [animation architecture](docs/animation/architecture.md).\r\n\r\n## Repository workflow\r\n\r\n`main` is canonical. At task start: fetch, inspect status/remotes, and pull with rebase when clean. At task end: validate, review the complete diff, commit intentionally, fetch/rebase if needed, push without force, and verify local `HEAD` equals `origin/main`. See [Codex handoff](docs/codex-handoff.md).\r\n\r\n## Important limitations\r\n\r\nThe full story, final Rive artwork/audio, horizontal scale-out for SSE, and production deployment are later work. The current sound is restrained and procedural. The sole third-party visual sample is MIT-licensed, local, documented, and development-only; production uses original SVG/CSS fallbacks. The repository is public, so only generic development content is permitted. See [known limitations](docs/known-limitations.md).\r\n\r\n## Next steps\r\n\r\nAdd CI for the complete validation gate, exercise the parallel MySQL schema in an integration environment, and replace process-local SSE fan-out before multi-instance deployment. Real surprise content remains blocked until repository visibility is verified private.\r\n--- DEV DOC README ---\r\n# Shared development documentation\r\n\r\n`Development_Docs` is the canonical repository location for shared development references, requirements, architecture notes, design decisions, implementation plans, testing notes, meeting notes, and other planning material that should remain available across Codex sessions and computers.\r\n\r\nFiles in this directory are normal Git-tracked project files. The repository's end-of-task synchronization workflow inspects additions, modifications, renames, moves, and deletions here and, after safety checks, can commit and push them through the same approved branch workflow used for `Codex_Chats`. The files are not copied into the conversation archive or packaged into a second store.\r\n\r\n## Working from another computer\r\n\r\nBefore relying on or editing these documents:\r\n\r\n1. Confirm that this is the intended repository and branch with `git remote -v`, `git branch --show-current`, and `git status --short --branch`.\r\n2. Preserve and review any local modifications. Do not reset, clean, restore, or force-push them away.\r\n3. Run `git fetch --prune origin`, then compare the branch with its upstream using `git rev-list --left-right --count \"HEAD...@{upstream}\"`.\r\n4. If the branch is only behind and the working tree is clean, update it with `git pull --ff-only`. If it is ahead, diverged, or locally modified, reconcile the work deliberately before editing these documents.\r\n5. If Git reports a conflict, preserve both versions and resolve it visibly. Do not push unresolved conflict markers. For an unmergeable binary document, retain clearly named local and remote variants for review when repository conventions permit.\r\n\r\nThis pull-before-edit check matters: a Codex session on a different computer must synchronize the latest repository state before treating local `Development_Docs` content as current.\r\n\r\n## Safety and change behavior\r\n\r\nDo not store secrets, credentials, private keys, access or API tokens, production database exports, private authentication caches, or sensitive company information here unless both the repository and the specific contents have been explicitly approved for that purpose. This repository may be publicly visible. Temporary, recovery, credential-shaped, and obvious secret files are ignored, and the synchronizer also checks changed file names, bounded textual content, and file sizes before staging.\r\n\r\nDeleting a tracked file locally and committing that deletion also removes it from the GitHub version after the commit is pushed. Git history can still preserve earlier versions of documents that were changed or deleted, subject to the repository's retention and history policies.\r\n\r\nPreserve authored formatting and native file types. Do not rewrite a document merely to normalize it, and do not use this directory as a dumping ground for terminal output, build artifacts, dependency archives, database dumps, application installers, or routine generated test results.\r\n\r\nGit tracks files rather than empty directories. A new subdirectory is synchronized automatically when it contains an eligible file; use an intentional `.gitkeep` only when preserving an otherwise empty directory is genuinely necessary.\r\n--- PACKAGE ---\r\n{\n  \"name\": \"forever-treasure-companion\",\n  \"version\": \"0.2.0\",\n  \"private\": true,\n  \"packageManager\": \"npm@11.9.0\",\n  \"engines\": {\n    \"node\": \">=22.0.0\"\n  },\n  \"scripts\": {\n    \"dev\": \"next dev\",\n    \"build\": \"next build\",\n    \"start\": \"next start\",\n    \"lint\": \"eslint .\",\n    \"format:check\": \"prettier --check .\",\n    \"typecheck\": \"tsc --noEmit\",\n    \"test\": \"vitest run\",\n    \"test:watch\": \"vitest\",\n    \"language:validate\": \"tsx scripts/validate-user-facing-language.ts\",\n    \"test:e2e\": \"playwright test\",\n    \"assets:validate\": \"tsx scripts/validate-animation-assets.ts\",\n    \"validate\": \"powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/test-all.ps1\",\n    \"dev:full\": \"powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/start-dev.ps1\",\n    \"dev:stop\": \"powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/stop-dev.ps1\",\n  collection   TaleAssetCollection @relation(fields: [collectionId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\r\n  asset        TaleAsset           @relation(fields: [assetId], references: [id], onDelete:     String\r\n  name                    String\r\n  slug                    String\r\n  region                  String?\r\n  generalDescription      String?\r\n  playerFacingDescription String?\r\n  captainNotes            String?\r\n  mapAssetId              String?\r\n  displayAssetId          String?\r\n  referenceCollectionId   String?\r\n  verificationProfile     String @updatedAt\r\n  tale                    TallTale  @relation(fields: [taleId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\r\n\r\n  @@unique([taleId, slug])\r\n  @@index([taleId,       String?\r\n  loreDescription         String?\r\n  ordinaryGameObjectLabel String?\r\n  artworkAssetId          String?\r\n  revealVideoAssetId      String?\r\n  modelAssetId @default(true)\r\n  archivedAt              DateTime?\r\n  createdAt               DateTime  @default(now())\r\n  updatedAt               DateTime  @updatedAt\r\n  tale                    TallTale  @relation(fields: [taleId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\r\n\r\n  @@index([taleId, sortOrder])\r\n}\r\n\r\nmodel TaleSession {\r\n  id                   String                    @id @default(cuid())\r\n  taleId               String\r\n  publishedVersionId   String?\r\n  ownerLabel           String?\r\n  voyageName           String?\r\n  captainId            String?\r\n  accessTokenHash      String                    @unique\r\n  status               String                    @default(\"ACTIVE\")\r\n  captainMode          String @default(false)\r\n  archiveMetadata      String                    @default(\"{}\")\r\n  currentChapterId     String?\r\n  currentBlockId       String?\r\n  currentSequence      Int                       @default(0)\r\n  previewMode          Boolean                   @default(false)\r\n  draftRevisionId      String?\r\n  previewSnapshot      String?\r\n  variables            String         @updatedAt\r\n  completedAt          DateTime?\r\n  expiresAt            DateTime?\r\n  lastHeartbeatAt      DateTime?\r\n  tale                 TallTale                  @relation(fields: [taleId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\r\n  version              PublishedTaleVersion?     @relation(fields: [publishedVersionId], references: [id])\r\n  events Invitation[]\r\n  revealStates         RevealState[]\r\n\r\n  @@index([taleId, status, previewMode])\r\n  @@index([publishedVersionId, status])\r\n}\r\n\r\nmodel PlayerProfile {\r\n  id displayName])\r\n}\r\n\r\nmodel PlayerIdentitySession {\r\n  id              String        @id @default(cuid())\r\n  playerProfileId String\r\n  tokenHash       String        @unique\r\n  csrfToken @@index([role, scopeType, scopeId, revokedAt])\r\n}\r\n\r\nmodel PlaythroughMembership {\r\n  id              String        @id @default(cuid())\r\n  playthroughId   String\r\n  playerProfileId @updatedAt\r\n  playthrough     TaleSession   @relation(fields: [playthroughId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\r\n  player          PlayerProfile @relation(fields: [playerProfileId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\r\n\r\n  @@unique([playthroughId, playerProfileId])\r\n  @@index([playerProfileId, status, updatedAt])\r\n}\r\n\r\nmodel Invitation {\r\n  id shortCodeHash        String            @unique\r\n  shortCodePrefix      String\r\n  pinHash              String?\r\n  recipientName        String\r\n  status               String DateTime?\r\n  replacesInvitationId String?           @unique\r\n  createdBy            String\r\n  createdAt            DateTime          @default(now())\r\n  updatedAt            DateTime          @updatedAt\r\n  playthrough          TaleSession       @relation(fields: [playthroughId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\r\n  intendedPlayer       PlayerProfile?    @relation(fields: [intendedPlayerId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)\r\n  replaces             Invitation?       @relation(\"InvitationReplacement\", fields: [replacesInvitationId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)\r\n  replacement          Invitation?       @relation(\"InvitationReplacement\")\r\n  events               InvitationEvent[]\r\n\r\n  @@index([playthroughId, status, expiresAt])\r\n  @@index([intendedPlayerId, status])\r\n}\r\n\r\nmodel InvitationEvent {\r\n  id           String     @id @default(cuid())\r\n  invitationId String\r\n  eventType    String\r\n  actorType    String\r\n  actorId      String?\r\n  metadata     String     @default(\"{}\")\r\n  createdAt    DateTime   @default(now())\r\n  invitation   Invitation @relation(fields: [invitationId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\r\n\r\n  @@index([invitationId, createdAt])\r\n}\r\n\r\nmodel RevealState {\r\n  id            String      @id @default(cuid())\r\n  playthroughId String\r\n  contentType   String\r\n  contentKey    String\r\n  status        String      @default(\"REVEALED\")\r\n  revealedBy    String?\r\n  revealedAt    DateTime    @default(now())\r\n  playthrough   TaleSession @relation(fields: [playthroughId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\r\n\r\n  @@unique([playthroughId, contentType, contentKey])\r\n  @@index([playthroughId, revealedAt])\r\n}\r\n\r\nmodel PlatformAuditEvent {\r\n  id            String   @id @default(cuid())\r\n  actorType     String\r\n  actorId       String?\r\n  action        String\r\n  resourceType  String\r\n  resourceId    String\r\n  outcome       String   @default(\"SUCCEEDED\")\r\n  correlationId String\r\n  metadata      String   @default(\"{}\")\r\n  createdAt     DateTime @default(now())\r\n\r\n  @@index([resourceType, resourceId, createdAt])\r\n  @@index([actorType, actorId, createdAt])\r\n  @@index([correlationId])\r\n}\r\n\r\nmodel TaleSessionEvent {\r\n  id                    String      @id @default(cuid())\r\n  sessionId             String\r\n  publishedVersionId    String\r\n  blockId               String?\r\n  eventType             String\r\n  sourceType            String\r\n  sourceId              String?\r\n  idempotencyKey        String      @unique\r\n  payload               String      @default(\"{}\")\r\n  sequence              Int\r\n  correlationId         String?\r\n  verificationRequestId String?\r\n  createdAt             DateTime    @default(now())\r\n  session               TaleSession @relation(fields: [sessionId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\r\n\r\n  @@unique([sessionId, sequence])\r\n  @@index([sessionId, createdAt])\r\n}\r\n\r\nmodel TaleVerificationRequest {\r\n  id                    String                  @id @default(cuid())\r\n  sessionId             String\r\n  blockId               String\r\n  providerType          String\r\n  status                String                  @default(\"PENDING\")\r\n  requestedAt           DateTime                @default(now())\r\n  resolvedAt            DateTime?\r\n  expiresAt             DateTime?\r\n  configurationSnapshot String                  @default(\"{}\")\r\n  providerCorrelationId String?\r\n  satisfiedByEventId    String?\r\n  session               TaleSession             @relation(fields: [sessionId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\r\n  submissions           TaleVerificationEvent[]\r\n\r\n  @@index([sessionId, status, requestedAt])\r\n  @@index([blockId, status])\r\n}\r\n\r\nmodel TaleVerificationEvent {\r\n  id schemaVersion    …25 tokens truncated…ssion) !== submission.publishedVersionId) reason = \"wrongVersion\";\n  else if (request.blockId !== submission.blockId || request.session.currentBlockId !== submission.blockId)\n    reason = \"wrongBlock\";\n  else if (request.status !== \"PENDING\") reason = \"staleRequest\";\n  else if (new Date(submission.observedAt).getTime() < request.requestedAt.getTime() - 1000)\n    reason = \"staleObservation\";\n  else if (request.expiresAt && request.expiresAt < new Date()) reason = \"expiredRequest\";\n  else if (\n    request.providerType !== submission.providerType &&\n    ![\"captainOverride\", \"simulator\"].includes(actor.sourceType)\n  )\n    reason = \"wrongProvider\";\n  if (reason) {\n    logger.warn(\n      {\n        area: \"tall-tale-verification\",\n        sessionId: submission.sessionId,\n        requestId: submission.verificationRequestId,\n        reason,\n      },\n      \"Verification event rejected\",\n    );\n    await db.taleVerificationEvent.create({\n      data: {\n        requestId: request.id,\n        eventId: submission.eventId,\n        idempotencyKey: submission.idempotencyKey,\n        providerType: submission.providerType,\n        providerInstanceId: submission.providerInstanceId,\n        result: submission.result,\n        confidence: submission.confidence,\n        evidence: JSON.stringify(submission.evidence ?? {}),\n        observedAt: new Date(submission.observedAt),\n        accepted: false,\n        rejectionReason: reason,\n VerificationRejectedError(\"unknownBlock\");\n  const event = await db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\n    await tx.taleVerificationEvent.create({\n      data: {\n        requestId: request.id,\n        eventId: submission.eventId,\n        idempotencyKey: submission.idempotencyKey,\n        providerType: submission.providerType,\n        providerInstanceId: submission.providerInstanceId,\n        result: submission.result,\n        confidence: submission.confidence,\n        evidence: JSON.stringify(submission.evidence ?? {}),\n        observedAt: new Date(submission.observedAt),\n        accepted: true,\n      },\n    });\n    const current = await tx.taleSession.findUniqueOrThrow({\n      where: { id: request.sessionId },\n      include: { version: true },\n    });\n    if (submission.result !== \"match\")\n      return appendEvent(tx, current, {\n        eventType: submission.result === \"uncertain\" ? \"verificationUncertain\" : \"verificationRejected\",\n        sourceType: actor.sourceType,\n        sourceId: actor.sourceId,\n        blockId: block.id,\n        idempotencyKey: `${submission.idempotencyKey}:outcome`,\n        payload: { providerType: submission.providerType, confidence: submission.confidence },\n        verificationRequestId: request.id,\n        correlationId: submission.eventId,\n      });\n    await tx.taleVerificationRequest.update({\n      where: { id: request.id },\n      data: { status: \"SATISFIED\", resolvedAt: new Date(), satisfiedByEventId: submission.eventId },\n    });\n    await appendEvent(tx, current, {\n      eventType: actor.sourceType === \"captainOverride\" ? \"captainManualUnlock\" : \"verificationSatisfied\",\n      sourceType: actor.sourceType,\n      sourceId: actor.sourceId,\n      blockId: block.id,\n      idempotencyKey: `${submission.idempotencyKey}:satisfied`,\n      payload: { providerType: submission.providerType, confidence: submission.confidence },\n      verificationRequestId: request.id,\n      correlationId: submission.eventId,\n    });\n    return completeBlock(\n      tx,\n      current,\n      snapshot,\n      block,\n      actor.sourceType,\n      actor.sourceId ?? null,\n      submission.idempotencyKey,\n    );\n  });\n  emit(request.sessionId, event);\n  logger.info(\n    {\n      area: \"tall-tale-verification\",\n      sessionId: request.sessionId,\n      requestId: request.id,\n      sourceType: actor.sourceType,\n    },\n    \"Verification event accepted\",\n  );\n  return { duplicate: false, accepted: true, rejectionReason: null };\n}\n\nexport async function captainSessionAction(\n  sessionId: string,\n  actorId: string,\n  input: {\n    action:\n      | \"approve\"\n      | \"reject\"\n      | \"override\"\n      | \"pause\"\n      | \"resume\"\n      | \"jump\"\n      | \"rollback\"\n      | \"presentation\"\n      | \"releaseHint\";\n    reason?: string;\n    targetBlockId?: string;\n    idempotencyKey: string;\n  },\n) {\n  const session = await db.taleSession.findUniqueOrThrow({\n    where: { id: sessionId },\n    include: {\n      version: true,\n      verificationRequests: { where: { status: \"PENDING\" }, orderBy: { requestedAt: \"desc\" }, take: 1 },\n      events: { where: { eventType: \"blockEntered\" }, orderBy: { sequence: \"desc\" }, take: 2 },\n    },\n  });\n  if (session.captainId && session.captainId !== actorId)\n    throw new Error(\"This voyage is assigned to another Captain.\");\n  const snapshot = snapshotOf(session);\n  const request = session.verificationRequests[0];\n  if ([\"approve\", \"reject\", \"override\"].includes(input.action)) {\n    if (!request) throw new Error(\"This session has no pending verification request.\");\n    return submitVerification(\n      {\n        schemaVersion: 1,\n        eventId: randomUUID(),\n        idempotencyKey: input.idempotencyKey,\n        eventType: \"verification.result\",\n        providerType: request.providerType as VerificationProviderType,\n        providerInstanceId: `captain:${actorId}`,\n        sessionId,\n        publishedVersionId: identity(session),\n        blockId: request.blockId,\n        verificationRequestId: request.id,\n        observedAt: new Date().toISOString(),\n        result: input.action === \"reject\" ? \"notMatch\" : \"match\",\n        confidence: 1,\n        evidence: { reason: input.reason ?? \"Captain action\", override: input.action === \"override\" },\n      },\n      { sourceType: input.action === \"override\" ? \"captainOverride\" : \"captainManual\", sourceId: actorId },\n    );\n  }\n  const event = await db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\n    const current = await tx.taleSession.findUniqueOrThrow({ where: { id: sessionId }, include: { version: true } });\n    if (input.action === \"pause\" || input.action === \"resume\") {\n      await tx.taleSession.update({\n        where: { id: sessionId },\n        data: { status: input.action === \"pause\" ? \"PAUSED\" : \"ACTIVE\" },\n      });\n      return appendEvent(tx, current, {\n        eventType: input.action === \"pause\" ? \"sessionPaused\" : \"sessionResumed\",\n        sourceType: \"captain\",\n        sourceId: actorId,\n        blockId: current.currentBlockId,\n        idempotencyKey: input.idempotencyKey,\n        payload: { reason: input.reason ?? null },\n      });\n    }\n    if (input.action === \"presentation\" || input.action === \"releaseHint\")\n      return appendEvent(tx, current, {\n        eventType: input.action === \"presentation\" ? \"presentationTriggered\" : \"hintReleased\",\n        sourceType: \"captain\",\n        sourceId: actorId,\n        blockId: current.currentBlockId,\n        idempotencyKey: input.idempotencyKey,\n        payload: { reason: input.reason ?? null },\n      });\n    const targetId = input.action === \"rollback\" ? session.events[1]?.blockId : input.targetBlockId;\n    const target = blockById(snapshot, targetId ?? null);\n    if (!target) throw new Error(\"Choose a valid target block.\");\n    await tx.taleVerificationRequest.updateMany({\n      where: { sessionId, status: \"PENDING\" },\n      data: { status: \"CANCELLED\", resolvedAt: new Date() },\n    });\n    await tx.taleSession.update({ where: { id: sessionId }, data: { status: \"ACTIVE\" } });\n    const audit = await appendEvent(tx, current, {\n      eventType: input.action === \"rollback\" ? \"captainRollback\" : \"captainJump\",\n      sourceType: \"captain\",\n      sourceId: actorId,\n      blockId: current.currentBlockId,\n      idempotencyKey: input.idempotencyKey,\n      payload: {\n        fromBlockId: current.currentBlockId,\n        targetBlockId: target.id,\n        reason: input.reason ?? \"No reason supplied\",\n      },\n    });\n    await enterBlock(tx, current, snapshot, target, \"captain\", actorId, input.idempotencyKey);\n    return audit;\n  });\n  emit(sessionId, event);\n  return { accepted: true };\n}\n\nexport async function listCaptainSessions() {\n  const sessions = await db.taleSession.findMany({\n    orderBy: { updatedAt: \"desc\" },\n    take: 100,\n    include: { tale: true, version: true, verificationRequests: { where: { status: \"PENDING\" }, take: 1 } },\n  });\n  return sessions.map((session) => ({\n    id: session.id,\n    taleId: session.taleId,\n    taleTitle: session.tale.title,\n    versionLabel: session.version?.versionLabel ?? \"Draft preview\",\n    ownerLabel: session.ownerLabel,\n    status: session.status,\n    previewMode: session.previewMode,\n    currentBlockId: session.currentBlockId,\n    pendingVerification: session.verificationRequests[0]\n      ? {\n          id: session.verificationRequests[0].id,\n          providerType: session.verificationRequests[0].providerType,\n          requestedAt: session.verificationRequests[0].requestedAt.toISOString(),\n        }\n      : null,\n    lastEventAt: session.updatedAt.toISOString(),\n    connected: Boolean(session.lastHeartbeatAt && Date.now() - session.lastHeartbeatAt.getTime() < 45000),\n  }));\n}\n\nexport async function createHelperPairing(sessionId: string, deviceId: string) {\n  const session = await db.taleSession.findUniqueOrThrow({ where: { id: sessionId } });\n  if (!session.publishedVersionId || session.previewMode || ![\"ACTIVE\", \"PAUSED\"].includes(session.status))\n    throw new Error(\"Helper devices can only pair with an active published tale session.\");\n  const token = randomBytes(32).toString(\"base64url\");\n  const pairing = await db.taleHelperPairing.create({\n    data: { sessionId, deviceId, tokenHash: digest(token), expiresAt: new Date(Date.now() + 15 * 60 * 1000) },\n  });\n  return { pairingId: pairing.id, token, expiresAt: pairing.expiresAt.toISOString() };\n}\n\nexport async function getHelperScope(token: string) {\n  const pairing = await db.taleHelperPairing.findFirst({\n    where: { tokenHash: digest(token), status: \"ACTIVE\", revokedAt: null, expiresAt: { gt: new Date() } },\n    include: {\n      session: {\n        include: {\n          verificationRequests: { where: { status: \"PENDING\" }, orderBy: { requestedAt: \"desc\" }, take: 1 },\n        },\n      },\n    },\n  });\n  if (!pairing || !pairing.session.publishedVersionId || pairing.session.previewMode)\n    throw new VerificationRejectedError(\"invalidPairing\");\n  const now = new Date();\n  await db.$transaction([\n    db.taleHelperPairing.update({ where: { id: pairing.id }, data: { lastSeenAt: now } }),\n    db.taleSession.update({ where: { id: pairing.sessionId }, data: { lastHeartbeatAt: now } }),\n  ]);\n  const request = pairing.session.verificationRequests[0] ?? null;\n  return {\n    pairing: {\n      id: pairing.id,\n      deviceId: pairing.deviceId,\n      status: pairing.status,\n      expiresAt: pairing.expiresAt.toISOString(),\n      heartbeatAt: now.toISOString(),\n    },\n    scope: {\n      sessionId: pairing.sessionId,\n      sessionStatus: pairing.session.status,\n      publishedVersionId: pairing.session.publishedVersionId,\n      currentBlockId: pairing.session.currentBlockId,\n      verificationRequest: request\n        ? {\n            id: request.id,\n            blockId: request.blockId,\n            providerType: request.providerType,\n            status: request.status,\n            requestedAt: request.requestedAt.toISOString(),\n            expiresAt: request.expiresAt?.toISOString() ?? null,\n          }\n        : null,\n    },\n  };\n}\n\nexport async function revokeHelperPairing(pairingId: string, actorId: string) {\n  const pairing = await db.taleHelperPairing.findUniqueOrThrow({ where: { id: pairingId } });\n  const revokedAt = new Date();\n  const updated = await db.taleHelperPairing.update({\n    where: { id: pairingId },\n    data: { status: \"REVOKED\", revokedAt },\n  });\n  const session = await db.taleSession.findUniqueOrThrow({ where: { id: pairing.sessionId } });\n  const event = await db.$transaction((tx) =>\n    appendEvent(tx, session, {\n      eventType: \"helperPairingRevoked\",\n      sourceType: \"captain\",\n      sourceId: actorId,\n      blockId: session.currentBlockId,\n      idempotencyKey: `helper-revoke:${pairingId}`,\n      payload: { pairingId, deviceId: pairing.deviceId },\n    }),\n  );\n  emit(session.id, event);\n  return { id: updated.id, status: updated.status, revokedAt: revokedAt.toISOString() };\n}\n\nexport async function submitHelperVerification(token: string, submission: VerificationSubmission) {\n  const pairing = await db.taleHelperPairing.findFirst({\n    where: { tokenHash: digest(token), status: \"ACTIVE\", revokedAt: null, expiresAt: { gt: new Date() } },\n  });\n  if (!pairing || pairing.sessionId !== submission.sessionId) throw new VerificationRejectedError(\"invalidPairing\");\n  await db.taleHelperPairing.update({ where: { id: pairing.id }, data: { lastSeenAt: new Date() } });\n  return submitVerification(submission, { sourceType: \"helper\", sourceId: pairing.deviceId });\n}\n\r\nimport { createHash } from \"node:crypto\";\nimport { db } from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { eventBus } from \"@/lib/events\";\nimport { getStudioTale } from \"@/tall-tale/studio-service\";\nimport type { PublishedTaleSnapshot } from \"@/tall-tale/types\";\nimport { validateTaleDraft } from \"@/tall-tale/validation\";\nimport { logger } from \"@/lib/logger\";\n\nexport class PublishValidationError extends Error {\n  constructor(public readonly validation: Awaited<ReturnType<typeof validateTaleDraft>>) {\n    super(\"This Chronicle cannot be published yet. Resolve the blocking validation issues, then publish again.\");\n  }\n}\n\nexport function snapshotFromStudio(studio: Awaited<ReturnType<typeof getStudioTale>>): PublishedTaleSnapshot {\n  return {\n    schemaVersion: 1,\n    tale: {\n      id: studio.tale.id,\n      slug: studio.tale.slug,\n      title: studio.tale.title,\n      subtitle: studio.tale.subtitle,\n      shortDescription: studio.tale.shortDescription,\n      longDescription: studio.tale.longDescription,\n      coverAssetId: studio.tale.coverAssetId,\n      theme: studio.tale.theme,\n      visibility: studio.tale.visibility,\n      playerCountMin: studio.tale.playerCountMin,\n      playerCountMax: studio.tale.playerCountMax,\n      estimatedDuration: studio.tale.estimatedDuration,\n      contentWarnings: studio.tale.contentWarnings,\n    },\n    chapters: studio.draft.chapters.map((chapter, chapterIndex) => ({\n      id: chapter.id,\n      title: chapter.title,\n      subtitle: chapter.subtitle,\n      description: chapter.description,\n      coverAssetId: chapter.coverAssetId,\n      estimatedDuration: chapter.estimatedDuration,\n      isOptional: chapter.isOptional,\n      metadata: chapter.metadata,\n      orderIndex: chapterIndex,\n      entryBlockId: chapter.blocks[0]?.id ?? null,\n      completionBlockId:\n        [...chapter.blocks]\n          .reverse()\n          .find((block) => block.blockType === \"chapterComplete\" || block.blockType === \"taleComplete\")?.id ??\n        chapter.blocks.at(-1)?.id ??\n        null,\n      blocks: chapter.blocks.map((block, blockIndex) => ({\n        id: block.id,\n        chapterId: chapter.id,\n        blockType: block.blockType,\n        title: block.title,\n        internalLabel: block.internalLabel,\n        configuration: block.configuration,\n        presentation: block.presentation,\n        completion: block.completion,\n        creatorNotes: null,\n        isEnabled: block.isEnabled,\n        schemaVersion: block.schemaVersion,\n        orderIndex: blockIndex,\n        nextBlockId: block.connections[0]?.targetBlockId ?? null,\n        connections: block.connections.map((connection, connectionIndex) => ({\n          ...connection,\n          orderIndex: connectionIndex,\n        })),\n      })),\n    })),\n    assets: studio.assets.map((asset) => ({\n      id: asset.id,\n      mediaType: asset.mediaType,\n      displayName: asset.displayName,\n      description: asset.description,\n      mimeType: asset.mimeType,\n      width: asset.width,\n      height: asset.height,\n      roles: asset.roles,\n      variants: asset.variants.map((variant) => ({\n        id: variant.id,\n        role: variant.role,\n        mimeType: variant.mimeType,\n        processingState: variant.processingState,\n      })),\n    })) as PublishedTaleSnapshot[\"assets\"],\n    locations: studio.locations.map((location) => ({ ...location, captainNotes: undefined })),\n    artifacts: studio.artifacts,\n    publishedAt: new Date().toISOString(),\n  };\n}\n\nexport async function publishTale(\n  taleId: string,\n  publisherId: string,\n  releaseNotes: string,\n  expectedAutosaveVersion?: number,\n) {\n  logger.info({ area: \"tall-tale-publish\", taleId, publisherId }, \"Chronicle publish validation started\");\n  const validation = await validateTaleDraft(taleId);\n  if (!validation.valid) throw new PublishValidationError(validation);\n  if (expectedAutosaveVersion !== undefined && validation.autosaveVersion !== expectedAutosaveVersion)\n    throw new Error(\"This Chronicle changed before publishing. Review the latest saved draft, then try again.\");\n  const studio = await getStudioTale(taleId);\n  if (studio.draft.autosaveVersion !== validation.autosaveVersion)\n    throw new Error(\"This Chronicle changed during validation. Review the current draft, then publish again.\");\n  const snapshot = snapshotFromStudio(studio);\n  const contentSnapshot = JSON.stringify(snapshot);\n  const checksum = createHash(\"sha256\").update(contentSnapshot).digest(\"hex\");\n  const version = await db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\n    const latest = await tx.publishedTaleVersion.findFirst({ where: { taleId }, orderBy: { versionNumber: \"desc\" } });\n    const versionNumber = (latest?.versionNumber ?? 0) + 1;\n    const structuredReleaseNotes =\n      releaseNotes.trim() ||\n      `Published ${snapshot.chapters.length} Chapters, ${snapshot.chapters.reduce((count, chapter) => count + chapter.blocks.length, 0)} Passages, and ${snapshot.assets.length} assets${latest ? ` from Version ${latest.versionLabel}` : \" as the first published Version\"}.`;\n    await tx.publishedTaleVersion.updateMany({ where: { taleId, isCurrent: true }, data: { isCurrent: false } });\n    const created = await tx.publishedTaleVersion.create({\n      data: {\n        taleId,\n        versionNumber,\n        versionLabel: versionNumber === 1 ? \"1.0\" : `1.${versionNumber - 1}`,\n        publishedBy: publisherId,\n        releaseNotes: structuredReleaseNotes,\n        contentSnapshot,\n        checksum,\n        isCurrent: true,\n      },\n    });\n    await tx.tallTale.update({\n      where: { id: taleId },\n      data: { status: \"PUBLISHED\", latestPublishedVersionId: created.id },\n    });\n    await tx.platformAuditEvent.create({\n      data: {\n        actorType: \"CREATOR\",\n        actorId: publisherId,\n        action: \"TALE_VERSION_PUBLISHED\",\n        resourceType: \"PUBLISHED_TALE_VERSION\",\n        resourceId: created.id,\n        correlationId: crypto.randomUUID(),\n        metadata: JSON.stringify({ taleId, versionLabel: created.versionLabel, checksum: created.checksum }),\n      },\n    });\n    return created;\n  });\n  eventBus.emit(\"tall-tale:catalog\", {\n    type: \"catalog.updated\",\n    taleId,\n    versionId: version.id,\n    at: version.publishedAt.toISOString(),\n  });\n  logger.info(\n    { area: \"tall-tale-publish\", taleId, versionId: version.id, versionLabel: version.versionLabel },\n    \"Immutable Chronicle version published\",\n  );\n  return {\n    id: version.id,\n    versionNumber: version.versionNumber,\n    versionLabel: version.versionLabel,\n    checksum: version.checksum,\n    publishedAt: version.publishedAt.toISOString(),\n  };\n}\n\nexport function parsePublishedSnapshot(raw: string): PublishedTaleSnapshot {\n  const snapshot = JSON.parse(raw) as PublishedTaleSnapshot;\n  if (snapshot.schemaVersion !== 1 || !Array.isArray(snapshot.chapters))\n    throw new Error(\"This Chronicle version uses an unsupported format. Update Voyagewright, then try again.\");\n  return snapshot;\n}\n\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

EXISTING PLATFORM DOC EXCERPT ---\r\n# Tall Tale Platform\r\n\r\n## Product surfaces\r\n\r\n`/` is a cinematic three-role gateway. Choosing Player, Captain, or Creator selects a workspace; it never grants a permission. Server sessions, capabilities, scoped assignments, playthrough membership, and exact resource ownership remain authoritative.\r\n\r\n- `/player/sign-in`, `/player/invitation`, and `/player/library` provide durable Player identity, code/link acceptance, and card-based browsing. `/player/playthroughs/[id]` owns invitation/lobby/waiting state. `/player/playthroughs/[id]/journal` is the canonical active, resumed, paused, and completed story surface; the former archive route redirects there.\r\n- `/captain/sign-in`, `/captain/library`, `/captain/invitations`, `/captain/sessions/[id]`, and Player-safe preview routes provide operational voyage control without exposing Creator-only internals.\r\n- `/studio/sign-in`, `/studio/library`, and `/studio/tales/*` remain the authoring, validation, asset, version, fork, and publishing workspace.\r\n- `/tales`, legacy token sessions, `/quartermaster`, and the original companion remain compatibility surfaces.\r\n\r\nThe gateway may offer a remembered-session continuation, but every destination repeats authorization. Workspace switching links never upgrade the current account.\r\n\r\n## Domain and lifecycle\r\n\r\n`TallTale` is the authored identity. `PublishedTaleVersion` is an immutable checksummed snapshot. `TaleSession` is the playthrough aggregate and stores the exact `publishedVersionId`, Captain assignment, configuration, schedule, state, concurrency token, runtime pointers, inventory, and ordered events.\r\n\r\n`PlayerProfile` and rotating `PlayerIdentitySession` records provide durable identity. `PlaythroughMembership` associates each Player with one playthrough and carries invitation/ready/active/completed/declined/removed state plus per-Player pin/hide preferences. `Invitation` stores only token/code/PIN hashes and safe prefixes; `InvitationEvent` records lifecycle. `RevealState` records content actually revealed. `PlatformRoleAssignment` supplements existing staff capabilities. `PlatformAuditEvent` records resource actions with correlation IDs and secret-filtered metadata.\r\n\r\nSupported playthrough progression is `DRAFT_SETUP -> INVITING -> READY/SCHEDULED -> ACTIVE <-> PAUSED -> COMPLETED`, with cancellation before launch and abandonment after launch. Memberships move through invited, accepted, ready, active, and completed or terminate as declined/removed. Invitation links and codes move through created/sent/copied/viewed into ready/consumed, or terminate as declined/expired/revoked/replaced. Terminal transitions do not reopen in place.\r\n\r\n## Invitations and crew launch\r\n\r\nThe Captain wizard selects an exact published version, configures progression/hints/side quests/schedule/accessibility, adds one or more Players, chooses PIN/account/lifetime policy, reviews Player-safe content, and creates the playthrough plus all membership/invitation records in one transaction. Each crew member receives a distinct high-entropy link, QR code, human short code, and copyable message. Full secrets are returned only by creation or replacement responses and are never listed again.\r\n\r\nLink and code resolution is rate-limited. The pending invitation is held in a short-lived HttpOnly SameSite cookie with its own CSRF token. Acceptance atomically claims one redemption, binds or creates the Player identity, readies membership, records invitation/audit events, and readies the playthrough only when no invitation remains pending. Safe retries of an already accepted credential return the existing membership. Expired, declined, revoked, and replaced credentials are rejected before protected content is rendered. Account-required invitations can target only an existing claimed Player and must be accepted while signed into that exact profile.\r\n\r\nLaunch requires an assigned Captain, a ready/scheduled playthrough, at least one ready Player, the expected concurrency version, and a playable entry block. It promotes ready memberships, consumes accepted invitations, appends ordered launch/block events, records reveal state, and pushes sanitized realtime progression. The waiting room polls canonical state as a fallback and opens the runtime only after persisted launch.\r\n\r\n## Projection and authorization rules\r\n\r\nPlayer projections are allowlists. They contain current Player-safe Tale copy, revealed blocks/assets, crew readiness, and exact edition metadata; accepted answers, Captain instructions, Creator notes, future branches, private variables, storage keys, and raw event payloads are removed. Completed archives are rebuilt from the pinned published snapshot plus visited events/reveal state, not from the current draft or latest release.\r\n\r\nThe journal projection extends that allowlist to all released readable blocks, typed presentation metadata, released hints, visited-choice summaries, current-objective indexes, and completion/edition data. Recursive sanitization runs before any page is built. Logic-only blocks and unreleased future content are omitted. Player reading position is stored separately in profile preferences and cannot advance, rewind, or otherwise mutate `TaleSession` progression.\r\n\r\nMedia reads require one of four explicit scopes: authenticated Creator asset management; Player membership in the exact version-bound playthrough plus reveal/role inclusion; pending invitation cover access; or the current public catalog cover. Original downloads require asset-management permission. SSE authenticates before streaming and periodically rechecks membership, emitting an access-revoked close signal when authorization changes.\r\n\r\nAll cookie-authenticated staff and Player mutations require CSRF, except legacy compatibility runtime cookies whose SameSite boundary and opaque session token are retained. Login, invitation-code lookup, Player actions, uploads, and helper endpoints are bounded by rate limits. Identity and invitation secrets are cryptographically random, stored only as hashes, sent through HttpOnly Secure-in-production cookies where applicable, and rotated/revoked on session replacement or logout. Audit metadata rejects secret-, answer-, snapshot-, payload-, and private-note-bearing fields.\r\n\r\n## Migration, backfill, and rollback\r\n\r\nSQLite migration `20260718020000_tall_tale_platform` and MySQL migration `0004_tall_tale_platform` are additive. They add platform fields and tables without rebuilding `TaleSession`, so existing IDs, version bindings, event sequences, access hashes, inventory, variables, and timestamps remain intact. The progress-preserving seed path creates missing development Player/staff assignments and backfills only non-preview sessions without memberships. Each gets a placeholder Player membership mapped from the existing session state, a voyage/launch value derived from existing fields, and block reveal records derived from historical `blockEntered` events. It does not reset campaign or Tall Tale progress.\r\n\r\n`scripts/verify-platform-backfill.ts` creates a legacy-shaped proof row in the disposable validation database, runs normal `--ensure`, then proves identity, event, version, membership, reveal, and timestamp preservation. `scripts/verify-database.ts` additionally checks published checksums, scoped development roles, membership coverage, exact version relations, and audit metadata.\r\n\r\nThere is no destructive down migration. Before production migration, back up the database and asset volume and verify restore. Rollback is application rollback plus restoration of that matched backup. The MySQL SQL is maintained for production parity, but a live MySQL integration environment is still required before production rollout.\r\n\r\n## Validation\r\n\r\nUnit tests cover playthrough/membership/invitation state rules, scoped authorization, and audit redaction. The Chromium mutation journey forks and publishes a Tale, creates and accepts an invitation, denies cross-role access and missing CSRF, launches, publishes a newer edition, proves the live session remains pinned, completes the Tale, validates the exact-version archive, persists pin/hide preferences, proves idempotent acceptance, and verifies replacement/revocation. The reduced-motion responsive gateway and authorization denials also run as a read-only mobile WebKit check. The repository release gate remains `npm run validate`.\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]
