[{"text": "Script completed\nWall time 1.2 seconds\nOutput:\n", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "Exit code: 0\nWall time: 1.1 seconds\nOutput:\n--- SNAPSHOT ---\r\nimport type {\n  ClientProgressEvent,\n  PublicArtifact,\n  PublicChapter,\n  PublicSideQuest,\n  PublicSnapshot,\n} from \"@/domain/story\";\nimport { projectChapterReleaseReplay } from \"@/domain/replay\";\nimport { eventToLogEntry } from \"@/domain/ships-log\";\nimport { isPlayerPresentationEventType, playerPresentationEventTypes, toClientEvent } from \"@/domain/visibility\";\nimport { db } from \"@/lib/db\";\n\nconst readableChapterStates = new Set([\"REVEALING\", \"ACTIVE\", \"SOLVED\", \"COMPLETE\"]);\nconst detailedQuestStates = new Set([\"DISCOVERED\", \"ACTIVE\", \"PARTIALLY_COMPLETE\", \"COMPLETE\", \"ARCHIVED\"]);\nconst detailedArtifactStates = new Set([\"DISCOVERED\", \"AWARDED\", \"INSPECTED\", \"CONNECTED\", \"ASSEMBLED\"]);\nconst coordinateStates = new Set([\"REVEALED\", \"ACTIVE_DESTINATION\", \"VISITED\", \"COMPLETED\", \"SIDE_QUEST\"]);\n\nexport const MAX_PLAYER_PRESENTATION_HISTORY = 100;\n\ntype StoredPresentationEvent = {\n  id: string;\n  type: string;\n  sequence: number;\n  version: number;\n  payload: string;\n  releaseAt: Date;\n  reversesEventId?: string | null;\n  supersededById?: string | null;\n};\n\nexport type PublicSnapshotWithPresentationHistory = PublicSnapshot & {\n  /**\n   * Bounded, oldest-first, Player-safe presentation events. This is delivery\n   * history, not proof that any scene was presented or acknowledged.\n   */\n  presentationHistory: ClientProgressEvent[];\n};\n\nexport function buildPlayerPresentationHistory(\n  storedEvents: readonly StoredPresentationEvent[],\n  chapters: readonly PublicChapter[],\n): ClientProgressEvent[] {\n  const byId = new Map<string, StoredPresentationEvent>();\n  for (const event of storedEvents) {\n    if (!isPlayerPresentationEventType(event.type)) continue;\n    const current = byId.get(event.id);\n    if (!current || event.sequence > current.sequence) byId.set(event.id, event);\n  }\n\n  return [...byId.values()]\n    .sort((left, right) => left.sequence - right.sequence || left.id.localeCompare(right.id))\n    .flatMap((event): ClientProgressEvent[] => {\n      const clientEvent = toClientEvent(event);\n      if (event.type !== \"CHAPTER_RELEASED\") return [clientEvent];\n      const replay = projectChapterReleaseReplay(event, chapters);\n      // A locked, reverted, missing, or invalid chapter has no Player-safe\n      // history fallback. Even the formerly public title is omitted.\n      if (replay.status === \"unavailable\") return [];\n      const authorizedChapter = chapters.find((chapter) => chapter.ordinal === replay.presentation.payload.ordinal);\n      if (!authorizedChapter?.title) return [];\n      return [\n        {\n          ...clientEvent,\n          // All chapter copy, including title, comes from the currently\n          // authorized readable chapter projection rather than stored payload.\n          payload: {\n            ...replay.presentation.payload,\n            title: authorizedChapter.title,\n          },\n        },\n      ];\n    })\n    .slice(-MAX_PLAYER_PRESENTATION_HISTORY);\n}\n\nfunction requirementList(raw: string): PublicSnapshot[\"finale\"][\"requirements\"] {\n  try {\n    const parsed = JSON.parse(raw);\n    return Array.isArray(parsed)\n      ? parsed.filter((item) => item && typeof item.key === \"string\" && typeof item.label === \"string\")\n      : [];\n  } catch {\n    return [];\n  }\n}\n\nexport async function buildPublicSnapshot(\n  campaignId: string,\n  playerAccessId?: string,\n  options?: Readonly<{ offlineAfterSequence?: number; synchronizedAt?: Date }>,\n): Promise<PublicSnapshotWithPresentationHistory> {\n  const campaign = await db.campaign.findUniqueOrThrow({\n    where: { id: campaignId },\n    include: {\n      chapters: {\n        orderBy: { ordinal: \"asc\" },\n        include: { content: true, clues: { orderBy: { ordinal: \"asc\" } }, hints: { orderBy: { ordinal: \"asc\" } } },\n      },\n      artifacts: { orderBy: { displayX: \"asc\" }, include: { awards: true } },\n      mapLocations: { orderBy: { key: \"asc\" } },\n      mapRoutes: { orderBy: { ordinal: \"asc\" } },\n      sideQuests: { orderBy: { key: \"asc\" }, include: { objectives: { orderBy: { ordinal: \"asc\" } } } },\n      journalEntries: { where: { releasedAt: { not: null } }, orderBy: { createdAt: \"asc\" } },\n      events: { where: { releaseAt: { lte: new Date() } }, orderBy: { sequence: \"desc\" }, take: 250 },\n    },\n  });\n  const [viewed, latestChapterRelease, storedPresentationHistory] = await Promise.all([\n    playerAccessId\n      ? db.viewedContent.findMany({\n          where: { playerAccessId },\n          select: { contentType: true, contentKey: true },\n        })\n      : Promise.resolve([]),\n    db.progressEvent.findFirst({\n      where: {\n        campaignId,\n        type: \"CHAPTER_RELEASED\",\n        releaseAt: { lte: new Date() },\n      },\n      orderBy: { sequence: \"desc\" },\n      select: {\n        id: true,\n        type: true,\n        sequence: true,\n        version: true,\n        payload: true,\n        releaseAt: true,\n      },\n    }),\n    db.progressEvent.findMany({\n      where: {\n        campaignId,\n        type: { in: [...playerPresentationEventTypes] },\n        releaseAt: { lte: new Date() },\n      },\n      orderBy: [{ sequence: \"desc\" }, { id: \"desc\" }],\n      take: MAX_PLAYER_PRESENTATION_HISTORY,\n      select: {\n        id: true,\n        type: true,\n        sequence: true,\n        version: true,\n        payload: true,\n        releaseAt: true,\n        reversesEventId: true,\n        supersededById: true,\n      },\n    }),\n  ]);\n  const viewedKeys = new Set(viewed.map((item) => `${item.contentType}:${item.contentKey}`));\n  const isUnseen = (type: string, key: string, released: boolean) => released && !viewedKeys.has(`${type}:${key}`);\n\n  const chapters: PublicChapter[] = campaign.chapters.map((item) => {\n    const readable = readableChapterStates.has(item.state);\n    const chapter: PublicChapter = {\n      ordinal: item.ordinal,\n      state: item.state as PublicChapter[\"state\"],\n      ...(item.safeTeaser ? { teaser: item.safeTeaser } : {}),\n      unseen: isUnseen(\"chapter\", String(item.ordinal), readable),\n    };\n    if (!readable) return chapter;\n    const annotations = campaign.journalEntries\n      .filter((entry) => entry.chapterOrdinal === item.ordinal)\n      .map((entry) => ({\n        key: entry.id,\n        title: entry.title,\n        body: entry.body,\n        createdAt: entry.createdAt.toISOString(),\n        unseen: isUnseen(\"annotation\", entry.id, true),\n      }));\n    return {\n      ...chapter,\n      title: item.content.title,\n      narrative: item.content.narrative,\n      objective: item.content.objective,\n      riddle: item.clues[0]?.body,\n      hints: item.hints\n        .filter((hint) => hint.releasedAt)\n        .map((hint) => ({\n          ordinal: hint.ordinal,\n          body: hint.body,\n          releasedAt: hint.releasedAt!.toISOString(),\n          unseen: isUnseen(\"hint\", hint.id, true),\n        })),\n      annotations,\n      related: {\n        ...(item.relatedMapKey ? { mapKey: item.relatedMapKey } : {}),\n        ...(item.relatedArtifactKey ? { artifactKey: item.relatedArtifactKey } : {}),\n        ...(item.relatedSideQuestKey ? { sideQuestKey: item.relatedSideQuestKey } : {}),\n      },\n    };\n  });\n  const chapter =\n    [...chapters].reverse().find((item) => [\"REVEALING\", \"ACTIVE\", \"SOLVED\"].includes(item.state)) ??\n    chapters.find((item) => item.state !== \"LOCKED\") ??\n    chapters[0];\n\n  const artifacts: PublicArtifact[] = campaign.artifacts.map((item) => {\n    const state = item.awards.length\n      ? item.state === \"UNKNOWN\" || item.state === \"SILHOUETTE\"\n        ? \"AWARDED\"\n        : item.state\n      : item.state;\n    const detailed = detailedArtifactStates.has(state);\n    return {\n      key: item.key,\n      state,\n      displayX: item.displayX,\n      displayY: item.displayY,\n      ...(item.assemblyGroup\n        ? { assemblyGroup: item.assemblyGroup, assemblyPosition: item.assemblyPosition ?? undefined }\n        : {}),\n      ...(state === \"SILHOUETTE\"\n        ? { safeName: item.safeName ?? undefined, silhouetteLabel: item.silhouetteLabel ?? \"Unidentified relic\" }\n        : {}),\n      ...(detailed\n        ? {\n            name: item.name,\n            category: item.category,\n            description: item.description,\n            discoveryText: item.discoveryText ?? undefined,\n            connectedArtifactKey: item.connectedArtifactKey ?? undefined,\n            chapterOrdinal: item.chapterOrdinal ?? undefined,\n            awardedAt: item.awards[0]?.awardedAt.toISOString(),\n          }\n        : {}),\n      unseen: isUnseen(\"artifact\", item.key, state !== \"UNKNOWN\"),\n    };\n  });\n\n  const mapLocations = campaign.mapLocations\n    .filter((item) => item.state !== \"UNKNOWN\")\n    .map((item) => {\n      const exact = coordinateStates.has(item.state) && Boolean(item.revealedAt);\n      return {\n        key: item.key,\n        state: item.state,\n        label: exact ? item.name : (item.safeLabel ?? \"A place beyond the fog\"),\n        name: exact ? item.name : (item.safeLabel ?? \"A place beyond the fog\"),\n        ...(exact\n          ? {\n              regionLabel: item.regionLabel,\n              locationType: item.locationType,\n              description: item.description ?? undefined,\n              exactness: item.exactness,\n              x: item.x,\n              y: item.y,\n              mobileX: item.mobileX ?? undefined,\n              mobileY: item.mobileY ?? undefined,\n              chapterOrdinal: item.chapterOrdinal ?? undefined,\n              sideQuestKey: item.sideQuestKey ?? undefined,\n            }\n          : {}),\n        unseen: isUnseen(\"map\", item.key, Boolean(item.revealedAt)),\n      };\n    });\n  const visibleLocationKeys = new Set(mapLocations.filter((item) => item.x !== undefined).map((item) => item.key));\n  const mapRoutes = campaign.mapRoutes\n    .filter(\n      (route) =>\n        route.state !== \"HIDDEN\" &&\n        route.revealedAt &&\n        visibleLocationKeys.has(route.fromKey) &&\n        visibleLocationKeys.has(route.toKey),\n    )\n    .map((route) => ({\n      key: route.key,\n      fromKey: route.fromKey,\n      toKey: route.toKey,\n      ordinal: route.ordinal,\n      state: route.state,\n      ...(route.annotation ? { annotation: route.annotation } : {}),\n      unseen: isUnseen(\"route\", route.key, true),\n    }));\n\n  const sideQuests: PublicSideQuest[] = campaign.sideQuests\n    .filter((item) => item.state !== \"HIDDEN\")\n    .map((item) => {\n      const detailed = detailedQuestStates.has(item.state);\n      return {\n        key: item.key,\n        state: item.state,\n        ...(item.safeTeaser ? { teaser: item.safeTeaser } : {}),\n        ...(detailed\n          ? {\n              title: item.title,\n              description: item.description ?? undefined,\n              objectives: item.objectives.map(({ ordinal, body, complete }) => ({ ordinal, body, complete })),\n              reward: item.rewardType ? { type: item.rewardType, label: item.rewardLabel ?? undefined } : undefined,\n              completionSummary: item.state === \"COMPLETE\" ? (item.completionSummary ?? undefined) : undefined,\n              chapterOrdinal: item.chapterOrdinal ?? undefined,\n              mapLocationKey: item.mapLocationKey ?? undefined,\n              artifactKey: item.artifactKey ?? undefined,\n            }\n          : {}),\n        unseen: isUnseen(\"quest\", item.key, item.state !== \"RUMORED\"),\n      };\n    });\n\n  const offlineSynchronization =\n    options?.offlineAfterSequence !== undefined\n      ? {\n          afterSequence: options.offlineAfterSequence,\n          synchronizedAt: options.synchronizedAt ?? new Date(),\n        }\n      : undefined;\n  const log = campaign.events\n    .map((event) => eventToLogEntry(event, isUnseen(\"log\", event.id, true), offlineSynchronization))\n    .filter((entry): entry is NonNullable<typeof entry> => Boolean(entry));\n  const finaleReleased = campaign.finaleState !== \"HIDDEN\";\n  const finale = {\n    state: campaign.finaleState,\n    ...(finaleReleased && campaign.finaleTeaser ? { teaser: campaign.finaleTeaser } : {}),\n    requirements: finaleReleased ? requirementList(campaign.finaleRequirements) : [],\n    unseen: isUnseen(\"finale\", campaign.finaleState, finaleReleased),\n  };\n  const unseen = {\n    journal:\n      chapters.filter((item) => item.unseen).length +\n      chapters.flatMap((item) => item.hints ?? []).filter((item) => item.unseen).length,\n    chart: mapLocations.filter((item) => item.unseen).length + mapRoutes.filter((item) => item.unseen).length,\n    treasures: artifacts.filter((item) => item.unseen).length,\n    quests: sideQuests.filter((item) => item.unseen).length,\n    log: log.filter((item) => item.unseen).length,\n    finale: finale.unseen ? 1 : 0,\n  };\n  const replayProjection = latestChapterRelease ? projectChapterReleaseReplay(latestChapterRelease, chapters) : null;\n  const presentationHistory = buildPlayerPresentationHistory(storedPresentationHistory, chapters);\n  return {\n    campaign: { slug: campaign.slug, title: campaign.title, status: campaign.status },\n    sequence: campaign.currentSequence,\n    chapter,\n    chapters,\n    artifacts,\n    mapLocations,\n    mapRoutes,\n    sideQuests,\n    sideQuest: sideQuests.find((item) => item.title)\n      ? { title: sideQuests.find((item) => item.title)!.title!, state: sideQuests.find((item) => item.title)!.state }\n      : null,\n    log,\n    finale,\n    unseen,\n    presentationHistory,\n    ...(replayProjection && replayProjection.status !== \"unavailable\"\n      ? { latestChapterReleasePresentation: replayProjection.presentation }\n      : {}),\n  };\n}\n\r\n--- LEGACY PROGRESSION ---\r\nimport type { Prisma } from \"@prisma/client\";\nimport { canTransition, type ClientProgressEvent, type ChapterState, type ProgressEventType } from \"@/domain/story\";\nimport { db } from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { publishCampaignEvent } from \"@/lib/events\";\nimport { buildPublicSnapshot } from \"@/lib/snapshot\";\nimport { toClientEvent } from \"@/domain/visibility\";\n\ntype Action =\n  | \"PREPARE_CHAPTER\"\n  | \"RELEASE_CHAPTER\"\n  | \"MARK_SOLVED\"\n  | \"AWARD_ARTIFACT\"\n  | \"REVEAL_MAP\"\n  | \"REVEAL_ROUTE\"\n  | \"REVEAL_ARTIFACT_SILHOUETTE\"\n  | \"CONNECT_ARTIFACTS\"\n  | \"DISCOVER_SIDE_QUEST\"\n  | \"UPDATE_SIDE_QUEST\"\n  | \"COMPLETE_SIDE_QUEST\"\n  | \"ADD_JOURNAL_ANNOTATION\"\n  | \"ADD_LOG_ENTRY\"\n  | \"TEASE_FINALE\"\n  | \"UPDATE_FINALE_REQUIREMENT\"\n  | \"UNDO_LAST\"\n  | \"PAUSE\"\n  | \"RESUME\";\n\nexport class ProgressionConflict extends Error {\n  constructor(\n    message: string,\n    public code = \"COMMAND_CONFLICT\",\n  ) {\n    super(message);\n  }\n}\n\nasync function saveBefore(tx: Prisma.TransactionClient, campaignId: string, reason: string, sequence: number) {\n  const campaign = await tx.campaign.findUniqueOrThrow({\n    where: { id: campaignId },\n    include: {\n      chapters: true,\n      awards: true,\n      artifacts: true,\n      mapLocations: true,\n      mapRoutes: true,\n      sideQuests: { include: { objectives: true } },\n      journalEntries: true,\n    },\n  });\n  await tx.saveStateSnapshot.create({\n    data: {\n      campaignId,\n      sequence,\n      reason,\n      state: JSON.stringify({\n        status: campaign.status,\n        chapters: campaign.chapters.map(({ id, state, revealedAt, solvedAt }) => ({ id, state, revealedAt, solvedAt })),\n        awardIds: campaign.awards.map((a) => a.id),\n        locations: campaign.mapLocations.map(({ id, revealedAt }) => ({ id, revealedAt })),\n        routes: campaign.mapRoutes.map(({ id, state, revealedAt }) => ({ id, state, revealedAt })),\n        artifacts: campaign.artifacts.map(({ id, state, connectedArtifactKey }) => ({\n          id,\n          state,\n          connectedArtifactKey,\n        })),\n        quests: campaign.sideQuests.map(({ id, state, completedAt, objectives }) => ({\n          id,\n          state,\n          completedAt,\n          objectives: objectives.map(({ id, complete }) => ({ id, complete })),\n        })),\n        journalEntryIds: campaign.journalEntries.map(({ id }) => id),\n        finaleState: campaign.finaleState,\n        finaleTeaser: campaign.finaleTeaser,\n        finaleRequirements: campaign.finaleRequirements,\n      }),\n    },\n  });\n}\n\nasync function appendEvent(\n  tx: Prisma.TransactionClient,\n  campaignId: string,\n  sequence: number,\n  type: ProgressEventType,\n  payload: Record<string, unknown>,\n  actor: string,\n  reversesEventId?: string,\n) {\n  return tx.progressEvent.create({\n    data: {\n      campaignId,\n      type,\n      payload: JSON.stringify(payload),\n      actor,\n      sequence,\n      reversesEventId,\n    },\n  });\n}\n\nexport async function executeProgressionAction(\n  slug: string,\n  action: Action,\n  userId: string,\n  options: {\n    targetKey?: string;\n    value?: string;\n    correlationId?: string;\n    reason?: string;\n    expectedSequence?: number;\n  } = {},\n) {\n  const result = await db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\n    const campaign = await tx.campaign.findUniqueOrThrow({\n      where: { slug },\n      include: {\n        chapters: { orderBy: { ordinal: \"asc\" }, include: { content: true, clues: true } },\n        artifacts: true,\n        mapLocations: true,\n        mapRoutes: { orderBy: { ordinal: \"asc\" } },\n        sideQuests: { include: { objectives: { orderBy: { ordinal: \"asc\" } } } },\n      },\n    });\n    const chapter =\n      action === \"PREPARE_CHAPTER\"\n        ? (campaign.chapters.find((item) => [\"LOCKED\", \"TEASER\"].includes(item.state)) ?? campaign.chapters[0])\n        : action === \"RELEASE_CHAPTER\"\n          ? (campaign.chapters.find((item) => item.state === \"READY\") ?? campaign.chapters[0])\n          : action === \"MARK_SOLVED\"\n            ? (campaign.chapters.find((item) => item.state === \"ACTIVE\") ?? campaign.chapters[0])\n            : ([...campaign.chapters].reverse().find((item) => [\"ACTIVE\", \"SOLVED\", \"COMPLETE\"].includes(item.state)) ??\n              campaign.chapters[0]);\n    let type: ProgressEventType;\n    let payload: Record<string, unknown> = {};\n    let reversesEventId: string | undefined;\n\n    if (campaign.status === \"PAUSED\" && ![\"RESUME\", \"UNDO_LAST\"].includes(action))\n      throw new ProgressionConflict(\n        \"The campaign is paused. Resume it before releasing progression.\",\n        \"CAMPAIGN_PAUSED\",\n      );\n\n    const expectedSequence = options.expectedSequence ?? campaign.currentSequence;\n    if (campaign.currentSequence !== expectedSequence)\n      throw new ProgressionConflict(\n        `State changed from sequence ${expectedSequence} to ${campaign.currentSequence}. Refresh before confirming.`,\n        \"STALE_SEQUENCE\",\n      );\n    const reservation = await tx.campaign.updateMany({\n      where: { id: campaign.id, currentSequence: expectedSequence },\n      data: { currentSequence: { increment: 1 } },\n    });\n    if (reservation.count !== 1)\n      throw new ProgressionConflict(\n        `State changed from sequence ${expectedSequence}. Refresh before confirming.`,\n        \"STALE_SEQUENCE\",\n      );\n    const reservedSequence = expectedSequence + 1;\n\n    if (action === \"UNDO_LAST\") {\n      const saved = await tx.saveStateSnapshot.findFirst({\n        where: { campaignId: campaign.id },\n        orderBy: { createdAt: \"desc\" },\n      });\n      const last = await tx.progressEvent.findFirst({\n        where: { campaignId: campaign.id, type: { not: \"STATE_REVERTED\" } },\n        orderBy: { sequence: \"desc\" },\n      });\n      if (!saved || !last) throw new ProgressionConflict(\"There is no progression action to undo.\", \"NOT_FOUND\");\n      const state = JSON.parse(saved.state) as {\n        status: string;\n        chapters: Array<{ id: string; state: string; revealedAt: string | null; solvedAt: string | null }>;\n        awardIds: string[];\n        locations: Array<{ id: string; revealedAt: string | null }>;\n        routes: Array<{ id: string; state: string; revealedAt: string | null }>;\n        artifacts: Array<{ id: string; state: string; connectedArtifactKey: string | null }>;\n        quests: Array<{\n          id: string;\n          state: string;\n          completedAt: string | null;\n          objectives: Array<{ id: string; complete: boolean }>;\n        }>;\n        journalEntryIds: string[];\n        finaleState: string;\n        finaleTeaser: string | null;\n        finaleRequirements: string;\n      };\n      await tx.campaign.update({ where: { id: campaign.id }, data: { status: state.status } });\n      await tx.campaign.update({\n        where: { id: campaign.id },\n        data: {\n          finaleState: state.finaleState,\n          finaleTeaser: state.finaleTeaser,\n          finaleRequirements: state.finaleRequirements,\n        },\n      });\n      for (const item of state.chapters)\n        await tx.chapter.update({\n          where: { id: item.id },\n          data: { state: item.state, revealedAt: item.revealedAt, solvedAt: item.solvedAt },\n        });\n      await tx.artifactAward.deleteMany({\n        where: { campaignId: campaign.id, id: { notIn: state.awardIds.length ? state.awardIds : [\"__none__\"] } },\n      });\n      for (const item of state.locations)\n        await tx.mapLocation.update({ where: { id: item.id }, data: { revealedAt: item.revealedAt } });\n      for (const item of state.routes)\n        await tx.mapRoute.update({ where: { id: item.id }, data: { state: item.state, revealedAt: item.revealedAt } });\n      for (const item of state.artifacts)\n        await tx.artifact.update({\n          where: { id: item.id },\n          data: { state: item.state, connectedArtifactKey: item.connectedArtifactKey },\n        });\n      for (const item of state.quests) {\n        await tx.sideQuest.update({\n          where: { id: item.id },\n          data: { state: item.state, completedAt: item.completedAt },\n        });\n        for (const objective of item.objectives)\n          await tx.sideQuestObjective.update({ where: { id: objective.id }, data: { complete: objective.complete } });\n      }\n      await tx.journalEntry.deleteMany({\n        where: {\n          campaignId: campaign.id,\n          id: { notIn: state.journalEntryIds.length ? state.journalEntryIds : [\"__none__\"] },\n        },\n      });\n      await tx.saveStateSnapshot.delete({ where: { id: saved.id } });\n      type = \"STATE_REVERTED\";\n      payload = { reversedEventId: last.id, reversedType: last.type };\n      reversesEventId = last.id;\n    } else {\n      await saveBefore(tx, campaign.id, action, expectedSequence);\n      if (action === \"PREPARE_CHAPTER\") {\n        if (!canTransition(chapter.state as ChapterState, \"READY\"))\n          throw new ProgressionConflict(`Chapter cannot be prepared from ${chapter.state}.`, \"INVALID_TRANSITION\");\n        await tx.chapter.update({ where: { id: chapter.id }, data: { state: \"READY\" } });\n        type = \"CHAPTER_PREPARED\";\n        payload = { ordinal: chapter.ordinal };\n      } else if (action === \"RELEASE_CHAPTER\") {\n        if (chapter.state !== \"READY\")\n          throw new ProgressionConflict(\"Prepare the chapter before releasing it.\", \"PREREQUISITE_FAILED\");\n        await tx.chapter.update({ where: { id: chapter.id }, data: { state: \"ACTIVE\", revealedAt: new Date() } });\n        const location = campaign.mapLocations[0];\n        if (location) await tx.mapLocation.update({ where: { id: location.id }, data: { revealedAt: new Date() } });\n        type = \"CHAPTER_RELEASED\";\n        payload = {\n          ordinal: chapter.ordinal,\n          title: chapter.content.title,\n          narrative: chapter.content.narrative,\n          objective: chapter.content.objective,\n          riddle: chapter.clues[0]?.body,\n          ...(location\n            ? {\n                mapLocation: {\n                  key: location.key,\n                  name: location.name,\n                  regionLabel: location.regionLabel,\n                  x: location.x,\n                  y: location.y,\n                },\n              }\n            : {}),\n        };\n      } else if (action === \"MARK_SOLVED\") {\n        if (!canTransition(chapter.state as ChapterState, \"SOLVED\"))\n          throw new ProgressionConflict(\"Only an active chapter can be solved.\", \"INVALID_TRANSITION\");\n        await tx.chapter.update({ where: { id: chapter.id }, data: { state: \"SOLVED\", solvedAt: new Date() } });\n        type = \"CHAPTER_SOLVED\";\n        payload = { ordinal: chapter.ordinal };\n      } else if (action === \"AWARD_ARTIFACT\") {\n        const artifact = campaign.artifacts.find((item) => item.key === options.targetKey) ?? campaign.artifacts[0];\n        if (!artifact) throw new ProgressionConflict(\"No artifact is configured.\", \"NOT_FOUND\");\n        const existingAward = await tx.artifactAward.findUnique({\n          where: { campaignId_artifactId: { campaignId: campaign.id, artifactId: artifact.id } },\n        });\n        if (existingAward) throw new ProgressionConflict(\"This artifact has already been awarded.\", \"ALREADY_RELEASED\");\n        await tx.artifactAward.create({ data: { campaignId: campaign.id, artifactId: artifact.id } });\n        type = \"ARTIFACT_AWARDED\";\n        payload = { key: artifact.key, name: artifact.name, description: artifact.description };\n      } else if (action === \"REVEAL_MAP\") {\n        const location =\n          campaign.mapLocations.find((item) => item.key === options.targetKey) ??\n          campaign.mapLocations.find((item) => !item.revealedAt);\n        if (!location) throw new ProgressionConflict(\"No map location is configured.\", \"NOT_FOUND\");\n        if (location.revealedAt)\n          throw new ProgressionConflict(\"This map location has already been revealed.\", \"ALREADY_RELEASED\");\n        await tx.mapLocation.update({\n          where: { id: location.id },\n          data: { revealedAt: new Date(), state: \"REVEALED\" },\n        });\n        type = \"MAP_LOCATION_REVEALED\";\n        payload = {\n          key: location.key,\n          name: location.name,\n          regionLabel: location.regionLabel,\n          x: location.x,\n          y: location.y,\n        };\n      } else if (action === \"REVEAL_ROUTE\") {\n        const route =\n          campaign.mapRoutes.find((item) => item.key === options.targetKey) ??\n          campaign.mapRoutes.find((item) => item.state === \"HIDDEN\");\n        if (!route) throw new ProgressionConflict(\"No hidden route segment remains.\", \"NOT_FOUND\");\n        await tx.mapRoute.update({ where: { id: route.id }, data: { state: \"REVEALED\", revealedAt: new Date() } });\n        type = \"MAP_ROUTE_REVEALED\";\n        payload = { key: route.key, fromKey: route.fromKey, toKey: route.toKey };\n      } else if (action === \"REVEAL_ARTIFACT_SILHOUETTE\") {\n        const artifact =\n          campaign.artifacts.find((item) => item.key === options.targetKey) ??\n          campaign.artifacts.find((item) => item.state === \"UNKNOWN\");\n        if (!artifact) throw new ProgressionConflict(\"No unknown artifact remains.\", \"NOT_FOUND\");\n        await tx.artifact.update({ where: { id: artifact.id }, data: { state: \"SILHOUETTE\" } });\n        type = \"ARTIFACT_SILHOUETTE_REVEALED\";\n        payload = { key: artifact.key, safeName: artifact.safeName, silhouetteLabel: artifact.silhouetteLabel };\n      } else if (action === \"CONNECT_ARTIFACTS\") {\n        const artifact =\n          campaign.artifacts.find((item) => item.key === options.targetKey) ??\n          campaign.artifacts.find((item) => item.connectedArtifactKey && item.state !== \"CONNECTED\");\n        if (!artifact?.connectedArtifactKey)\n          throw new ProgressionConflict(\"No safe development artifact connection is configured.\", \"NOT_FOUND\");\n        await tx.artifact.updateMany({\n          where: { campaignId: campaign.id, key: { in: [artifact.key, artifact.connectedArtifactKey] } },\n          data: { state: \"CONNECTED\" },\n        });\n        type = \"ARTIFACT_CONNECTED\";\n        payload = { key: artifact.key, connectedArtifactKey: artifact.connectedArtifactKey };\n      } else if (action === \"DISCOVER_SIDE_QUEST\") {\n        const quest =\n          campaign.sideQuests.find((item) => item.key === options.targetKey) ??\n          campaign.sideQuests.find((item) => [\"HIDDEN\", \"RUMORED\"].includes(item.state));\n        if (!quest) throw new ProgressionConflict(\"No undiscovered side quest remains.\", \"NOT_FOUND\");\n        await tx.sideQuest.update({ where: { id: quest.id }, data: { state: \"DISCOVERED\" } });\n        type = \"SIDE_QUEST_DISCOVERED\";\n        payload = { key: quest.key, title: quest.title };\n      } else if (action === \"UPDATE_SIDE_QUEST\") {\n        const quest =\n          campaign.sideQuests.find((item) => item.key === options.targetKey) ??\n          campaign.sideQuests.find((item) => [\"DISCOVERED\", \"ACTIVE\", \"PARTIALLY_COMPLETE\"].includes(item.state));\n        if (!quest) throw new ProgressionConflict(\"No active side quest can be updated.\", \"NOT_FOUND\");\n        const objective = quest.objectives.find((item) => !item.complete);\n        if (objective) await tx.sideQuestObjective.update({ where: { id: objective.id }, data: { complete: true } });\n        await tx.sideQuest.update({ where: { id: quest.id }, data: { state: \"ACTIVE\" } });\n        type = \"SIDE_QUEST_UPDATED\";\n        payload = { key: quest.key, objectiveOrdinal: objective?.ordinal };\n      } else if (action === \"COMPLETE_SIDE_QUEST\") {\n        const quest =\n          campaign.sideQuests.find((item) => item.key === options.targetKey) ??\n          campaign.sideQuests.find((item) => [\"DISCOVERED\", \"ACTIVE\", \"PARTIALLY_COMPLETE\"].includes(item.state));\n        if (!quest) throw new ProgressionConflict(\"No active side quest can be completed.\", \"NOT_FOUND\");\n        await tx.sideQuestObjective.updateMany({ where: { sideQuestId: quest.id }, data: { complete: true } });\n        await tx.sideQuest.update({ where: { id: quest.id }, data: { state: \"COMPLETE\", completedAt: new Date() } });\n        type = \"SIDE_QUEST_COMPLETED\";\n        payload = { key: quest.key, title: quest.title, rewardLabel: quest.rewardLabel };\n      } else if (action === \"ADD_JOURNAL_ANNOTATION\") {\n        const entry = await tx.journalEntry.create({\n          data: {\n            campaignId: campaign.id,\n            title: \"Development margin note\",\n            body: options.value ?? \"A safe development observation was added beside the active clue.\",\n            kind: \"ANNOTATION\",\n            chapterOrdinal: chapter.ordinal,\n            releasedAt: new Date(),\n          },\n        });\n        type = \"JOURNAL_ANNOTATION_ADDED\";\n        payload = { key: entry.id, title: entry.title, chapterOrdinal: chapter.ordinal };\n      } else if (action === \"ADD_LOG_ENTRY\") {\n        type = \"PLAYER_LOG_ENTRY_ADDED\";\n        payload = { key: `log-${campaign.currentSequence + 1}`, title: options.value ?? \"Development captainâ€™s note\" };\n      } else if (action === \"TEASE_FINALE\") {\n        await tx.campaign.update({\n          where: { id: campaign.id },\n          data: {\n            finaleState: \"TEASED\",\n            finaleTeaser: \"A dormant arrangement of brass rings answers from behind the final seal.\",\n          },\n        });\n        type = \"FINALE_TEASED\";\n        payload = { state: \"TEASED\" };\n      } else if (action === \"UPDATE_FINALE_REQUIREMENT\") {\n        const requirements = JSON.parse(campaign.finaleRequirements) as Array<{\n          key: string;\n          label: string;\n          current: number;\n          target: number;\n          optional?: boolean;\n        }>;\n        const target = requirements.find((item) => item.key === options.targetKey) ?? requirements[0];\n        if (!target) throw new ProgressionConflict(\"No safe finale requirement is configured.\", \"NOT_FOUND\");\n        target.current = Math.min(target.target, target.current + 1);\n        await tx.campaign.update({\n          where: { id: campaign.id },\n          data: { finaleState: \"REQUIREMENTS_PARTIAL\", finaleRequirements: JSON.stringify(requirements) },\n        });\n        type = \"FINALE_REQUIREMENT_UPDATED\";\n        payload = { key: target.key };\n      } else if (action === \"PAUSE\") {\n        if (campaign.status === \"PAUSED\")\n          throw new ProgressionConflict(\"Campaign is already paused.\", \"INVALID_TRANSITION\");\n        await tx.campaign.update({ where: { id: campaign.id }, data: { status: \"PAUSED\" } });\n        type = \"CAMPAIGN_PAUSED\";\n      } else {\n        if (campaign.status === \"ACTIVE\")\n          throw new ProgressionConflict(\"Campaign is already underway.\", \"INVALID_TRANSITION\");\n        await tx.campaign.update({ where: { id: campaign.id }, data: { status: \"ACTIVE\" } });\n        type = \"CAMPAIGN_RESUMED\";\n      }\n    }\n    const event = await appendEvent(tx, campaign.id, reservedSequence, type, payload, userId, reversesEventId);\n    await tx.campaignSnapshot.create({\n      data: { campaignId: campaign.id, sequence: event.sequence, state: JSON.stringify({ eventType: type, payload }) },\n    });\n    await tx.adminAuditLog.create({\n      data: {\n        campaignId: campaign.id,\n        userId,\n        action,\n        correlationId: options.correlationId,\n        reason: options.reason,\n        metadata: JSON.stringify({\n          eventId: event.id,\n          sequence: event.sequence,\n          reservedSequence,\n          targetKey: options.targetKey,\n        }),\n      },\n    });\n    return { campaignId: campaign.id, event };\n  });\n  const clientEvent: ClientProgressEvent = toClientEvent(result.event);\n  publishCampaignEvent(result.campaignId, clientEvent);\n  return { event: clientEvent, snapshot: await buildPublicSnapshot(result.campaignId) };\n}\n\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

## Assistant
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-21T15:17:41.620Z

The investigation found the actual split: the legacy `Campaign` stack still owns Player, Quartermaster, snapshot, event, and audit writes, while the newer platform owns Studio, published editions, sessions, identity, invitations, and its own event stream. I’m freezing that evidence into the required inventory and ADR before changing runtime code; this is the line that prevents a cosmetic migration.

`tests/*`, `Development_Docs/*` | shared | developer workflow and CI | update/deprecate as cutover lands |\n+\n+## Cutover conclusion\n+\n+At the baseline, two independent authoritative writers exist. The legacy writer updates Campaign-derived tables and its own event/snapshot/audit records; the newer writer updates Chronicle Session records and its own events/audit records. Project One Voyage must route the legacy APIs through one canonical command, projection, policy, and audit path before any legacy tables are made read-only. The term **Chronicle** replaces the historical product/domain term “Tall Tale” in live code, active documentation, types, and paths; only forward database compatibility SQL may name a historical table when required to preserve installed data.\n*** Add File: Forever-Treasure-Integration/Development_Docs/Project_One_Voyage_Canonical_Domain_ADR.md\n+# ADR: Project One Voyage canonical Chronicle domain\n+\n+- Status: accepted for implementation\n+- Date: 2026-07-21\n+- Decision: Chronicle is the only production writer of authored and runtime state after Stage E.\n+\n+## Decision\n+\n+`Chronicle` is the authored aggregate root. A Chronicle has drafts, chapters, blocks, connections, assets, locations, and artifacts. `PublishedTaleVersion` remains the existing physical/version model name during the compatibility release, but represents an immutable **Published Chronicle Version**. It is checksummed, schema-versioned, and never changes after publication.\n+\n+`TaleSession` remains the live-playthrough aggregate during this compatibility release and represents a **Chronicle Session**. It pins exactly one published version, owns current story position, lifecycle state, monotonic sequence, variables, inventory, reveal state, memberships, invitations, and `TaleSessionEvent` history. Runtime state is mutable only through the canonical command service. A command is idempotent by key and produces one canonical event sequence and one correlated `PlatformAuditEvent`.\n+\n+The model/table naming compatibility is deliberate and temporary: live application code exposes Chronicle terminology; forward migration preserves installed data and can refer to pre-cutover table identifiers only inside migration SQL. No new feature may introduce a `TallTale`, `tall-tale`, or parallel Campaign writer.\n+\n+## Ownership boundaries\n+\n+| Boundary | Canonical owner | Rule |\n+| --- | --- | --- |\n+| Authored content | Chronicle, draft graph, assets, locations, artifacts | Creator-authorized, mutable only in a draft |\n+| Published content | Published Chronicle Version | immutable snapshot, checksum, schema version |\n+| Live playthrough | Chronicle Session | one pinned version, mutable only through canonical commands |\n+| Runtime history | Chronicle Session Event | ordered, monotonic, idempotent command correlation |\n+| Player identity | PlayerProfile and PlayerIdentitySession | rotating canonical credential, never a Campaign token as authority |\n+| Player access | PlaythroughMembership and Invitation | membership is the resource scope; invitation is an acquisition credential |\n+| Captain / Creator access | PlatformRoleAssignment plus existing staff account capability | explicit role and resource policy checks |\n+| Artifacts, side quests, map, routes, variables | Chronicle authored definitions plus session variables/inventory/RevealState | definitions are versioned; earned/revealed state is session-local |\n+| Presentation acknowledgement | canonical session event and RevealState/reading-state projection | acknowledgement cannot advance story |\n+| Projection / replay | canonical projector from pinned version and events/reveals | no CampaignSnapshot read after canonical-read cutover |\n+| Audit | PlatformAuditEvent | one sanitized record correlated with canonical event/command |\n+| Compatibility | `src/compatibility/legacy-companion` | translations only; no independent decisions or state writes |\n+\n+## State model\n+\n+Published content is immutable. Drafts are mutable and never replace a version already bound to a session. A session has `DRAFT_SETUP -> INVITING -> READY/SCHEDULED -> ACTIVE <-> PAUSED -> COMPLETED`, with cancellation before launch and abandonment after launch. Current position is the Session’s current block and current sequence, derived from the last accepted event and persisted projection. Player-visible content is the intersection of the pinned version, membership scope, RevealState, and Player-safe projection policy.\n+\n+Events are the authoritative history for progression, commands, presentation, and recovery. `variables`, `inventory`, `RevealState`, and current pointers are materialized projections updated atomically with the event. Cached projections may be rebuilt; they are never a second authority. Side-quest completion, artifact ownership, chapter completion, and map/route visibility derive from canonical events and projections.\n+\n+## Legacy migration and compatibility\n+\n+`LegacyEntityReference` is the deterministic mapping authority: source domain, model, source ID, canonical model/ID, migration version, source checksum, and timestamps are unique. The runner can resume, reject changed source checksums, resolve legacy URLs, and prove a rerun did not duplicate records. Legacy public slugs are retained by the Chronicle. A valid legacy access code exchanges for a canonical scoped identity/membership; it cannot grant global identity or broaden a role.\n+\n+Compatibility adapters may translate request/response shape, resolve source IDs, redirect URLs, and exchange old credentials. They call canonical policy, command, projection, and audit services. They may never independently mutate Campaign, chapter, artifact, snapshot, access, command, or audit tables after canonical writes are enabled.\n+\n+## Rollout and rollback\n+\n+Stages are: A inventory/ADR; B migration tooling; C shadow comparison with legacy authoritative; D canonical reads for migrated sessions; E canonical-only writes; F compatibility-only legacy routes and read-only legacy tables; G retirement after removal gates. Feature configuration is typed and stage-specific, defaults to legacy reads until a verified migration has been explicitly enabled, and has a documented rollback switch.\n+\n+Before any canonical-only write, rollback is application routing back to the last authoritative legacy projection after a verified backup. Once a canonical-only event exists, simple pointer rollback is forbidden: use a maintenance restore or reverse projection/reconciliation. Production rollback is not claimed until it is rehearsed against a database backup.\n+\n+## Deletion gate\n+\n+Legacy code may be deleted only after: all source models/fields are mapped; a migration and rerun pass; shadow comparison has zero unexplained semantic mismatches; all legacy public links and Quartermaster workflows use adapters; production reachability is zero except named adapters; legacy writers are blocked by tests; the compatibility release has shipped for one full release; and backup/rollback evidence is retained.\n*** Add File: Forever-Treasure-Integration/Development_Docs/Project_One_Voyage_Rollback_Runbook.md\n+# Project One Voyage rollback runbook\n+\n+## Preconditions\n+\n+1. Record application commit, schema migration version, `LegacyEntityReference` checksum report, feature stage, database backup identity/hash, and asset backup identity before enabling canonical reads or writes.\n+2. Take a consistent database and asset-volume backup. Verify a restore to an isolated database; do not test against production.\n+3. Run migration `--verify`, shadow comparison, authorization matrix, and restart recovery against the exact release candidate.\n+\n+## Rollback before canonical-only writes\n+\n+1. Put the application into maintenance/read-only mode.\n+2. Disable the typed canonical-read stage for the affected migrated sessions and restore legacy route resolution.\n+3. Re-enable legacy reads only after verifying the Campaign tables were not changed by adapters.\n+4. Preserve the canonical mapping and mismatch reports for investigation; do not delete them.\n+5. Validate Player, Captain, invitation, public-slug, artifact, map, session, and audit projections against the recorded pre-cutover baseline.\n+\n+## Boundary after canonical-only writes\n+\n+After a canonical-only command has appended a Chronicle Session Event, directing traffic back to stale Campaign tables is unsafe and prohibited. Select one of these reviewed recovery paths:\n+\n+- restore the matched database and asset backup during a maintenance window; or\n+- run an approved reverse projection/reconciliation tool, then compare all semantic state and audit correlation.\n+\n+Do not claim a simple rollback is available until one path has been rehearsed on an isolated copy and its evidence is linked to the release.\n+\n+## Verification after recovery\n+\n+- Confirm the database identity nonce through `/api/dev/validation/database-identity` in the isolated environment.\n+- Run migration verification in read-only mode and confirm no duplicate mappings.\n+- Compare current position, sequence, artifacts, side quests, map/routes, presentation acknowledgements, memberships, invitations, session lifecycle, and audit correlation.\n+- Confirm legacy credentials remain scoped and revoked memberships remain revoked.\n+- Record the final route stage, application commit, restoration source, and any irreversible canonical events.\n*** Add File: Forever-Treasure-Integration/Development_Docs/legacy-retirement-manifest.json\n+{\n+  \"$schema\": \"./legacy-retirement-manifest.schema.json\",\n+  \"project\": \"Project One Voyage\",\n+  \"canonicalDomain\": \"Chronicle\",\n+  \"cutoverStage\": \"B-migration-tooling\",\n+  \"components\": [\n+    {\n+      \"component\": \"Campaign\",\n+      \"status\": \"migration-source\",\n+      \"canonicalReplacement\": \"Chronicle plus Published Chronicle Version\",\n+      \"productionCallersRemaining\": \"baseline-inventory-required\",\n+      \"canWrite\": true,\n+      \"removalGate\": \"zero shadow mismatches, canonical reads and writes, compatibility release plus one\"\n+    },\n+    {\n+      \"component\": \"CampaignSnapshotBuilder\",\n+      \"status\": \"replace-with-adapter\",\n+      \"canonicalReplacement\": \"ChronicleSessionProjectionService\",\n+      \"productionCallersRemaining\": \"baseline-inventory-required\",\n+      \"canWrite\": false,\n+      \"removalGate\": \"bookmark, command, and authorization compatibility release plus one\"\n+    }\n+  ]\n+}\n*** End Patch";

body; kind; eventId; chapterOrdinal; releasedAt\",Player journal/presentation,Chronicle Session Event,\"journalEntry payload; sequence\",Append sanitized event tied to source mapping,\"unreleased omitted\",Null event ID uses source ID idempotency,Medium,\"released journal projection equals\",journal/replay parity,\"rebuild journal projection\"\n+MapLocation,\"key; TARGET EXPERIENCE\\r\\n\\r\\nThe site should eventually be hosted at a subdomain such as:\\r\\n\\r\\n`forevertreasure.absoluterelativesystems.com`\\r\\n\\r\\nThe architecture must be compatible with:\\r\\n\\r\\n* Debian Linux\\r\\n* NGINX reverse proxy\\r\\n* HTTPS\\r\\n* Node.js runtime\\r\\n* MySQL\\r\\n* Environment-based configuration\\r\\n* A future production deployment using systemd or a container\\r\\n\\r\\nDo not deploy to production during this task.\\r\\n\\r\\nCreate production-ready deployment documentation, but keep actual deployment separate.\\r\\n\\r\\n---\\r\\n\\r\\n# TECHNOLOGY FOUNDATION\\r\\n\\r\\nUse a maintainable modern stack suitable for self-hosting.\\r\\n\\r\\nPreferred foundation:\\r\\n\\r\\n* Next.js using the App Router\\r\\n* TypeScript with strict major dependency and why it is used.\\r\\n\\r\\nAvoid unnecessary dependency bloat.\\r\\n\\r\\n---\\r\\n\\r\\n# RESPONSIVE EXPERIENCE\\r\\n\\r\\nThe application must work beautifully on:\\r\\n\\r\\n* Desktop computers\\r\\n* Large secondary monitors\\r\\n* 1440p displays\\r\\n* Laptop displays\\r\\n* iPhones\\r\\n* Mobile Safari\\r\\n* Narrow portrait orientation\\r\\n* Landscape phone space, not fragile browser-name assumptions.\\r\\n\\r\\n---\\r\\n\\r\\n# DESKTOP EXPERIENCE\\r\\n\\r\\nOn a large desktop display, the Player Experience should resemble an open physical story workspace.\\r\\n\\r\\nThe first vertical slice should contain:\\r\\n\\r\\n* A central open journal or parchment spread\\r\\n* A visible current-chapter area\\r\\n* A voyage-chart panel or map region\\r\\n* A treasure or artifact display region\\r\\n* Ambient background depth\\r\\n* Clearly readable text\\r\\n* Intentional negative space\\r\\n* No excessive settings-dashboard appearance\\r\\n\\r\\nThe desktop layout should make productive use of 1920Ã—1080 and 2560Ã—1440 displays.\\r\\n\\r\\nAvoid simply stretching a mobile column across the screen.\\r\\n\\r\\nThe page should feel appropriate to leave open on Seraâ€™s second monitor while playing.\\r\\n\\r\\nCreate a fullscreen-friendly Companion Mode.\\r\\n\\r\\nThe fullscreen-friendly experience should minimize browser-like clutter within the application itself and place the current objective where it can be understood at a glance.\\r\\n\\r\\n---\\r\\n\\r\\n# IPHONE EXPERIENCE\\r\\n\\r\\nThe iPhone experience must be intentionally redesigned rather than merely squeezed.\\r\\n\\r\\nUse:\\r\\n\\r\\n* A single-column story flow\\r\\n* Large touch targets\\r\\n* Safe-area handling around the notch and home indicator\\r\\n* A compact navigation system\\r\\n* Readable clue text without constant zooming\\r\\n* Smooth sheet-like transitions\\r\\n* A persistent but unobtrusive current-objective control\\r\\n* A clear way to return to the active clue\\r\\n* Audio controls that remain accessible\\r\\n* No hover-dependent interaction\\r\\n\\r\\nThe journal can become vertically paginated or appear as layered parchment sheets.\\r\\n\\r\\nThe voyage chart and artifact collection can become separate mobile views accessible through a compact bottom navigation system.\\r\\n\\r\\nDo not force all desktop panels onto one narrow screen.\\r\\n\\r\\n---\\r\\n\\r\\n# VISUAL DESIGN DIRECTION\\r\\n\\r\\nCreate an original visual design system before building arbitrary page components.\\r\\n\\r\\nThe palette should initially center around:\\r\\n\\r\\n* Deep ocean green\\r\\n* Near-black navy\\r\\n* Warm charcoal\\r\\n* Weathered parchment\\r\\n* Antique brass\\r\\n* Muted crimson\\r\\n* Moonlit blue-green magical highlights\\r\\n\\r\\nThe romantic component must be subtle at first.\\r\\n\\r\\nDo not make the opening experience resemble a Valentineâ€™s Day card.\\r\\n\\r\\nRomantic symbolism should emerge through:\\r\\n\\r\\n* Red thread\\r\\n* Paired stars\\r\\n* Constellation geometry\\r\\n* Heart-like arrangements that are not initially obvious\\r\\n* Moon imagery\\r\\n* Compass motifs\\r\\n* Small crimson accents\\r\\n* Increasing warmth as progression occurs\\r\\n\\r\\nCreate documented design tokens for:\\r\\n\\r\\n* Colors\\r\\n* Typography\\r\\n* Spacing\\r\\n* Shadows\\r\\n* Border treatments\\r\\n* Parchment surfaces\\r\\n* Brass accents\\r\\n* Magical glow\\r\\n* Animation timing\\r\\n* Sound levels\\r\\n* Responsive breakpoints\\r\\n* Focus states\\r\\n* Layering and z-index rules\\r\\n\\r\\nTypography must remain readable.\\r\\n\\r\\nUse decorative typography sparingly for headings, chapter numbers, seals, and short phrases.\\r\\n\\r\\nUse a highly readable body typeface for riddles, objectives, and journal text.\\r\\n\\r\\n---\\r\\n\\r\\n# TEXTURE AND DEPTH\\r\\n\\r\\nThe page should not look like flat colored rectangles.\\r\\n\\r\\nCreate subtle layered depth through techniques such as:\\r\\n\\r\\n* Procedural CSS paper texture\\r\\n* Soft grain\\r\\n* Vignette\\r\\n* Edge wear\\r\\n* Fold lines\\r\\n* Ink variation\\r\\n* Brass highlights\\r\\n* Gentle shadowing\\r\\n* Candlelight illumination\\r\\n* Layered map material\\r\\n* Slightly raised artifact frames\\r\\n\\r\\nAvoid overly noisy textures that reduce readability.\\r\\n\\r\\nAvoid excessive blur effects.\\r\\n\\r\\nAvoid making every component glow.\\r\\n\\r\\nThe magic should feel rare and meaningful.\\r\\n\\r\\n---\\r\\n\\r\\n# CORE STORY STATE MODEL\\r\\n\\r\\nCreate a real story progression model.\\r\\n\\r\\nAt minimum, support these chapter states:\\r\\n\\r\\n* LOCKED\\r\\n* TEASER\\r\\n* READY\\r\\n* REVEALING\\r\\n* ACTIVE\\r\\n* SOLVED\\r\\n* COMPLETE\\r\\n\\r\\nDo not model progression as only a single chapter number.\\r\\n\\r\\nCreate an event-driven state system capable of supporting future events such as:\\r\\n\\r\\n* CAMPAIGN_STARTED\\r\\n* CHAPTER_PREPARED\\r\\n* CHAPTER_RELEASED\\r\\n* CHAPTER_REVEAL_STARTED\\r\\n* CHAPTER_REVEAL_COMPLETED\\r\\n* CHAPTER_SOLVED\\r\\n* HINT_RELEASED\\r\\n* ARTIFACT_AWARDED\\r\\n* SIDE_QUEST_DISCOVERED\\r\\n* SIDE_QUEST_UPDATED\\r\\n* JOURNAL_ENTRY_ADDED\\r\\n* MAP_LOCATION_REVEALED\\r\\n* GM_MESSAGE_RELEASED\\r\\n* FINALE_UNLOCKED\\r\\n* STATE_REVERTED\\r\\n\\r\\nEvents must have:\\r\\n\\r\\n* Unique identifiers\\r\\n* Campaign identifier\\r\\n* Event type\\r\\n* Payload\\r\\n* Creation time\\r\\n* Release time\\r\\n* Actor\\r\\n* Sequence number\\r\\n* Version information where appropriate\\r\\n* Reversal or supersession metadata where appropriate\\r\\n\\r\\nThe system must be resilient to:\\r\\n\\r\\n* Refreshing the page\\r\\n* Reconnecting after internet loss\\r\\n* Opening the site on another device\\r\\n* Duplicate event delivery\\r\\n* Events arriving while the tab is in the background\\r\\n* Two events being triggered close together\\r\\n* The Game Master undoing an accidental action\\r\\n\\r\\nUse idempotent processing.\\r\\n\\r\\nThe player should never receive the same theatrical sequence twice because of a network reconnect unless the Game Master intentionally chooses Replay.\\r\\n\\r\\n---\\r\\n\\r\\n# DATABASE FOUNDATION\\r\\n\\r\\nDesign a normalized schema that can grow to support the full experience.\\r\\n\\r\\nAt minimum, plan for entities such as:\\r\\n\\r\\n* Campaign\\r\\n* Player access\\r\\n* Game Master user\\r\\n* Chapter\\r\\n* Chapter content\\r\\n* Clue\\r\\n* Hint\\r\\n* Artifact\\r\\n* Artifact award\\r\\n* Side quest\\r\\n* Side-quest objective\\r\\n* Journal entry\\r\\n* Map location\\r\\n* Progress event\\r\\n* Current campaign snapshot\\r\\n* Admin audit log\\r\\n* Audio preference where appropriate\\r\\n* Content version\\r\\n* Backup or save-state snapshot\\r\\n\\r\\nUse appropriate foreign keys, uniqueness constraints, indexes, timestamps, and enums.\\r\\n\\r\\nUse migrations.\\r\\n\\r\\nProvide a seed campaign containing only development content for the first vertical slice.\\r\\n\\r\\nDevelopment seed content must be clearly separated from final Tall Tale content.\\r\\n\\r\\nDo not hardcode the final story into React components.\\r\\n\\r\\nStory text, chapter configuration, sequence configuration, artifacts, hints, and map markers must be data-driven.\\r\\n\\r\\n---\\r\\n\\r\\n# PLAYER ACCESS\\r\\n\\r\\nThe Player Experience should not require Sera to create an account.\\r\\n\\r\\nDesign support for a private campaign URL, such as:\\r\\n\\r\\n`/tale/[campaignSlug]`\\r\\n\\r\\nProtect the campaign with at least one of these mechanisms:\\r\\n\\r\\n* A long unguessable campaign access token\\r\\n* A simple access code\\r\\n* A private signed invitation link\\r\\n\\r\\nThe final method should be easy for Sera to use while preventing casual discovery.\\r\\n\\r\\nDo not place Game Master controls in the same client bundle or interface in a way that makes them accessible through ordinary player navigation.\\r\\n\\r\\n---\\r\\n\\r\\n# GAME MASTER AUTHENTICATION\\r\\n\\r\\nThe Game Master Dashboard must require real authentication.\\r\\n\\r\\nFor the initial implementation:\\r\\n\\r\\n* Use a secure password hash\\r\\n* Use server-side session handling\\r\\n* Use secure, HTTP-only cookies\\r\\n* Use CSRF protection where relevant\\r\\n* Use environment variables for secrets\\r\\n* Rate-limit login attempts\\r\\n* Record important administrative actions in an audit log\\r\\n* Never expose passwords or secret hashes to the client\\r\\n\\r\\nDo not use route obscurity as authentication.\\r\\n\\r\\nA hidden `/quartermaster` route can be used for theme, but it must still be secured.\\r\\n\\r\\n---\\r\\n\\r\\n# GAME MASTER DASHBOARD VERTICAL SLICE\\r\\n\\r\\nCreate a private Game Master dashboard for the seeded development campaign.\\r\\n\\r\\nThe dashboard should display:\\r\\n\\r\\n* Campaign status\\r\\n* Current active chapter\\r\\n* Player connection status if reasonably available\\r\\n* The next prepared chapter\\r\\n* Recent progression events\\r\\n* Current artifact inventory\\r\\n* Current side-quest state\\r\\n* A preview of what the player currently sees\\r\\n\\r\\nFor the first vertical slice, implement these controls:\\r\\n\\r\\n* Prepare Chapter\\r\\n* Release Chapter\\r\\n* Preview Reveal\\r\\n* Replay Reveal Locally\\r\\n* Mark Chapter Solved\\r\\n* Award Test Artifact\\r\\n* Reveal Test Map Location\\r\\n* Undo Last Progression Action\\r\\n* Pause Campaign\\r\\n* Resume Campaign\\r\\n\\r\\nDangerous controls must not fire from one careless click.\\r\\n\\r\\nUse a deliberate interaction such as:\\r\\n\\r\\n1. Select the action\\r\\n2. Show exactly what will happen\\r\\n3. Require confirmation\\r\\n4. Execute atomically\\r\\n5. Show the resulting event ID and state change\\r\\n6. Offer an appropriate undo action\\r\\n\\r\\nThe dashboard itself should be atmospheric but operationally clear.\\r\\n\\r\\nDo not sacrifice usability for decoration.\\r\\n\\r\\n---\\r\\n\\r\\n# LIVE UPDATE ARCHITECTURE\\r\\n\\r\\nUse Server-Sent Events unless a documented technical limitation makes another option clearly superior.\\r\\n\\r\\nImplement:\\r\\n\\r\\n* A player event stream endpoint\\r\\n* Authenticated or token-protected campaign subscription\\r\\n* Automatic reconnection\\r\\n* Event IDs\\r\\n* Last-Event-ID support\\r\\n* Missed-event recovery\\r\\n* Heartbeat messages\\r\\n* Connection-state indicators\\r\\n* Snapshot reconciliation after reconnect\\r\\n\\r\\nThe player client should:\\r\\n\\r\\n1. Load a complete current campaign snapshot\\r\\n2. Connect to the event stream\\r\\n3. Receive newly released events\\r\\n4. Queue theatrical sequences\\r\\n5. Process them in order\\r\\n6. Acknowledge local completion where appropriate\\r\\n7. Reconcile with the server if state diverges\\r\\n\\r\\nDo not rely on a client-only timer as the source of truth.\\r\\n\\r\\n---\\r\\n\\r\\n# THEATRICAL EVENT ORCHESTRATOR\\r\\n\\r\\nBuild a reusable client-side cinematic event orchestrator.\\r\\n\\r\\nIt must not be a collection of unrelated `setTimeout` calls scattered across components.\\r\\n\\r\\nCreate a centralized event queue or state machine that can:\\r\\n\\r\\n* Accept progression events\\r\\n* Determine the corresponding sequence\\r\\n* Prevent overlapping ceremonies\\r\\n* Pause while another reveal is active\\r\\n* Resume safely\\r\\n* Skip or accelerate when requested\\r\\n* Respect reduced-motion settings\\r\\n* Handle audio being unavailable\\r\\n* Complete state application even if animation fails\\r\\n* Recover after a refresh\\r\\n* Distinguish unseen events from already-viewed events\\r\\n\\r\\nThe reveal sequence should be configurable through structured timing values.\\r\\n\\r\\nDocument the choreography and timing tokens.\\r\\n\\r\\n---\\r\\n\\r\\n# FIRST CHAPTER RELEASE SEQUENCE\\r\\n\\r\\nImplement the following complete sequence for the seeded first chapter.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Initial state\\r\\n\\r\\nBefore release:\\r\\n\\r\\n* The journal is present but the active chapter is sealed.\\r\\n* The main clue text is unreadable or hidden beneath a sealed parchment treatment.\\r\\n* The map location is obscured.\\r\\n* The artifact frame is empty.\\r\\n* The page has subtle ambient motion.\\r\\n* No large distracting animation loops continuously.\\r\\n* The player can see that the tale is awaiting the captainâ€™s signal.\\r\\n\\r\\nThe page must not expose the unreleased clue through:\\r\\n\\r\\n* HTML source\\r\\n* Client state\\r\\n* Accessibility text\\r\\n* Network payload\\r\\n* Prefetched API data\\r\\n\\r\\nUnreleased content must remain server-side.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Trigger\\r\\n\\r\\nKato presses Release Chapter in the Game Master dashboard.\\r\\n\\r\\nThe server atomically:\\r\\n\\r\\n1. Changes the chapter state\\r\\n2. Creates the progression event\\r\\n3. Assigns the event sequence\\r\\n4. Records the audit action\\r\\n5. Updates the campaign snapshot\\r\\n6. Publishes the event to the player stream\\r\\n\\r\\n## Player ceremony\\r\\n\\r\\nWhen the player receives the chapter-release event, perform this choreography:\\r\\n\\r\\n### Stage 1: Omen\\r\\n\\r\\n* Ambient light dims around the journal.\\r\\n* The wider page becomes darker without making the text inaccessible.\\r\\n* Candlelight flickers irregularly rather than as a repetitive sine wave.\\r\\n* A low ship-timber sound or distant thunder sound may play if audio has been enabled.\\r\\n* Duration should feel deliberate but not painfully slow.\\r\\n\\r\\n### Stage 2: Attention\\r\\n\\r\\n* Peripheral panels subtly recede.\\r\\n* The sealed journal page becomes the visual focus.\\r\\n* A soft brass or moonlit highlight traces the edge of the relevant parchment.\\r\\n* The camera effect must remain subtle.\\r\\n* Do not create motion sickness with aggressive zooming.\\r\\n\\r\\n### Stage 3: Wax Seal\\r\\n\\r\\n* The wax seal reacts as if pressure or magic is building.\\r\\n* Fine cracks develop.\\r\\n* The seal breaks with a synchronized sound when audio is enabled.\\r\\n* Small fragments or particles should be restrained and performant.\\r\\n* The seal should not simply disappear with opacity.\\r\\n\\r\\n### Stage 4: Parchment Opening\\r\\n\\r\\n* The sealed fold or covering releases.\\r\\n* The parchment shifts with believable layered motion.\\r\\n* Shadows and edge lighting update as the page opens.\\r\\n* The effect must work without WebGL if practical.\\r\\n* Provide a reduced-motion equivalent.\\r\\n\\r\\n### Stage 5: Ink Revelation\\r\\n\\r\\n* The chapter heading appears first.\\r\\n* Narrative text follows.\\r\\n* The objective appears next.\\r\\n* The riddle or clue is written or revealed last.\\r\\n* Ink should appear organically by line, mask, or controlled reveal.\\r\\n* Do not animate each individual character with hundreds of DOM elements.\\r\\n* Maintain screen-reader accessibility.\\r\\n* Allow the user to reveal all text immediately.\\r\\n\\r\\n### Stage 6: Map Discovery\\r\\n\\r\\n* After the clue becomes readable, the voyage chart responds.\\r\\n* Fog or darkness withdraws from one map region.\\r\\n* A hand-drawn rou…252155 tokens truncated…ncies remain deliberately bounded: Prisma for persistence and transactions, bcryptjs for portable hashes, Zod for payload validation, GSAP for cinematic orchestration, Motion for React interaction/presence, StPageFlip for the journal surface, Rive/Lottie for isolated vector assets, Pino for structured redacted logs, and Vitest/Playwright for validation. See `docs/animation/architecture.md` and `docs/animation/library-ownership.md`.\\n\\nPhase 3 adds an administrative intent layer. `/api/gm/preview` projects without writes, `/api/gm/staging` persists unreleased intent, and `/api/gm/commands` enforces expected sequence and idempotency. `PlayerPresence` supplies expiring evidence. See [admin command pipeline](admin-command-pipeline.md) and [player presence](player-presence-and-sync.md).\\n\\n## Tall Tale Studio Phase 1\\n\\nStudio is additive to the original campaign companion. `TallTale` owns identity and catalog metadata; `TaleDraft` owns optimistic autosave state; chapters, blocks, and connections form the editable graph. Publishing validates that graph and writes a complete, checksummed `PublishedTaleVersion.contentSnapshot`. Published rows and referenced asset variants are immutable from the player runtime's perspective.\\n\\n`src/tall-tale/progression.ts` is the authoritative session engine. Each non-preview `TaleSession` is pinned to one published version and advances through idempotent, ordered events. The player receives its current projection and SSE updates; Captain actions call the same engine. Verification providers all enter through one versioned submission contract, so the simulator and a future paired helper cannot bypass progression rules. See `docs/tall-tale-studio.md`.\\n\\r\\n# Shared development documentation\\n\\n`Development_Docs` is the canonical repository location for shared development references, requirements, architecture notes, design decisions, implementation plans, testing notes, meeting notes, and other planning material that should remain available across Codex sessions and computers.\\n\\nFiles in this directory are normal Git-tracked project files. The repository's end-of-task synchronization workflow inspects additions, modifications, renames, moves, and deletions here and, after safety checks, can commit and push them through the same approved branch workflow used for `Codex_Chats`. The files are not copied into the conversation archive or packaged into a second store.\\n\\n## Working from another computer\\n\\nBefore relying on or editing these documents:\\n\\n1. Confirm that this is the intended repository and branch with `git remote -v`, `git branch --show-current`, and `git status --short --branch`.\\n2. Preserve and review any local modifications. Do not reset, clean, restore, or force-push them away.\\n3. Run `git fetch --prune origin`, then compare the branch with its upstream using `git rev-list --left-right --count \\\"HEAD...@{upstream}\\\"`.\\n4. If the branch is only behind and the working tree is clean, update it with `git pull --ff-only`. If it is ahead, diverged, or locally modified, reconcile the work deliberately before editing these documents.\\n5. If Git reports a conflict, preserve both versions and resolve it visibly. Do not push unresolved conflict markers. For an unmergeable binary document, retain clearly named local and remote variants for review when repository conventions permit.\\n\\nThis pull-before-edit check matters: a Codex session on a different computer must synchronize the latest repository state before treating local `Development_Docs` content as current.\\n\\n## Safety and change behavior\\n\\nDo not store secrets, credentials, private keys, access or API tokens, production database exports, private authentication caches, or sensitive company information here unless both the repository and the specific contents have been explicitly approved for that purpose. This repository may be publicly visible. Temporary, recovery, credential-shaped, and obvious secret files are ignored, and the synchronizer also checks changed file names, bounded textual content, and file sizes before staging.\\n\\nDeleting a tracked file locally and committing that deletion also removes it from the GitHub version after the commit is pushed. Git history can still preserve earlier versions of documents that were changed or deleted, subject to the repository's retention and history policies.\\n\\nPreserve authored formatting and native file types. Do not rewrite a document merely to normalize it, and do not use this directory as a dumping ground for terminal output, build artifacts, dependency archives, database dumps, application installers, or routine generated test results.\\n\\nGit tracks files rather than empty directories. A new subdirectory is synchronized automatically when it contains an eligible file; use an intentional `.gitkeep` only when preserving an otherwise empty directory is genuinely necessary.\\n\\r\\nALTER TABLE \\\"GameMasterUser\\\" ADD COLUMN \\\"role\\\" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'CAPTAIN_CREATOR';\\nALTER TABLE \\\"GameMasterUser\\\" ADD COLUMN \\\"capabilities\\\" TEXT NOT The Game Master Dashboard, privately used by Kato while the Tall Tale is underway\\r\\n\\r\\nThe first implementation phase must create the architectural foundation and one complete, polished vertical slice of the experience.\\r\\n\\r\\nDo not rush into implementing every planned page or chapter. Build the underlying system correctly, then make the first chapter-release sequence exceptionally polished.\\r\\n\\r\\n---\\r\\n\\r\\n# CRITICAL WORKING RULES\\r\\n\\r\\n## Do not modify unrelated projects\\r\\n\\r\\nThis must be a standalone project.\\r\\n\\r\\nBefore changing anything:\\r\\n\\r\\n1. Inspect the current working directory.\\r\\n2. Confirm whether it is already a dedicated Forever Treasure project.\\r\\n3. Document the resulting path and repository state.\\r\\n\\r\\nDo not modify EOAT Atlas, work projects, or unrelated personal applications.\\r\\n\\r\\n---\\r\\n\\r\\n## Do not produce a shallow prototype\\r\\n\\r\\nThis phase is not complete merely because:\\r\\n\\r\\n* The app compiles\\r\\n* A button changes some text\\r\\n* A database row updates\\r\\n* A basic animation runs\\r\\n* The
