input.payload.value : undefined,\r\n            correlationId,\r\n            reason: input.reason,\r\n            expectedSequence: input.expectedSequence,\r\n          },\r\n        );\r\n        stored = commandReceipt(result, correlationId);\r\n      } catch (error) {\r\n        if (error instanceof ProgressionConflict) throw new CommandConflict(error.message, error.code);\r\n        throw error;\r\n      }\r\n    } else if (input.command === \"PREPARE_HINT\") {\r\n      const staged = await stageAdminCommand(input, userId, { correlationId, reason: input.reason });\r\n      const snapshot = await buildPublicSnapshot(campaign.id);\r\n      stored = stagedCommandReceipt(staged, correlationId, snapshot);\r\n    } else stored = commandReceipt(await appendCustomEvent(input, userId, correlationId), correlationId);\r\n    await db.commandExecution.update({\r\n      where: { id: execution.id },\r\n      data: { status: \"SUCCEEDED\", result: completeResultEnvelope(stored, fingerprint), completedAt: new Date() },\r\n    });\r\n    return stored;\r\n  } catch (error) {\r\n    const committed = await recoverCommittedMutation(campaign.id, correlationId);\r\n    if (committed) {\r\n      await db.commandExecution.update({\r\n        where: { id: execution.id },\r\n        data: { status: \"SUCCEEDED\", result: completeResultEnvelope(committed, fingerprint), completedAt: new Date() },\r\n      });\r\n      return committed;\r\n    }\r\n    await db.commandExecution.update({\r\n      where: { id: execution.id },\r\n      data: {\r\n        status: \"FAILED\",\r\n        completedAt: new Date(),\r\n      },\r\n    });\r\n    await db.adminAuditLog.create({\r\n      data: {\r\n        campaignId: campaign.id,\r\n        userId,\r\n        action: input.command,\r\n        correlationId,\r\n        outcome: \"FAILED\",\r\n        reason: input.reason,\r\n        metadata: JSON.stringify({ code: error instanceof CommandConflict ? error.code : \"COMMAND_FAILED\" }),\r\n      },\r\n    });\r\n    if (error instanceof CommandConflict) throw error;\r\n    throw new CommandFailure(correlationId);\r\n  }\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function stageAdminCommand(\r\n  input: Pick<Input, \"command\" | \"campaignSlug\" | \"expectedSequence\" | \"targetKey\" | \"payload\"> & {\r\n    scheduledFor?: string;\r\n  },\r\n  userId: string,\r\n  context: { correlationId?: string; reason?: string } = {},\r\n) {\r\n  const canonicalCorrelationId = context.correlationId ?? randomUUID();\r\n  const resolved = await resolveLegacyCampaign(input.campaignSlug);\r\n  if (!resolved) throw new CommandConflict(\"This Voyage is unavailable.\", \"NOT_FOUND\");\r\n  return db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\r\n    const session = await tx.taleSession.findUniqueOrThrow({ where: { id: resolved.sessionId } });\r\n    const idempotencyKey = `legacy-stage:${session.id}:${input.command}:${input.expectedSequence}:${input.targetKey ?? \"none\"}`;\r\n    const prior = await tx.taleSessionEvent.findUnique({ where: { idempotencyKey } });\r\n    if (prior)\r\n      return {\r\n        id: prior.id,\r\n        command: input.command,\r\n        targetKey: input.targetKey ?? null,\r\n        reservedSequence: prior.sequence,\r\n        status: input.scheduledFor ? \"SCHEDULED\" : \"PREPARED\",\r\n        preparedAt: prior.createdAt,\r\n        publication: \"NOT_APPLICABLE\" as const,\r\n        delivery: \"NOT_ATTEMPTED\" as const,\r\n      },\r\n    });\r\n    return {\r\n      ...staged,\r\n      reservedSequence,\r\n      publication: \"NOT_APPLICABLE\" as const,\r\n      delivery: \"NOT_ATTEMPTED\" as const,\r\n      deliveryScope: \"NO_PLAYER_EVENT\" as const,\r\n    };\r\n  });\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function previewAdminCommand(input: Omit<Input, \"idempotencyKey\">) {\r\n  const resolved = await resolveLegacyCampaign(input.campaignSlug);\r\n  if (!resolved) throw new CommandConflict(\"This Voyage is unavailable.\", \"NOT_FOUND\");\r\n  const state = await getTaleSessionState(resolved.sessionId, undefined, true);\r\n  const prerequisites: string[] = [];\r\n  if (state.session.currentSequence !== input.expectedSequence)\r\n    prerequisites.push(\"Captain's Console state is stale. Refresh before continuing.\");\r\n  if (state.session.status === \"PAUSED\" && ![\"RESUME\", \"REQUEST_RECONCILIATION\"].includes(input.command))\r\n    prerequisites.push(\"Resume the Chronicle Session first.\");\r\n  return {\r\n    preview: true,\r\n    watermark: \"PREVIEW â€” NOT RELEASED\",\r\n    currentSequence: state.session.currentSequence,\r\n    projectedSequence: state.session.currentSequence + 1,\r\n    eventType: eventForPreview(input.command),\r\n    affectedSystems: affectedSystems(input.command),\r\n    undoAvailable: input.command === \"UNDO_LAST\",\r\n    prerequisites,\r\n    canExecute: prerequisites.length === 0,\r\n    snapshot: state,\r\n  };\r\n  /* Retired Campaign preview implementation retained below for audit only. */\r\n  const campaign = await db.campaign.findUniqueOrThrow({ where: { slug: input.campaignSlug } });\r\n  const snapshot = await buildPublicSnapshot(campaign.id);\r\n  const projected = structuredClone(snapshot);\r\n  const prerequisites: string[] = [];\r\n  if (campaign.currentSequence !== input.expectedSequence)\r\n    prerequisites.push(\"Captain's Console state is stale. Refresh before continuing.\");\r\n  if (campaign.status === \"PAUSED\" && ![\"RESUME\", \"REQUEST_RECONCILIATION\"].includes(input.command))\r\n    prerequisites.push(\"Resume the Voyage first.\");\r\n  if (input.command === \"RELEASE_CHAPTER\" && snapshot.chapter.state !== \"READY\")\r\n    prerequisites.push(\"Prepare this Chapter first.\");\r\n  if (input.command === \"RELEASE_CHAPTER\" && !prerequisites.length) projected.chapter.state = \"ACTIVE\";\r\n  if (input.command === \"MARK_SOLVED\" && snapshot.chapter.state !== \"ACTIVE\")\r\n    prerequisites.push(\"Only the active Chapter can be solved.\");\r\n  if (input.command === \"DISCOVER_SIDE_QUEST\" || input.command === \"ADVANCE_SIDE_QUEST\") {\r\n    const quest = await db.sideQuest.findFirst({\r\n      where: { campaignId: campaign.id, ...(input.targetKey ? { key: input.targetKey } : {}) },\r\n      include: { objectives: { orderBy: { ordinal: \"asc\" } } },\r\n    });\r\n    if (!quest) prerequisites.push(\"This Echo is not configured.\");\r\n    else {\r\n      const plan = planSideQuestTransition(input.command, quest.state, quest.objectives);\r\n      if (!plan.allowed) prerequisites.push(plan.message);\r\n    }\r\n  }\r\n  if (input.command === \"PAUSE\") projected.campaign.status = \"PAUSED\";\r\n  if (input.command === \"RESUME\") projected.campaign.status = \"ACTIVE\";\r\n  return {\r\n    preview: true,\r\n    watermark: \"PREVIEW â€” NOT RELEASED\",\r\n    currentSequence: campaign.currentSequence,\r\n    projectedSequence: campaign.currentSequence + 1,\r\n    eventType: input.command === \"PREPARE_HINT\" ? \"HINT_PREPARED\" : eventForPreview(input.command),\r\n    affectedSystems: affectedSystems(input.command),\r\n    undoAvailable: ![\"REQUEST_RECONCILIATION\", \"PREPARE_HINT\"].includes(input.command),\r\n    prerequisites,\r\n    canExecute: prerequisites.length === 0,\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

migrationVersion: LEGACY_COMPANION_MIGRATION_VERSION,\r\n      sourceChecksum: accessCodeHash,\r\n    };\r\n    await tx.legacyEntityReference.create({\r\n      data: { ...referenceData, \"\");\r\n  const actual = key.startsWith(\"artifact:\") ? inventory.includes(key.slice(9)) : variables[key];\r\n  const expected = config.value;\r\n  if (config.operator === \"notEquals\") return actual !== expected;\r\n  if (config.operator === \"greaterThan\") return Number(actual) > Number(expected);\r\n  if (config.operator === \"lessThan\") return Number(actual) < Number(expected);\r\n  if (config.operator === \"contains\")\r\n    return Array.isArray(actual) ? actual.includes(expected) : String(actual ?? \"\").includes(String(expected ?? \"\"));\r\n  return actual === expected;\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction chooseNext(\r\n  snapshot: PublishedTaleSnapshot,\r\n  block: PublishedBlock,\r\n  variables: JsonObject,\r\n  inventory: string[],\r\n  selected?: string,\r\n) {\r\n  const blocks = blocksOf(snapshot);\r\n  if (selected) return blocks.find((candidate) => candidate.id === selected) ?? null;\r\n  if (block.blockType === \"condition\") {\r\n    const target = String(\r\n      block.configuration[\r\n        conditionPasses(block, variables, inventory) ? \"successTargetBlockId\" : \"failureTargetBlockId\"\r\n      ] ?? \"\",\r\n    );\r\n    return blocks.find((candidate) => candidate.id === target) ?? null;\r\n  }\r\n  const target =\r\n    block.connections.find((connection) => connection.connectionType === \"DEFAULT\")?.targetBlockId ?? block.nextBlockId;\r\n  return blocks.find((candidate) => candidate.id === target) ?? null;\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function mutateVariables(block: PublishedBlock, variables: JsonObject) {\r\n  if (block.blockType !== \"setVariable\") return variables;\r\n  const next = { ...variables };\r\n  const key = String(block.configuration.variable ?? \"\");\r\n  const operation = String(block.configuration.operation ?? \"set\");\r\n  if (!key) return next;\r\n  if (operation === \"increment\") next[key] = Number(next[key] ?? 0) + Number(block.configuration.value ?? 1);\r\n  else if (operation === \"decrement\") next[key] = Number(next[key] ?? 0) - Number(block.configuration.value ?? 1);\r\n  else if (operation === \"toggle\") next[key] = !Boolean(next[key]);\r\n  else next[key] = block.configuration.value;\r\n  return next;\r\n}\r\n\r\nasync function completeBlock(\r\n  tx: Prisma.TransactionClient,\r\n  session: {\r\n    id: string;\r\n    publishedVersionId: string | null;\r\n    draftRevisionId: string | null;\r\n    variables: string;\r\n    inventory: string;\r\n  },\r\n  snapshot: PublishedTaleSnapshot,\r\n  block: PublishedBlock,\r\n  sourceType: string,\r\n  sourceId: string | null,\r\n  key: string,\r\n  selectedTarget?: string,\r\n) {\r\n  const completedBefore = await tx.taleSessionEvent.findFirst({\r\n    where: { sessionId: session.id, blockId: block.id, eventType: \"blockCompleted\" },\r\n  });\r\n  if (completedBefore) return completedBefore;\r\n  let variables = mutateVariables(block, parseJsonObject(session.variables));\r\n  let inventory = parseJsonArray<string>(session.inventory);\r\n  if ([\"artifactReveal\", \"collectionUpdate\"].includes(block.blockType)) {\r\n    const artifactId = String(block.configuration.artifactId ?? \"\");\r\n    if (artifactId && !inventory.includes(artifactId)) {\r\n      inventory = [...inventory, artifactId];\r\n      await tx.revealState.upsert({\r\n        where: {\r\n          playthroughId_contentType_contentKey: {\r\n            playthroughId: session.id,\r\n            contentType: \"ARTIFACT\",\r\n            contentKey: artifactId,\r\n          },\r\n        },\r\n        update: {},\r\n        create: {\r\n          playthroughId: session.id,\r\n          contentType: \"ARTIFACT\",\r\n          contentKey: artifactId,\r\n          revealedBy: sourceId ?? sourceType,\r\n        },\r\n      });\r\n      await appendEvent(tx, session, {\r\n        eventType: \"artifactGranted\",\r\n        sourceType,\r\n        sourceId,\r\n        blockId: block.id,\r\n        idempotencyKey: `grant:${session.id}:${block.id}:${artifactId}`,\r\n        payload: { artifactId },\r\n        correlationId: key,\r\n      });\r\nimport { CaptainLibrary } from \"@/components/platform/CaptainLibrary\";\r\nimport { requireGmCapability } from \"@/lib/security\";\r\nimport { redirect } from \"next/navigation\";\r\nexport const dynamic = \"force-dynamic\";\r\nexport default async function CaptainPage() {\r\n  if (!(await requireGmCapability(\"CAPTAIN\"))) redirect(\"/captain/sign-in\");\r\n  return <CaptainLibrary />;\r\n}\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

Record application commit, schema migration version, `LegacyEntityReference` checksum report, feature stage, database backup identity/hash, and asset backup identity before enabling canonical reads or writes.\r\n2. Take a consistent database and asset-volume backup. Verify a restore to an isolated database; do not test against production.\r\n3. Run migration `--verify`, shadow comparison, authorization matrix, and restart recovery against the exact release candidate.\r\n\r\n## Rollback before canonical-only writes\r\n\r\n1. Put the application into maintenance/read-only mode.\r\n2. Disable the typed canonical-read stage for the affected migrated sessions and restore legacy route resolution.\r\n3. Re-enable legacy reads only after verifying the Campaign tables were not changed by adapters.\r\n4. Preserve the canonical mapping and mismatch reports for investigation; do not delete them.\r\n5. Validate Player, Captain, invitation, public-slug, artifact, map, session, and audit projections against the recorded pre-cutover baseline.\r\n\r\n## Boundary after canonical-only writes\r\n\r\nAfter a canonical-only command has appended a Chronicle Session Event, directing traffic back to stale Campaign tables is unsafe and prohibited. Select one of these reviewed recovery paths:\r\n\r\n- restore the matched database and asset backup during a maintenance window; or\r\n- run an approved reverse projection/reconciliation tool, then compare all semantic state and audit correlation.\r\n\r\nDo not claim a simple rollback is available until one path has been rehearsed on an isolated copy and its evidence is linked to the release.\r\n\r\n## Verification after recovery\r\n\r\n- Confirm the database identity nonce through `/api/dev/validation/database-identity` in the isolated environment.\r\n- Run migration verification in read-only mode and confirm no duplicate mappings.\r\n- Compare current position, sequence, artifacts, side quests, map/routes, presentation acknowledgements, memberships, invitations, session lifecycle, and audit correlation.\r\n- Confirm legacy credentials remain scoped and revoked memberships remain revoked.\r\n- Record the final route stage, application commit, restoration source, and any irreversible canonical events.\r\n{\r\n  \"$schema\": \"./legacy-retirement-manifest.schema.json\",\r\n  \"project\": \"Project One Voyage\",\r\n  \"canonicalDomain\": \"Chronicle\",\r\n  \"cutoverStage\": \"B-migration-tooling\",\r\n  \"components\": [\r\n    {\r\n      \"component\": \"Campaign\",\r\n      \"status\": \"migration-source\",\r\n      \"canonicalReplacement\": \"Chronicle plus Published Chronicle Version\",\r\n      \"productionCallersRemaining\": \"baseline-inventory-required\",\r\n      \"canWrite\": true,\r\n      \"removalGate\": \"zero shadow mismatches, canonical reads and writes, compatibility release plus one\"\r\n    },\r\n    {\r\n      \"component\": \"CampaignSnapshotBuilder\",\r\n      \"status\": \"replace-with-adapter\",\r\n      \"canonicalReplacement\": \"ChronicleSessionProjectionService\",\r\n      \"productionCallersRemaining\": \"baseline-inventory-required\",\r\n      \"canWrite\": false,\r\n      \"removalGate\": \"legacy Player URLs pass canonical projection compatibility tests\"\r\n    },\r\n    {\r\n      \"component\": \"LegacyProgressionService\",\r\n      \"status\": \"replace-with-adapter\",\r\n Quartermaster routes perform zero legacy writes\"\r\n    },\r\n    {\r\n      \"component\": \"PlayerAccess\",\r\n      \"status\": \"migration-source\",\r\n      \"canonicalReplacement\": \"PlayerProfile, PlayerIdentitySession, PlaythroughMembership, Invitation\",\r\n      \"productionCallersRemaining\": \"baseline-inventory-required\",\r\n      \"canWrite\": true,\r\n      \"removalGate\": \"legacy token exchange is canonical-policy-only and all active credentials migrated\"\r\n    },\r\n    {\r\n      \"component\": \"AdminAuditLog\",\r\n      \"status\": \"migration-source\",\r\n      \"canonicalReplacement\": \"PlatformAuditEvent\",\r\n      \"productionCallersRemaining\": \"baseline-inventory-required\",\r\n      \"canWrite\": true,\r\n      \"removalGate\": \"all commands correlate to canonical events and audit records\"\r\n    },\r\n    {\r\n      \"component\": \"Quartermaster\",\r\n      \"status\": \"compatibility-ui\",\r\n      \"canonicalReplacement\": \"Captain runtime services\",\r\n      \"productionCallersRemaining\": \"baseline-inventory-required\",\r\n      \"canWrite\": false,\r\n      \"removalGate\": \"bookmark, command, and authorization compatibility release plus one\"\r\n    }\r\n  ]\r\n}\r\n# ADR: Project One Voyage canonical Chronicle domain\r\n\r\n- Status: accepted for implementation\r\n- Date: 2026-07-21\r\n- Decision: Chronicle is the only production writer of authored and runtime state after Stage E.\r\n\r\n## Decision\r\n\r\n`Chronicle` is the authored aggregate root. A Chronicle has drafts, chapters, blocks, connections, assets, locations, and artifacts. `PublishedTaleVersion` remains the existing physical/version model name during the compatibility release, but represents an immutable **Published Chronicle Version**. It is checksummed, schema-versioned, and never changes after publication.\r\n\r\n`TaleSession` remains the live-playthrough aggregate during this compatibility release and represents a **Chronicle Session**. It pins exactly one published version, owns current story position, lifecycle state, monotonic sequence, variables, inventory, reveal state, memberships, invitations, and `TaleSessionEvent` history. Runtime state is mutable only through the canonical command service. A command is idempotent by key and produces one canonical event sequence and one correlated `PlatformAuditEvent`.\r\n\r\nThe model/table naming compatibility is deliberate and temporary: live application code exposes Chronicle terminology; forward migration preserves installed data and can refer to pre-cutover table identifiers only inside migration SQL. No new feature may introduce a `Chronicle`, `chronicle`, or parallel Campaign writer.\r\n\r\n## Ownership boundaries\r\n\r\n| Boundary | Canonical owner | Rule |\r\n| --- | --- | --- |\r\n| Authored content | Chronicle, draft graph, assets, locations, artifacts | Creator-authorized, mutable only in a draft |\r\n| Published content | Published Chronicle Version | immutable snapshot, checksum, schema version |\r\n| Live playthrough | Chronicle Session | one pinned version, mutable only through canonical commands |\r\n| Runtime history | Chronicle Session Event | ordered, monotonic, idempotent command correlation |\r\n| Player identity | PlayerProfile and PlayerIdentitySession | rotating canonical credential, never a Campaign token as authority |\r\n| Player access | PlaythroughMembership and Invitation | membership is the resource scope; invitation is an acquisition credential |\r\n| Captain / Creator access | PlatformRoleAssignment plus existing staff account capability | explicit role and resource policy checks |\r\n| Artifacts, side quests, map, routes, variables | Chronicle authored definitions plus session variables/inventory/RevealState | definitions are versioned; earned/revealed state is session-local |\r\n| Presentation acknowledgement | canonical session event and RevealState/reading-state projection | acknowledgement cannot advance story |\r\n| Projection / replay | canonical projector from pinned version and events/reveals | no CampaignSnapshot read after canonical-read cutover |\r\n| Audit | PlatformAuditEvent | one sanitized record correlated with canonical event/command |\r\n| Compatibility | `src/compatibility/legacy-companion` | translations only; no independent decisions or state writes |\r\n\r\n## State model\r\n\r\nPublished content is immutable. Drafts are mutable and never replace a version already bound to a session. A session has `DRAFT_SETUP -> INVITING -> READY/SCHEDULED -> ACTIVE <-> PAUSED -> COMPLETED`, with cancellation before launch and abandonment after launch. Current position is the Sessionâ€™s current block and current sequence, derived from the last accepted event and persisted projection. Player-visible content is the intersection of the pinned version, membership scope, RevealState, and Player-safe projection policy.\r\n\r\nEvents are the authoritative history for progression, commands, presentation, and recovery. `variables`, `inventory`, `RevealState`, and current pointers are materialized projections updated atomically with the event. Cached projections may be rebuilt; they are never a second authority. Side-quest completion, artifact ownership, chapter completion, and map/route visibility derive from canonical events and projections.\r\n\r\n## Legacy migration and compatibility\r\n\r\n`LegacyEntityReference` is the deterministic mapping authority: source domain, model, source ID, canonical model/ID, migration version, source checksum, and timestamps are unique. The runner can resume, reject changed source checksums, resolve legacy URLs, and prove a rerun did not duplicate records. Legacy public slugs are retained by the Chronicle. A valid legacy access code exchanges for a canonical scoped identity/membership; it cannot grant global identity or broaden a role.\r\n\r\nCompatibility adapters may translate request/response shape, resolve source IDs, redirect URLs, and exchange old credentials. They call canonical policy, command, projection, and audit services. They may never independently mutate Campaign, chapter, artifact, snapshot, access, command, or audit tables after canonical writes are enabled.\r\n\r\n## Rollout and rollback\r\n\r\nStages are: A inventory/ADR; B migration tooling; C shadow comparison with legacy authoritative; D canonical reads for migrated sessions; E canonical-only writes; F compatibility-only legacy routes and read-only legacy tables; G retirement after removal gates. Feature configuration is typed and stage-specific, defaults to legacy reads until a verified migration has been explicitly enabled, and has a documented rollback switch.\r\n\r\nBefore any canonical-only write, rollback is application routing back to the last authoritative legacy projection after a verified backup. Once a canonical-only event exists, simple pointer rollback is forbidden: use a maintenance restore or reverse projection/reconciliation. Production rollback is not claimed until it is rehearsed against a database backup.\r\n\r\n## Deletion gate\r\n\r\nLegacy code may be deleted only after: all source models/fields are mapped; a migration and rerun pass; shadow comparison has zero unexplained semantic mismatches; all legacy public links and Quartermaster workflows use adapters; production reachability is zero except named adapters; legacy writers are blocked by tests; the compatibility release has shipped for one full release; and backup/rollback evidence is retained.\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

[{"text": "Script completed\nWall time 0.9 seconds\nOutput:\n", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "Exit code: 0\nWall time: 0.9 seconds\nOutput:\n# Chronicle Platform implementation record\r\n\r\nStatus: implemented and release-gate validated on 2026-07-17  \r\nSpecification: `Chronicle_Platform_Codex_Implementation_Specification.pdf`\r\nRepository baseline: `main` at `d0906e2`, synchronized with `origin/main` on 2026-07-17\r\n\r\n## Repository inventory before implementation\r\n\r\nThe current application already provides two additive but connected domains:\r\n\r\n- The original `Campaign` companion supports password-only Player access, the Quartermaster/Game Master command center, ordered progression events, presence, server-side public projections, CSRF-protected Captain mutations, audit records, and sanitized SSE.\r\n- Chronicle Studio Phase 1 provides `Chronicle`, editable `TaleDraft` graphs, immutable checksummed `PublishedTaleVersion` snapshots, version-pinned `TaleSession` runtime records, ordered idempotent session events, authorized media variants, Creator validation/publishing, Captain session controls, helper verification, and Player runtime pages.\r\n\r\nCurrent primary routes are `/`, `/tale/[campaignSlug]`, `/quartermaster`, `/tales`, `/play/[taleSlug]`, `/play/[taleSlug]/session/[sessionId]`, `/captain`, and `/studio`. Existing GM credentials and server-side sessions are shared by Captain and Creator capabilities. Existing Chronicle Player sessions use an opaque random token whose hash is persisted and whose clear value is stored only in an HttpOnly cookie, but they do not yet provide a durable Player identity, library, invitation lifecycle, or multi-playthrough history selector.\r\n\r\n## Architecture decision\r\n\r\nThe platform implementation will extend the existing domain rather than introduce a disconnected replacement:\r\n\r\n- `Chronicle` remains the authored identity.\r\n- `PublishedTaleVersion` remains the immutable release and historical rendering source.\r\n- `TaleSession` becomes the persisted playthrough aggregate. Existing preview-session behavior remains isolated through `previewMode`; every real new playthrough must reference one published version.\r\n- New Player profile/session, membership, invitation, invitation-event, reveal-state, scoped-role, and platform-audit records provide identity, library access, invitation lifecycle, revocation, and traceability.\r\n- Existing `GameMasterUser` credentials remain the strong account authority. Scoped role assignments supplement existing capability checks without invalidating current Captain/Creator logins.\r\n- Workspace-specific services return explicit Player-safe, Captain, and Creator view models. Raw Prisma entities and complete published snapshots are never serialized to Player library or invitation clients.\r\n- Existing `/tales`, `/play/[taleSlug]`, and `/captain` paths receive safe compatibility behavior while `/player/*`, `/join/*`, `/captain/library`, `/captain/invitations`, and `/studio/library` become the canonical platform families.\r\n\r\n## Migration and compatibility risks\r\n\r\n1. Existing `TaleSession` rows may already be active or completed. The migration must preserve their identifiers, access-token hashes, version binding, state, event order, inventory, variables, and timestamps.\r\n2. Legacy sessions do not have a durable Player profile or membership. Backfill must create deterministic guest profiles and memberships without changing their current runtime cookie behavior.\r\n3. Existing status values start at `ACTIVE`; new setup and invitation states must be accepted without breaking the progression engine, which may mutate only `ACTIVE` or `PAUSED` sessions and must keep `COMPLETED` terminal.\r\n4. Existing GM role/capability behavior must remain functional while explicit Captain and Creator denial is enforced on every new route and API.\r\n5. Published asset responses currently authorize by version identity. Player requests must additionally prove membership/session access and reveal scope; public-safe cover variants require a narrowly defined exception.\r\n6. SQLite is the supported local and test database, while a MySQL-parity migration must be maintained. The migration is additive and rollback is backup/restore plus application rollback; destructive down-migrations are not automated.\r\n7. The repository is public. Seeded accounts, invitations, story copy, names, PINs, and media remain fictional development data only.\r\n\r\n## Vertical implementation slices\r\n\r\n1. Add additive schema/migrations, domain state machines, authorization policies, token hashing, backfill, and development fixtures.\r\n2. Add secure Player session/sign-in, invitation resolution/acceptance/decline, library projection, waiting room, archive projection, and protected asset policy.\r\n3. Add Captain library, atomic playthrough/invitation creation, secure link/QR/short-code delivery, lifecycle management, launch synchronization, and audit history.\r\n4. Add the three-role cinematic gateway, role-aware sign-in and workspace switching, responsive Player/Captain screens, reduced-motion behavior, and compatibility redirects.\r\n5. Complete version comparison, restore/fork ancestry, immutable-release guards, authorization/concurrency tests, full role-journey browser coverage, migration validation, and documentation.\r\n\r\n## Completion evidence\r\n\r\nThe implementation is complete against the specification and is integrated with the existing Campaign, Quartermaster, and Chronicle Studio domains.\r\n\r\n- Additive SQLite migration deployment succeeded from an empty validation database across all five repository migrations. A matching forward-only MySQL migration and schema were syntax-validated; a live MySQL service was not available in this workspace, so production MySQL deployment remains an operator-controlled rollout step with a verified backup.\r\n- The normal development seed created the platform foundation without resetting progress. A dedicated legacy-shaped playthrough proof then ran the same seed path and verified that the original playthrough ID, published-version binding, event history, timestamps, reveal history, and new membership all survived intact. A second seed after the complete browser journey preserved all accepted progress and backfilled zero additional playthroughs.\r\n- Static and unit gates passed: Prettier, ESLint, strict TypeScript, local animation assets, 25 Vitest files, and 80 unit/component tests.\r\n- The complete browser matrix passed 21 tests with 7 intentionally skipped mutation-heavy mobile permutations. Chromium exercised the full invitation-to-archive platform journey and the established Campaign, Command Center, animation, and Studio journeys. Mobile WebKit exercised protected access, the role gateway, reduced motion, responsive layouts, and accessibility.\r\n- Acceptance-state verification found 5 persisted playthroughs, 16 progression events, 18 legacy audit entries, and 14 platform audit events. It also proved immutable-version pinning, exact-version archive rendering, Player-safe projections, role/resource denial, CSRF enforcement, invitation replacement/revocation, idempotent acceptance, and per-membership archive preferences.\r\n- The optimized Next.js production build completed successfully, followed by two clean production start/health/stop cycles. `npm audit` reported zero known vulnerabilities after the dependency security updates.\r\n\r\nThe remaining deployment risks are operational rather than hidden implementation gaps: production secrets must replace development credentials, MySQL migration must be applied only after a backup in a maintenance window, persistent multi-instance rate limiting needs a shared backend, and uploaded assets need object storage plus malware scanning for multi-instance production. The implementation commit is `e469b12`; the subsequent path-scoped synchronization commit records this implementation document and the associated Codex transcript.\r\n# Project One Voyage architecture inventory\r\n\r\n## Baseline and method\r\n\r\nThis inventory was taken from the `integration/lanternwake-phase5-universal-language` checkout at `a0a2111c` before the Project One Voyage cutover. It uses Prisma schema inspection, imports, Next route registration, API clients, direct database calls, seed tooling, tests, and documentation references. `Campaign` is the legacy Companion aggregate. `Chronicle` is the renamed canonical authored aggregate (called `Chronicle` in the baseline schema). No conclusion of â€œunusedâ€ is made from a missing UI link alone.\r\n\r\nReachable legacy production surfaces at the baseline are `/tale/[campaignSlug]`, `/quartermaster`, `/api/player/access`, `/api/player/[campaignSlug]/*`, and `/api/gm/*`. authoritative writers exist. The legacy writer updates Campaign-derived tables and its own event/snapshot/audit records; the newer writer updates Chronicle Session records and its own events/audit records. Project One Voyage must route the legacy APIs through one canonical command, projection, policy, and audit path before any legacy tables are made read-only. The term **Chronicle** replaces the historical product/domain term â€œChronicleâ€ in live code, active documentation, types, and paths; only forward database compatibility SQL may name a historical table when required to preserve installed data.\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

It keeps\r\n * the old field name while requiring the same concurrency and idempotency\r\n * evidence as the canonical command endpoint.\r\n */\r\nexport const actionCommandSchema = z\r\n  .object({ action: z.enum(adminCommands) })\r\n  .passthrough()\r\n  .transform(({ action, ...input }) => ({ ...input, command: action }))\r\n  .pipe(commandSchema);\r\n\r\nexport const commandPublicationStates = [\"PROCESS_PUBLISHED\", \"PROCESS_PUBLICATION_FAILED\", \"NOT_APPLICABLE\"] as const;\r\nexport const playerDeliveryStates = [\"UNCONFIRMED\"] as const;\r\n\r\nexport type AdminCommandReceiptTruth = Readonly<{\r\n  kind: \"PROGRESSION_EVENT\" | \"STAGED_ACTION\";\r\n  persistence: \"COMMITTED\";\r\n  publication: (typeof commandPublicationStates)[number];\r\n  /** @deprecated Compatibility alias. This refers only to the in-process event bus. */\r\n  delivery: \"PUBLISHED\" | \"PUBLICATION_FAILED\" | \"NOT_ATTEMPTED\";\r\n  deliveryScope: \"PROCESS_SUBSCRIBERS_ONLY\" | \"NO_PLAYER_EVENT\";\r\n  playerDelivery: (typeof playerDeliveryStates)[number];\r\n  playerPresentation: \"UNCONFIRMED\";\r\n  playerAcknowledgment: \"UNCONFIRMED\";\r\n}>;\r\n\r\nexport type StagedActionReceiptIdentity = Readonly<{\r\n  preparedActionId: string;\r\n  command: string;\r\n  targetKey: string | null;\r\n  reservedSequence: number;\r\n  status: string;\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

# 3. Advance Beyond Stage B

The implementation must progress through the remaining controlled stages.

## Stage C: Shadow reads

For representative migrated fixtures:

1. build the legacy projection;
2. build the canonical projection;

## Stage D: Canonical reads

Switch migrated production flows to canonical reads.

Legacy routes may translate canonical output into old response shapes.

They must not query legacy state to reconstruct business truth.

## Stage E: Canonical writes

All production mutations must write canonical state only.

* session start;
* pause;
* resume;
* answer approval;
* answer rejection;

## Stage F: Compatibility-only legacy layer

Legacy routes, URLs, tokens, and Quartermaster surfaces may remain only as bounded compatibility adapters.

Each adapter must:

* authenticate canonically;
* authorize canonically;
* resolve legacy IDs through mappings;
* call canonical services;
* translate canonical results;
* emit deprecation telemetry;
* perform no legacy write.

Do not destructively drop legacy tables during this branch unless the original completion gate explicitly requires it and parity proves removal safe.

\"RELEASE_CHAPTER\" && !prerequisites.length) projected.chapter.state = \"ACTIVE\";\r\n  if (input.command === \"MARK_SOLVED\" && snapshot.chapter.state !== \"ACTIVE\")\r\n    prerequisites.push(\"Only the active Chapter can be solved.\");\r\n  if (input.command === \"DISCOVER_SIDE_QUEST\" || input.command === \"ADVANCE_SIDE_QUEST\") {\r\n    const quest = await db.sideQuest.findFirst({\r\n      where: { campaignId: campaign.id, ...(input.targetKey ? { key: input.targetKey } : {}) },\r\n      include: { objectives: { orderBy: { ordinal: \"asc\" } } },\r\n    });\r\n    if (!quest) prerequisites.push(\"This Echo is not configured.\");\r\n    else {\r\n      const plan = planSideQuestTransition(input.command, quest.state, quest.objectives);\r\n      if (!plan.allowed) prerequisites.push(plan.message);\r\n    }\r\n  }\r\n  if (input.command === \"PAUSE\") projected.campaign.status = \"PAUSED\";\r\n  if (input.command === \"RESUME\") projected.campaign.status = \"ACTIVE\";\r\n  return {\r\n    preview: true,\r\n    watermark: \"PREVIEW â€” NOT RELEASED\",\r\n    currentSequence: campaign.currentSequence,\r\n    projectedSequence: campaign.currentSequence + 1,\r\n    eventType: input.command === \"PREPARE_HINT\" ? \"HINT_PREPARED\" : eventForPreview(input.command),\r\n    affectedSystems: affectedSystems(input.command),\r\n    undoAvailable: ![\"REQUEST_RECONCILIATION\", \"PREPARE_HINT\"].includes(input.command),\r\n    prerequisites,\r\n    canExecute: prerequisites.length === 0,\r\n    snapshot: projected,\r\n  };\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction eventForPreview(command: AdminCommand) {\r\n  const known: Partial<Record<AdminCommand, string>> = {\r\n    PREPARE_CHAPTER: \"CHAPTER_PREPARED\",\r\n    RELEASE_CHAPTER: \"CHAPTER_RELEASED\",\r\n    MARK_SOLVED: \"CHAPTER_SOLVED\",\r\n    REVEAL_MAP: \"MAP_LOCATION_REVEALED\",\r\n    AWARD_ARTIFACT: \"ARTIFACT_AWARDED\",\r\n    PAUSE: \"CAMPAIGN_PAUSED\",\r\n    RESUME: \"CAMPAIGN_RESUMED\",\r\n    UNDO_LAST: \"STATE_REVERTED\",\r\n  };\r\n  return known[command] ?? eventFor(command);\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction affectedSystems(command: AdminCommand) {\r\n  const systems: Partial<Record<AdminCommand, string[]>> = {\r\n      playerPresentation: \"UNCONFIRMED\",\r\n      playerAcknowledgment: \"UNCONFIRMED\",\r\n      playerEvent: { id: \"event-5\", type: \"ARTIFACT_AWARDED\", sequence: 5 },\r\n    });\r\n  });\r\n\r\n  it(\"stores and replays the full versioned request fingerprint without mutating twice\", async () => {\r\n    const first = await executeAdminCommand(input, \"gm-1\");\r\n    expect(JSON.parse(String(dependencies.db.commandExecution.create.mock.calls[0][0].data.result))).toEqual({\r\n      version: 1,\r\n      state: \"PENDING\",\r\n      fingerprint: commandRequestFingerprint(input),\r\n    });\r\n    const envelope = succeededEnvelope();\r\n    expect(JSON.parse(envelope)).toMatchObject({\r\n      version: 1,\r\n      state: \"COMPLETE\",\r\n      fingerprint: commandRequestFingerprint(input),\r\n      receipt: { kind: \"PROGRESSION_EVENT\", correlationId: first.correlationId, persistence: \"COMMITTED\" },\r\n    });\r\n\r\n    dependencies.db.commandExecution.findUnique.mockResolvedValue({\r\n      id: \"execution-1\",\r\n      campaignId: \"campaign-1\",\r\n      correlationId: first.correlationId,\r\n      command: input.command,\r\n      expectedSequence: input.expectedSequence,\r\n      status: \"SUCCEEDED\",\r\n      result: envelope,\r\n    });\r\n    dependencies.executeProgressionAction.mockClear();\r\n\r\n    await expect(executeAdminCommand(input, \"gm-1\")).resolves.toEqual({ ...first, idempotentReplay: true });\r\n    expect(dependencies.executeProgressionAction).not.toHaveBeenCalled();\r\n  });\r\n\r\n  it(\"rejects idempotency-key reuse when command, sequence, target, payload, or reason changes\", async () => {\r\n    const receipt = commandReceipt({ event: publicEvent }, \"correlation-1\");\r\n    const envelope = JSON.stringify({\r\n      version: 1,\r\n\r\n  it(\"marks PREPARE_HINT committed without claiming any Crew publication\", async () => {\r\n    const result = await executeAdminCommand(preparedInput, \"gm-1\");\r\n\r\n    expect(result).toMatchObject({\r\n      kind: \"STAGED_ACTION\",\r\n      event: null,\r\n      preparedActionId: \"prepared-5\",\r\n      stagedAction: {\r\n        preparedActionId: \"prepared-5\",\r\n        command: \"PREPARE_HINT\",\r\n        targetKey: \"hint-1\",\r\n        reservedSequence: 5,\r\n        status: \"PREPARED\",\r\n        preparedAt: \"2026-07-18T12:00:00.000Z\",\r\n      },\r\n      persistence: \"COMMITTED\",\r\n      publication: expect(replay).toMatchObject({ kind: \"STAGED_ACTION\", event: null, idempotentReplay: true });\r\n    expect(Object.isFrozen(replay.kind === \"STAGED_ACTION\" ? replay.stagedAction : null)).toBe(true);\r\n    expect(dependencies.transaction.campaign.updateMany).toHaveBeenCalledWith({\r\n      where: { id: \"campaign-1\", currentSequence: 4 },\r\n      data: { currentSequence: { Use Browser when no such tool exists, the tool cannot access the resource or lacks a required capability, or UI work remains; use available browser context before asking the user to repeat it.\n\nUse this skill for browser automation tasks such as inspecting pages, navigating, testing local apps, clicking, typing, taking screenshots, and reading visible page state.\r\n\r\nIf this plugin is listed as available in the session, treat that as mandatory reading before browser work. Open and follow this skill before saying that Browser is unavailable and before falling back to standalone Playwright or Computer Use.\r\n\r\nDo not skip this skill just because Computer Use MCP tool calls are directly visible or appear easier to invoke. The presence of Computer Use tools is not evidence that Computer Use is the preferred browser surface.\n\n## Setup Documentation\nUse `await agent.documentation.get(\"<name>\")` when one of these setup topics applies:\n- `bootstrap-troubleshooting`: read when browser setup succeeds but discovery or selection fails\n- `chrome-troubleshooting`: read when Chrome extension setup, installation, or communication fails\n\n## Bootstrap\r\nThese setup details are internal. User-facing progress updates should be less technical in nature. Never mention `Node REPL`, `node_repl`, `REPL`, JavaScript sessions, module exports, reading documentation, or loading instructions unless a user is asking for that exact information. If setup or recovery is needed, describe it naturally as connecting to the browser or retrying the browser connection.\r\n\r\nThe `browser-client` module is the core entry point for browser use, and is available under `scripts/browser-client.mjs` in this plugin's root directory. ALWAYS import it using an absolute path. IMPORTANT: If this path cannot be found, stop and report that this plugin is missing `scripts/browser-client.mjs`. NEVER use the built in `browser-client` library.\r\n\r\nRun browser setup code through the Node REPL `js` tool. In this environment the callable tool id typically appears as `mcp__node_repl__js`. If it is not already available, use tool discovery for `node_repl js` without setting a result limit. You need the `js` execution tool: `js_reset` only clears state, and `js_add_node_module_dir` only changes package resolution. Do not call either helper while trying to expose `js`. If `js` is still not available, search again for `node_repl js` with `limit: 10`.\r\n\r\nInitialize the runtime once per fresh Node session. If `agent.browsers` already exists, reuse it; do not import or initialize another browser runtime.\r\n\r\n```js\r\nif (globalThis.agent?.browsers == null) {\r\n  const { setupBrowserRuntime } = await import(\"<plugin root>/scripts/browser-client.mjs\");\r\n  await setupBrowserRuntime({ globals: globalThis });\r\n}\r\n```\r\n\r\nOnce a browser connection is established, reuse its existing browser binding across later turns and do not reread this skill. Once you have read a browser's complete documentation, do not read it again unless you select a different browser.\r\n\r\nA tab binding is separate from its browser binding. If a later turn reports that a tab is missing, stale, closed, or not part of the current browser session, discard that tab binding and obtain or create a fresh tab from the existing browser binding. An empty `browser.tabs.list()` or `browser.user.openTabs()` result is normal after tab cleanup and does not invalidate the browser binding. Never call `agent.browsers.get*` to recover a tab; only an explicit browser-disconnected error invalidates the binding.\r\n\r\n## Browser selection\r\n\r\nThe scenarios below are for the initial browser selection only. Before calling any `agent.browsers.get*` method, reuse an existing `globalThis.browser`, `globalThis.iab`, or `globalThis.chrome` binding that already serves the task. A new user turn does not invalidate a browser binding or require another selection or documentation call.\r\n\r\nSelect the initial browser with exactly one of these scenarios, in the order\r\nshown. An explicit request for the in-app browser or Chrome always wins over URL\r\nselection. Never call `getForUrl()` when the user names a browser.\r\n\r\nApp-provided in-app-browser context is ambient UI state, not a user instruction to select or switch browsers. Only the text of the user's request can explicitly choose a browser.\r\n\r\nDo not inspect browser cookies, local storage, profiles, passwords, or session stores. Browser discovery must remain read-only.\r\n\r\nWhen authentication blocks requested browser navigation, do not replace it with web search, a search engine, another site, or another source merely to bypass sign-in.\r\n\r\n### The user explicitly requests a browser\r\n\r\nA plugin mention in the user's request explicitly names its browser.\r\n`[@Browser](plugin://browser@openai-bundled)` names the in-app browser.\r\n`[@Chrome](plugin://chrome@openai-bundled)`,\r\n`[@chrome-internal](plugin://chrome-internal@openai-bundled)`, and\r\n`[@chrome-dev](plugin://chrome-dev@openai-bundled)` name Chrome. Follow the\r\ncorresponding explicit-browser scenario below.\r\n\r\nThe in-app browser is available only when the Browser skill is listed for the session. If the user explicitly requests the in-app browser and that skill is available, use a distinct persistent binding and immediately read its complete documentation:\r\n\r\n```js\r\nif (globalThis.iab == null) {\r\n  globalThis.iab = await agent.browsers.get(\"iab\");\r\n  nodeRepl.write(await iab.documentation());\r\n}\r\n```\r\n\r\nIf the user explicitly requests the in-app browser but the Browser skill is not available, report that the in-app browser is unavailable instead of substituting another browser.\r\n\r\nChrome is available only when the Chrome skill is listed for the session. If the user explicitly requests Chrome and that skill is available, use a separate persistent binding and immediately read its complete documentation:\r\n\r\n```js\r\nif (globalThis.chrome == null) {\r\n  globalThis.chrome = await agent.browsers.get(\"extension\");\r\n  nodeRepl.write(await chrome.documentation());\r\n}\r\n```\r\n\r\nIf the user explicitly requests Chrome but the Chrome skill is not available, report that Chrome is unavailable instead of substituting another browser.\r\n\r\nAn explicit browser choice remains in force for the task. If authentication blocks the task in an explicitly selected browser, your next response must explicitly ask the user to sign in in that browser and tell you when it is ready, unless that browser's documentation provides a supported authentication flow to try first. Merely reporting that sign-in is required is not sufficient. Do not switch to another browser unless the user asks or approves the switch.\r\n\r\n### The task requires browser interaction, the user does not specify a browser, and the task has a target URL\r\n\r\nWhen the user supplies a URL or the intended URL can be reasonably inferred from the request, replace the example below with that URL and let browser-client choose the browser best suited to it:\r\n\r\n```js\r\nif (globalThis.browser == null) {\r\n  globalThis.browser = await agent.browsers.getForUrl(\"https://example.com/\");\r\n  nodeRepl.write(await browser.documentation());\r\n}\r\n```\r\n\r\n### The user specifies neither a browser nor a target URL\r\n\r\nUse the runtime default, which prefers the in-app browser when it is available and otherwise uses Chrome. Do not list browsers first:\r\n\r\n```js\r\nif (globalThis.browser == null) {\r\n  globalThis.browser = await agent.browsers.getDefault();\r\n  nodeRepl.write(await browser.documentation());\r\n}\r\n```\r\n\r\n## After setup\r\n\r\nIf setup succeeds but browser discovery or selection fails, read `await agent.documentation.get(\"bootstrap-troubleshooting\")` before resetting the JavaScript session or trying another browser-control mechanism.\r\n\r\nIf the failure is specific to Chrome extension setup, installation, or communication, read `await agent.documentation.get(\"chrome-troubleshooting\")` before retrying or taking another recovery action.\r\n\r\nWhen the user did not explicitly choose a browser, a browser selected by the runtime is not a user constraint. Do not switch browsers based only on an assumption about authentication. If navigation shows that the selected browser lacks the required authentication, select another available browser before asking the user to sign in. You may select it without resetting the Node session. Preserve existing `iab`, `chrome`, and `browser` bindings when they are still useful. Existing tabs remain bound to the browser that created them. After selecting a different browser, obtain a tab from that browser before continuing and read its complete documentation.\r\n\r\nThe ability to interact directly with browsers is exposed through the `browser-client` runtime via the `agent.browsers.*` API. Before trying to interact with a selected browser for the first time, you MUST emit and read the complete documentation returned by its `documentation()` call in one go. For the initial documentation read, run the exact direct `nodeRepl.write(await <browser>.documentation());` call shown in the applicable scenario above. Do not assign the documentation to a variable, inspect its length, slice it, truncate it, summarize it, or emit only an excerpt. Do not proactively split the documentation into pages or chunks. const patch = "*** Begin Patch\n*** Delete File: \\\\gwplastics.com\\VT\\Users\\kgray\\My Documents\\treasurehunt\\Forever-Treasure-Integration\\src\\server\\admin-command.ts\n*** Add File: \\\\gwplastics.com\\VT\\Users\\kgray\\My Documents\\treasurehunt\\Forever-Treasure-Integration\\src\\server\\admin-command.ts\n+import { randomUUID } from \"node:crypto\";\n+import type { AdminCommand } from \"@/domain/admin\";\n+import { getTaleSessionState } from \"@/chronicle/progression\";\n+import { db } from \"@/lib/db\";\n+import { resolveLegacyCampaign } from \"@/compatibility/legacy-companion\";\n+import { executeLegacyQuartermasterCommand, LegacyQuartermasterConflict } from \"@/compatibility/legacy-quartermaster\";\n+\n+export type AdminCommandInput = {\n+  command: AdminCommand;\n+  campaignSlug: string;\n+  expectedSequence: number;\n+  idempotencyKey: string;\n+  targetKey?: string;\n+  payload: Record<string, unknown>;\n+  reason?: string;\n+};\n+\n+type CanonicalEventReceipt = Readonly<{\n+  kind: \"PROGRESSION_EVENT\";\n+  event: { id: string; type: string; sequence: number; payload: Record<string, unknown>; releaseAt: string };\n+  correlationId: string;\n+  persistence: \"COMMITTED\";\n+  publication: \"PROCESS_PUBLISHED\" | \"PROCESS_PUBLICATION_FAILED\";\n+  delivery: \"PUBLISHED\" | \"PUBLICATION_FAILED\";\n+  deliveryScope: \"PROCESS_SUBSCRIBERS_ONLY\";\n+  playerDelivery: \"UNCONFIRMED\";\n+  playerPresentation: \"UNCONFIRMED\";\n+  playerAcknowledgment: \"UNCONFIRMED\";\n+  playerEvent: { id: string; type: string; sequence: number };\n+}>;\n+\n+type StagedActionReceipt = Readonly<{\n+  kind: \"STAGED_ACTION\";\n+  event: null;\n+  stagedAction: {\n+    preparedActionId: string;\n+    command: string;\n+    targetKey: string | null;\n+    reservedSequence: number;\n+    status: string;\n+    preparedAt: string;\n+  };\n+  preparedActionId: string;\n+  correlationId: string;\n+  persistence: \"COMMITTED\";\n+  publication: \"NOT_APPLICABLE\";\n+  delivery: \"NOT_ATTEMPTED\";\n+  deliveryScope: \"NO_PLAYER_EVENT\";\n+  playerDelivery: \"UNCONFIRMED\";\n+  playerPresentation: \"UNCONFIRMED\";\n+  playerAcknowledgment: \"UNCONFIRMED\";\n+  playerEvent: null;\n+}>;\n+\n+export type AdminCommandReceipt = CanonicalEventReceipt | StagedActionReceipt;\n+\n+export class CommandConflict extends Error {\n+  constructor(\n+    message: string,\n+    public readonly code = \"COMMAND_CONFLICT\",\n+  ) {\n+    super(message);\n+  }\n+}\n+\n+export class CommandFailure extends Error {\n+  constructor(public readonly correlationId: string) {\n+    super(\"The Chronicle command could not be completed. No progress has changed.\");\n+  }\n+}\n+\n+function canonicalJson(value: unknown): string {\n+  if (value === null || typeof value !== \"object\") return JSON.stringify(value);\n+  if (Array.isArray(value)) return `[${value.map(canonicalJson).join(\",\")}]`;\n+  return `{${Object.entries(value as Record<string, unknown>)\n+    .sort(([left], [right]) => left.localeCompare(right))\n+    .map(([key, item]) => `${JSON.stringify(key)}:${canonicalJson(item)}`)\n+    .join(\",\")}}`;\n+}\n+\n+export function commandRequestFingerprint(input: AdminCommandInput) {\n+  return canonicalJson({\n+    campaignSlug: input.campaignSlug,\n+    command: input.command,\n+    expectedSequence: input.expectedSequence,\n+    targetKey: input.targetKey ?? null,\n+    payload: input.payload,\n+    reason: input.reason ?? null,\n+  });\n+}\n+\n+/**\n+ * The only command entrypoint used by both historical GM URLs. It delegates\n+ * every mutation to the Chronicle-session command service; Campaign tables\n+ * are never loaded or written here.\n+ */\n+export async function executeAdminCommand(\n+  input: AdminCommandInput,\n+  userId: string,\n+  context: { correlationId?: string } = {},\n+): Promise<AdminCommandReceipt> {\n+  const correlationId = context.correlationId ?? randomUUID();\n+  try {\n+    return await executeLegacyQuartermasterCommand(input, userId, correlationId);\n+  } catch (error) {\n+    if (error instanceof LegacyQuartermasterConflict) throw new CommandConflict(error.message, error.code);\n+    throw error;\n+  }\n+}\n+\n+export async function stageAdminCommand(\n+  input: Pick<AdminCommandInput, \"command\" | \"campaignSlug\" | \"expectedSequence\" | \"targetKey\" | \"payload\"> & {\n+    scheduledFor?: string;\n+  },\n+  userId: string,\n+  context: { correlationId?: string; reason?: string } = {},\n+) {\n+  const correlationId = context.correlationId ?? randomUUID();\n+  const resolved = await resolveLegacyCampaign(input.campaignSlug);\n+  if (!resolved) throw new CommandConflict(\"This Voyage is unavailable.\", \"NOT_FOUND\");\n+  return db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\n+    const session = await tx.taleSession.findUniqueOrThrow({ where: { id: resolved.sessionId } });\n+    if (!session.publishedVersionId) throw new CommandConflict(\"This Chronicle Session has no published version.\", \"NOT_FOUND\");\n+    const idempotencyKey = `chronicle-stage:${session.id}:${input.idempotencyKey ?? `${input.command}:${input.expectedSequence}`}`;\n+    const prior = await tx.taleSessionEvent.findUnique({ where: { idempotencyKey } });\n+    if (prior)\n+      return {\n+        id: prior.id,\n+        command: input.command,\n+        targetKey: input.targetKey ?? null,\n+        reservedSequence: prior.sequence,\n+        status: input.scheduledFor ? \"SCHEDULED\" : \"PREPARED\",\n+        preparedAt: prior.createdAt,\n+        publication: \"NOT_APPLICABLE\" as const,\n+        delivery: \"NOT_ATTEMPTED\" as const,\n+        deliveryScope: \"NO_PLAYER_EVENT\" as const,\n+      };\n+    if (session.currentSequence !== input.expectedSequence)\n+      throw new CommandConflict(\"This prepared Chronicle command is stale. Refresh Captain's Console before preparing it again.\", \"STALE_SEQUENCE\");\n+    const next = await tx.taleSession.update({\n+      where: { id: session.id },\n+      data: { currentSequence: { increment: 1 } },\n+      select: { currentSequence: true },\n+    });\n+    const staged = await tx.taleSessionEvent.create({\n+      data: {\n+        sessionId: session.id,\n+        publishedVersionId: session.publishedVersionId,\n+        blockId: session.currentBlockId,\n+        eventType: \"chronicle.commandPrepared\",\n+        sourceType: \"captain-command-service\",\n+        sourceId: userId,\n+        idempotencyKey,\n+        payload: JSON.stringify({ command: input.command, targetKey: input.targetKey ?? null, scheduledFor: input.scheduledFor ?? null }),\n+        sequence: next.currentSequence,\n+        correlationId,\n+      },\n+    });\n+    await tx.platformAuditEvent.create({\n+      data: {\n+        actorType: \"CAPTAIN\",\n+        actorId: userId,\n+        action: \"CHRONICLE_COMMAND_PREPARED\",\n+        resourceType: \"CHRONICLE_SESSION\",\n+        resourceId: session.id,\n+        correlationId,\n+        metadata: JSON.stringify({ command: input.command, targetKey: input.targetKey ?? null, scheduledFor: input.scheduledFor ?? null }),\n+      },\n+    });\n+    return {\n+      id: staged.id,\n+      command: input.command,\n+      targetKey: input.targetKey ?? null,\n+      reservedSequence: next.currentSequence,\n+      status: input.scheduledFor ? \"SCHEDULED\" : \"PREPARED\",\n+      preparedAt: staged.createdAt,\n+      publication: \"NOT_APPLICABLE\" as const,\n+      delivery: \"NOT_ATTEMPTED\" as const,\n+      deliveryScope: \"NO_PLAYER_EVENT\" as const,\n+    };\n+  });\n+}\n+\n+export async function previewAdminCommand(input: Omit<AdminCommandInput, \"idempotencyKey\">) {\n+  const resolved = await resolveLegacyCampaign(input.campaignSlug);\n+  if (!resolved) throw new CommandConflict(\"This Voyage is unavailable.\", \"NOT_FOUND\");\n+  const state = await getTaleSessionState(resolved.sessionId, undefined, true);\n+  const prerequisites: string[] = [];\n+  if (state.session.currentSequence !== input.expectedSequence)\n+    prerequisites.push(\"Captain's Console state is stale. Refresh before continuing.\");\n+  if (state.session.status === \"PAUSED\" && ![\"RESUME\", \"REQUEST_RECONCILIATION\"].includes(input.command))\n+    prerequisites.push(\"Resume the Chronicle Session first.\");\n+  return {\n+    preview: true,\n+    watermark: \"PREVIEW — NOT RELEASED\",\n+    currentSequence: state.session.currentSequence,\n+    projectedSequence: state.session.currentSequence + 1,\n+    eventType: `chronicle.${input.command.toLowerCase()}`,\n+    affectedSystems: [\"chronicle-session\", \"chronicle-event\", \"platform-audit\"],\n+    undoAvailable: input.command === \"UNDO_LAST\",\n+    prerequisites,\n+    canExecute: prerequisites.length === 0,\n+    snapshot: state,\n+  };\n+}\n*** End Patch";
const result = await tools.apply_patch(patch);
text(typeof result === "string" ? result : JSON.stringify(result));

\"PLAYER_LOG_ENTRY_ADDED\",\r\n  RELEASE_JOURNAL_ENTRY: \"NARRATIVE_MESSAGE_RELEASED\",\r\n  TEASE_FINALE: \"FINALE_TEASED\",\r\n  UPDATE_FINALE_REQUIREMENT: \"FINALE_REQUIREMENT_UPDATED\",\r\n  PAUSE: \"CAMPAIGN_PAUSED\",\r\n  RESUME: \"CAMPAIGN_RESUMED\",\r\n  REQUEST_RECONCILIATION: \"PLAYER_RECONCILIATION_REQUESTED\",\r\n};\r\n\r\nfunction legacyState(raw: string): LegacyVariables {\r\n  const parsed = parseJsonObject(raw) as LegacyVariables;\r\n  const legacy = (parsed.legacy ?? {}) as JsonObject;\r\n  parsed.legacy = legacy;\r\n  if (!legacy.chapters || typeof legacy.chapters !== \"object\" || Array.isArray(legacy.chapters)) legacy.chapters = {};\r\n  if (!legacy.sideQuests || typeof legacy.sideQuests !== \"object\" || Array.isArray(legacy.sideQuests)) legacy.sideQuests = {};\r\n  if (!legacy.routes || typeof legacy.routes !== \"object\" || Array.isArray(legacy.routes)) legacy.routes = {};\r\n  return parsed;\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction chapterForCommand(snapshot: ReturnType<typeof parsePublishedSnapshot>, variables: LegacyVariables, command: AdminCommand) {\r\n  const chapters = (variables.legacy?.chapters ?? {}) as Record<string, string>;\r\n  if (command === \"PREPARE_CHAPTER\")\r\n    return snapshot.chapters.find((chapter) => [\"LOCKED\", \"TEASER\"].includes(chapters[String(chapter.orderIndex)] ?? \"LOCKED\"));\r\n  if (command === \"RELEASE_CHAPTER\")\r\n    return snapshot.chapters.find((chapter) => (chapters[String(chapter.orderIndex)] ?? \"LOCKED\") === \"READY\");\r\n  return [...snapshot.chapters]\r\n    .reverse()\r\n    .find((chapter) => [\"ACTIVE\", \"SOLVED\", \"COMPLETE\"].includes(chapters[String(chapter.orderIndex)] ?? \"LOCKED\"));\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction receipt(event: { id: string; eventType: string; sequence: number; createdAt: Date }, correlationId: string) {\r\n  return {\r\n    kind: \"PROGRESSION_EVENT\" as const,\r\n    event: {\r\n      id: event.id,\r\n      type: event.eventType,\r\n      sequence: event.sequence,\r\n      payload: {},\r\n      releaseAt: event.createdAt.toISOString(),\r\n    },\r\n    correlationId,\r\n    persistence: \"COMMITTED\" as const,\r\n    publication: \"PROCESS_PUBLISHED\" as const,\r\n    delivery: \"PUBLISHED\" as const,\r\n    deliveryScope: \"PROCESS_SUBSCRIBERS_ONLY\" as const,\r\n    playerDelivery: \"UNCONFIRMED\" as const,\r\n    playerPresentation: \"UNCONFIRMED\" as const,\r\n    playerAcknowledgment: \"UNCONFIRMED\" as const,\r\n    playerEvent: { id: event.id, type: event.eventType, sequence: event.sequence },\r\n  };\r\n}\r\n\r\n/**\r\n * Adapter for Quartermaster request shapes. It resolves the historical Campaign\r\n * only as an ID lookup; all decisions, sequence increments, events, reveals,\r\n * inventory and audit writes are canonical Chronicle-session writes.\r\n */\r\nexport async function executeLegacyQuartermasterCommand(\r\n  input: LegacyCommandInput,\r\n  userId: string,\r\n  correlationId: string = randomUUID(),\r\n) {\r\n  const resolved = await resolveLegacyCampaign(input.campaignSlug);\r\n  if (!resolved) throw new LegacyQuartermasterConflict(\"This Voyage is unavailable.\", \"NOT_FOUND\");\r\n  const key = `legacy-quartermaster:${resolved.sessionId}:${input.idempotencyKey}`;\r\n  const event = await db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\r\n    const prior = await tx.taleSessionEvent.findUnique({ where: { idempotencyKey: key } });\r\n    if (prior) return prior;\r\n    const session = await tx.taleSession.findUniqueOrThrow({ where: { id: resolved.sessionId }, include: { version: true } });\r\n    if (session.currentSequence !== input.expectedSequence)\r\n      throw new LegacyQuartermasterConflict(\r\n        `Chronicle state changed from sequence ${input.expectedSequence} to ${session.currentSequence}. Refresh Captain's Console.`,\r\n        \"STALE_SEQUENCE\",\r\n      );\r\n    if (session.status === \"PAUSED\" && ![\"RESUME\", \"REQUEST_RECONCILIATION\"].includes(input.command))\r\n      throw new LegacyQuartermasterConflict(\"This Chronicle Session is paused. Resume it before releasing progression.\", \"SESSION_PAUSED\");\r\n    const type = eventTypeFor[input.command];\r\n    if (!type || input.command === \"UNDO_LAST\")\r\n      throw new LegacyQuartermasterConflict(\r\n        \"This historical command requires the canonical Captain rollback control.\",\r\n        \"UNSUPPORTED_COMMAND\",\r\n      );\r\n    if (!session.version) throw new LegacyQuartermasterConflict(\"This Chronicle Session has no published version.\", \"NOT_FOUND\");\r\n    const snapshot = parsePublishedSnapshot(session.version.contentSnapshot);\r\n    const variables = legacyState(session.variables);\r\n    const inventory = parseJsonArray<string>(session.inventory);\r\n    let currentBlockId = session.currentBlockId;\r\n    let currentChapterId = session.currentChapterId;\r\n    let status = session.status;\r\n    let reveal: { contentType: string; contentKey: string } | null = null;\r\n    const payload: JsonObject = { command: input.command, targetKey: input.targetKey ?? null };\r\n    const chapter = chapterForCommand(snapshot, variables, input.command);\r\n\r\n    if ([\"PREPARE_CHAPTER\", \"RELEASE_CHAPTER\", \"MARK_SOLVED\", \"COMPLETE_CHAPTER\"].includes(input.command)) {\r\n      if (!chapter) throw new LegacyQuartermasterConflict(\"No matching imported Chapter is available.\", \"NOT_FOUND\");\r\n      const state =\r\n        input.command === \"PREPARE_CHAPTER\"\r\n          ? \"READY\"\r\n          : input.command === \"RELEASE_CHAPTER\"\r\n            ? \"ACTIVE\"\r\n            : input.command === \"MARK_SOLVED\"\r\n              ? \"SOLVED\"\r\n              : \"COMPLETE\";\r\n      (variables.legacy!.chapters as Record<string, string>)[String(chapter.orderIndex)] = state;\r\n      if (input.command === \"RELEASE_CHAPTER\") {\r\n        currentChapterId = chapter.id;\r\n        currentBlockId = chapter.entryBlockId;\r\n        if (chapter.entryBlockId) reveal = { contentType: \"BLOCK\", contentKey: chapter.entryBlockId };\r\n      }\r\n      payload.chapterId = chapter.id;\r\n      payload.chapterState = state;\r\n    } else if (input.command === \"PAUSE\" || input.command === \"RESUME\") {\r\n      status = input.command === \"PAUSE\" ? \"PAUSED\" : \"ACTIVE\";\r\n    } else if (input.command === \"REVEAL_MAP\" || input.command === \"REVEAL_ROUTE\") {\r\n      const contentKey = input.targetKey ?? \"default\";\r\n      reveal = { contentType: input.command === \"REVEAL_MAP\" ? \"map-location\" : \"map-route\", contentKey };\r\n      if (input.command === \"REVEAL_ROUTE\") (variables.legacy!.routes as Record<string, string>)[contentKey] = \"REVEALED\";\r\n    } else if ([\"AWARD_ARTIFACT\", \"REVEAL_ARTIFACT_SILHOUETTE\", \"CONNECT_ARTIFACTS\"].includes(input.command)) {\r\n      const artifact = await tx.taleArtifact.findFirst({\r\n        where: { taleId: session.taleId, ...(input.targetKey ? { legacyKey: input.targetKey } : {}) },\r\n        orderBy: { sortOrder: \"asc\" },\r\n      });\r\n      if (!artifact) throw new LegacyQuartermasterConflict(\"No mapped Artifact is available.\", \"NOT_FOUND\");\r\n      reveal = { contentType: \"artifact\", contentKey: artifact.legacyKey ?? artifact.id };\r\n      if (input.command === \"AWARD_ARTIFACT\" && !inventory.includes(artifact.id)) inventory.push(artifact.id);\r\n      payload.artifactId = artifact.id;\r\n    } else if ([\"DISCOVER_SIDE_QUEST\", \"UPDATE_SIDE_QUEST\", \"COMPLETE_SIDE_QUEST\", \"ADVANCE_SIDE_QUEST\"].includes(input.command)) {\r\n      const quest = await tx.taleSideQuest.findFirst({\r\n        where: { taleId: session.taleId, ...(input.targetKey ? { legacyKey: input.targetKey } : {}) },\r\n        orderBy: { legacyKey: \"asc\" },\r\n      });\r\n      if (!quest) throw new LegacyQuartermasterConflict(\"No mapped side quest is available.\", \"NOT_FOUND\");\r\n      const questState =\r\n        input.command === \"DISCOVER_SIDE_QUEST\"\r\n          ? \"DISCOVERED\"\r\n          : input.command === \"COMPLETE_SIDE_QUEST\"\r\n            ? \"COMPLETE\"\r\n            : \"ACTIVE\";\r\n      (variables.legacy!.sideQuests as Record<string, JsonObject>)[quest.legacyKey] = { state: questState };\r\n      reveal = { contentType: \"side-quest\", contentKey: quest.legacyKey };\r\n      payload.sideQuestKey = quest.legacyKey;\r\n      payload.sideQuestState = questState;\r\n    } else if (input.command === \"TEASE_FINALE\" || input.command === \"UPDATE_FINALE_REQUIREMENT\") {\r\n      variables.legacy = { ...variables.legacy, finaleState: input.command === \"TEASE_FINALE\" ? \"TEASED\" : \"REQUIREMENTS_PARTIAL\" };\r\n    } else if (input.command === \"ADD_JOURNAL_ANNOTATION\" || input.command === \"ADD_LOG_ENTRY\" || input.command === \"RELEASE_JOURNAL_ENTRY\") {\r\n      payload.value = typeof input.payload.value === \"string\" ? input.payload.value : null;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    const guarded = await tx.taleSession.updateMany({\r\n      where: { id: session.id, currentSequence: input.expectedSequence },\r\n      data: {\r\n        currentSequence: { increment: 1 },\r\n        status,\r\n        currentBlockId,\r\n        currentChapterId,\r\n        variables: JSON.stringify(variables),\r\n        inventory: JSON.stringify(inventory),\r\n      },\r\n    });\r\n    if (guarded.count !== 1)\r\n      throw new LegacyQuartermasterConflict(\"Another Captain command was saved first. Refresh before retrying.\", \"STALE_SEQUENCE\");\r\n    const updated = await tx.taleSession.findUniqueOrThrow({ where: { id: session.id }, select: { currentSequence: true } });\r\n    if (reveal)\r\n      await tx.revealState.upsert({\r\n        where: { playthroughId_contentType_contentKey: { playthroughId: session.id, ...reveal } },\r\n        update: {},\r\n        create: { playthroughId: session.id, ...reveal, revealedBy: userId },\r\n      });\r\n    const created = await tx.taleSessionEvent.create({\r\n      data: {\r\n        sessionId: session.id,\r\n        publishedVersionId: session.publishedVersionId!,\r\n        blockId: currentBlockId,\r\n        eventType: type,\r\n        sourceType: \"legacy-quartermaster-adapter\",\r\n        sourceId: userId,\r\n        idempotencyKey: key,\r\n        payload: JSON.stringify(payload),\r\n        sequence: updated.currentSequence,\r\n        correlationId,\r\n      },\r\n    });\r\n    await tx.platformAuditEvent.create({\r\n      data: {\r\n        actorType: \"CAPTAIN\",\r\n        actorId: userId,\r\n        action: `LEGACY_QUARTERMASTER_${input.command}`,\r\n        resourceType: \"CHRONICLE_SESSION\",\r\n        resourceId: session.id,\r\n        correlationId,\r\n        metadata: JSON.stringify({ adapter: \"legacy-quartermaster\", targetKey: input.targetKey ?? null }),\r\n      },\r\n    });\r\n    return created;\r\n  });\r\n  publishTaleSessionEvent(resolved.sessionId, {\r\n    id: event.id,\r\n    eventType: event.eventType,\r\n    sequence: event.sequence,\r\n    createdAt: event.createdAt.toISOString(),\r\n  });\r\n  return receipt(event, correlationId);\r\n}\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

preserved without advancing progression.\",\n+  );\n+  return { campaignSlug, mapped: true, semanticMatch: mismatches.length === 0, mismatches };\n+}\n*** Add File: \\\\gwplastics.com\\VT\\Users\\kgray\\My Documents\\treasurehunt\\Forever-Treasure-Integration\\scripts\\verify-legacy-shadow-parity.ts\n+import { db } from \"@/lib/db\";\n+import { shadowCompareLegacyCampaign } from \"@/chronicle/shadow-parity\";\n+\n+async function main() {\n+  const selector = process.argv[2];\n+  const campaigns = await db.campaign.findMany({\n+    where: selector ? { slug: selector } : undefined,\n+    select: { slug: true },\n+    orderBy: { slug: \"asc\" },\n+  });\n+  if (selector && !campaigns.length) throw new Error(`No legacy Campaign has slug ${selector}.`);\n+  const reports = await Promise.all(campaigns.map((campaign) => shadowCompareLegacyCampaign(campaign.slug)));\n+  process.stdout.write(`${JSON.stringify({ reports }, null, 2)}\\n`);\n+  if (reports.some((report) => !report.semanticMatch)) process.exitCode = 1;\n+}\n+\n+main()\n+  .catch((error: unknown) => {\n+    process.stderr.write(`${error instanceof Error ? error.message : \"Shadow parity verification failed.\"}\\n`);\n+    process.exitCode = 1;\n+  })\n+  .finally(async () => db.$disconnect());\n*** End Patch";

text(typeof result === "string" ? result : JSON.stringify(result));

eventIds: string[]) {\r\n  return db.taleSessionEvent.findMany({\r\n    where: {\r\n      id: { in: eventIds },\r\n      sessionId,\r\n      eventType: { in: [\"CHAPTER_RELEASED\", \"HINT_RELEASED\", NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invitation required.\" }, { status: 401 });\r\n  const url = new URL(request.url);\r\n  // Batch contract: repeat the plural key, for example\r\n  // `?deviceId=<uuid>&eventIds=<id-1>&eventIds=<id-2>`. The singular\r\n  // `eventId` form below retains its existing response and 409 semantics.\r\n  if (url.searchParams.has(\"eventIds\")) {\r\n    if (url.searchParams.has(\"eventId\")) {\r\n      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid acknowledgement query.\" }, { status: 400 });\r\n    }\r\n    const parsedBatch = ceremonyBatchSchema.safeParse({\r\n      eventIds: url.searchParams.getAll(\"eventIds\"),\r\n      deviceId: url.searchParams.get(\"deviceId\"),\r\n    });\r\n    if (!parsedBatch.success) {\r\n      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid acknowledgement query.\" }, { status: 400 });\r\n    }\r\n    const requestedEventIds = [...new Set(parsedBatch.data.eventIds)];\r\n    const eligible = await eligiblePlayerPresentations(access.sessionId, requestedEventIds);\r\n    const eligibleIds = new Set(eligible.map((event) => event.id));\r\n    const viewed = await db.revealState.findMany({\r\n      where: {\r\n        playthroughId: access.sessionId,\r\n        contentType: \"acknowledgement:event\",\r\n        contentKey: { in: [...eligibleIds].map((eventId) => receipt.contentKey.split(\":\").at(-1) ?? \"\"))]\r\n      .filter((eventId) => eligibleIds.has(eventId))\r\n      .sort((left, right) => left.localeCompare(right));\r\n    return NextResponse.json({ (!parsed.success) return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid acknowledgement.\" }, { status: 400 });\r\n  const event = await eligiblePlayerPresentation(access.sessionId, parsed.data.eventId);\r\n  if (!event) {\r\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Presentation is not eligible for acknowledgement.\" }, { status: 409 });\r\n  }\r\n  const viewed = await db.revealState.findFirst({\r\n    where: {\r\n      playthroughId: access.sessionId,\r\n      contentType: \"acknowledgement:event\",\r\n      contentKey: `${access.playerId}:${parsed.data.deviceId}:${parsed.data.eventId}`,\r\n    },\r\n    select: { id: true },\r\n  });\r\n  return NextResponse.json({ acknowledged: Boolean(viewed) });\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function POST(request: Request, context: { params: Promise<{ campaignSlug: string }> }) {\r\n  const { campaignSlug } = await context.params;\r\n  const access = await requireLegacyCompatibilityAccess(campaignSlug);\r\n  if (!access) return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invitation required.\" }, { status: 401 });\r\n  const parsed = schema.safeParse(await request.json().catch(() => null));\r\n  if (!parsed.success) return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid acknowledgement.\" }, { status: 400 });\r\n  if (\"eventId\" in parsed.data) {\r\n    const event = await eligiblePlayerPresentation(access.sessionId, parsed.data.eventId);\r\n    if (!event) {\r\n playthroughId_contentType_contentKey: {\r\n          playthroughId: access.sessionId,\r\n          contentType: \"acknowledgement:event\",\r\n          contentKey: `${access.playerId}:${parsed.data.deviceId}:${parsed.data.eventId}`,\r\n        },\r\n      },\r\n      update: {},\r\n      create: {\r\n        playthroughId: access.sessionId,\r\n        contentType: \"acknowledgement:event\",\r\n        contentKey: `${access.playerId}:${parsed.data.deviceId}:${parsed.data.eventId}`,\r\n        status: \"ACKNOWLEDGED\",\r\n        revealedBy: access.playerId,\r\n      },\r\n    });\r\n  } else {\r\n    const { contentType, contentKeys } = parsed.data;\r\n    await db.$transaction(\r\n      contentKeys.map((contentKey) =>\r\n        db.revealState.upsert({\r\n          where: {\r\n            playthroughId_contentType_contentKey: {\r\n              playthroughId: access.sessionId,\r\n              contentType: `acknowledgement:${contentType}`,\r\n              contentKey: `${access.playerId}:${contentKey}`,\r\n            },\r\n          },\r\n          update: { revealedAt: new Date() },\r\n          create: {\r\n            playthroughId: access.sessionId,\r\n            contentType: `acknowledgement:${contentType}`,\r\n            contentKey: `${access.playerId}:${contentKey}`,\r\n            status: \"ACKNOWLEDGED\",\r\n            revealedBy: access.playerId,\r\n          },\r\n        }),\r\n      ),\r\n    );\r\n  }\r\n  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\r\n}\r\nimport { eventBus } from \"@/lib/events\";\r\nimport { db } from \"@/lib/db\";\r\nimport { requireLegacyCompatibilityAccess } from \"@/compatibility/legacy-companion\";\r\n\r\nexport const dynamic = \"force-dynamic\";\r\n// Keep the established stream contracts explicit while this historical URL\r\n// reads the canonical Chronicle-session event stream.\r\nexport const PLAYER_EVENT_HEARTBEAT_MS = 15_000;\r\nexport const PLAYER_EVENT_HISTORY_PAGE_SIZE = 50;\r\nexport const PLAYER_EVENT_DEDUPE_WINDOW_SIZE = 256;\r\nexport const PLAYER_EVENT_LIVE_BUFFER_LIMIT = 128;\r\nexport const PLAYER_EVENT_STREAM_HIGH_WATER_MARK = 64;\r\n\r\nfunction cursor(request: Request) {\r\n  const raw = request.headers.get(\"last-event-id\") ?? new URL(request.url).searchParams.get(\"after\") ?? \"0\";\r\n  return /^\\d+$/u.test(raw) ? Number(raw) : 0;\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction playerEvent(event: { id: string; eventType: string; sequence: number; payload: string; createdAt: Date }) {\r\n  let payload: Record<string, unknown> = {};\r\n  try {\r\n    const parsed: unknown = JSON.parse(event.payload);\r\n    if (parsed && typeof parsed === \"object\" && !Array.isArray(parsed)) payload = parsed as Record<string, unknown>;\r\n  } catch {}\r\n  return { id: event.id, type: event.eventType, sequence: event.sequence, payload, releaseAt: event.createdAt.toISOString() };\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function GET(request: Request, context: { params: Promise<{ campaignSlug: string }> }) {\r\n  const access = await requireLegacyCompatibilityAccess((await context.params).campaignSlug);\r\n  if (!access) return new Response(\"Invitation required.\", { status: 401 });\r\n  const requested = cursor(request);\r\n  const encoder = new TextEncoder();\r\n      let close = () => undefined;\r\n  const stream = new ReadableStream({\r\n    async start(controller) {\r\n      let last = requested;\r\n      const send = (event: { id: string; eventType: string; sequence: number; payload: string; createdAt: Date }) => {\r\n        if (event.sequence <= last) return;\r\n        last = event.sequence;\r\n        controller.enqueue(encoder.encode(`id: ${event.sequence}\\nevent: progression\\ndata: ${JSON.stringify(playerEvent(event))}\\n\\n`));\r\n      };\r\n      const historical = await db.taleSessionEvent.findMany({\r\n        where: { sessionId: access.sessionId, sequence: { gt: requested } },\r\n        orderBy: { sequence: \"asc\" },\r\n        take: PLAYER_EVENT_HISTORY_PAGE_SIZE,\r\n      });\r\n      for (const event of historical) send(event);\r\n      const channel = `tale-session:${access.sessionId}`;\r\n      const listener = async (incoming: { id: string; eventType: string; sequence: number; createdAt: string }) => {\r\n        const event = await db.taleSessionEvent.findUnique({ where: { id: incoming.id } });\r\n        if (event) send(event);\r\n      };\r\n      eventBus.on(channel, listener);\r\n      const heartbeat = setInterval(() => controller.enqueue(encoder.encode(\": tide connected\\n\\n\")), PLAYER_EVENT_HEARTBEAT_MS);\r\n      close = () => {\r\n        clearInterval(heartbeat);\r\n        eventBus.off(channel, listener);\r\n        try {\r\n          controller.close();\r\n        } catch {}\r\n      };\r\n      request.signal.addEventListener(\"abort\", close, { once: true });\r\n    },\r\n    cancel() {\r\n      close();\r\n    },\r\n  });\r\n  return new Response(stream, {\r\n    headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"text/event-stream\", \"Cache-Control\": \"no-cache, no-transform\", Connection: \"keep-alive\" },\r\n  });\r\n}\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]
